items.name: accordion
items.description: A vertically stacked list of disclosure panels (PARTIAL tier, controlled/uncontrolled). accordion.jte is a styled container that wraps accordion-item.jte instances. Each item is a native <details>/<summary> pair: zero JS, zero server round-trip just to open. Uncontrolled = browser manages open/close state. Controlled = caller binds open="${serverFact}" on each item (the server reflects which panels are expanded; the item is a pure presentation shell). Single-open grouping: all sibling items share the same name= attribute; the platform <details name=...> group enforces at most one open at a time. Variants: default (bordered card, --lv-shadow-xs), ghost (dividers only, transparent outer), borderless (fully flat). No enhancer: keyboard + aria-expanded + Tab order are all platform-supplied (native <details>/<summary>). APG Accordion pattern. Requires accordion.css for chevron rotation.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui accordion partial -- a vertically stacked list of disclosure panels. TIER: PARTIAL presentational (controlled/uncontrolled). Zero JS, zero server round-trip just to open or close a panel. The browser owns the open/close state (uncontrolled mode via native
); when expansion is a server fact the CALLER binds open="${yourField}" on each accordion-item (controlled mode). The accordion itself declares no @Wire field and no Java component. This is the arch-contract §1 PARTIAL tier: overlay + stateful primitives as headless controlled/uncontrolled, never bespoke WIRE. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Accordion (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/accordion/): each panel is a native
/ pair -- the platform gives aria-expanded, Enter/Space activation, Tab traversal, and (with
) single-open exclusivity for free. This is the "platform does it all" composition the spec endorses. No enhancer, no roving tabindex, no focus trap (APG defines none of these for Accordion). Uncontrolled (default): the caller omits open= on each item; the browser persists the expanded state client-side for the lifetime of the page. Controlled: the caller binds open="${yourWireField.contains(itemId)}" on each item; the server renders which panels are open; a wire action on the trigger button on the CALLER side updates the state and re-renders. The accordion-item partial is a pure presentation shell in both modes. Single-mode exclusivity: pass a shared name= to every sibling accordion-item; the platform
group ensures at most one is open at a time -- zero JS. Multiple-mode: omit name= (each item is independent). Inventory reference: Ant Design Collapse (ghost/borderless/default variants, dividers, multiple-expand, nested panels). Original generation; no Ant Design code copied. Styling: inspired by Tailwind UI disclosure; original generation over --lv-* tokens. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Accordion): - Native
exposes aria-expanded automatically (the browser owns it). - Native is the disclosure trigger (role=button, Enter/Space, Tab: platform). - Panel body: role="region" + aria-labelledby on the content
inside each item. - Keyboard: Enter/Space on summary = platform; Tab/Shift+Tab = standard page order; ArrowDown/Up/Home/End = NOT in APG Accordion; intentionally absent. - Focus: no trap, no roving tabindex, no restore -- standard page Tab sequence. - No live region (the expand/collapse is visible in-place; no AT announcement needed). - No enhancer: all keyboard + role + focus is platform-supplied. Params: gg.jte.Content content the accordion items (zero or more @@template.lievit.accordion-item(...)). String variant = "default" "default" (bordered card) | "ghost" (dividers only, transparent outer) | "borderless" (fully flat, no borders). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root container. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw attribute string ($unsafe) -- STATIC, author-typed strings only (data-testid, wire directives on root). Usage: @@template.lievit.accordion(variant = "default", content = @@` @@template.lievit.accordion-item(label = "Section one", id = "alpha", open = true, name = "faq", content = @@`

Body of the first section.

`) @@template.lievit.accordion-item(label = "Section two", id = "beta", name = "faq", content = @@`

Body of the second section.

`) `) @@template.lievit.accordion(variant = "ghost", content = @@` @@template.lievit.accordion-item(label = "Terms", id = "terms", content = @@`

These are the terms.

`) `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String variant = "default" @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" <%-- Variant-driven outer container style. Ghost and borderless suppress the outer border/shadow/radius; dividers between items are controlled per-item in accordion-item.jte. --%> !{var variantStyle = switch (variant) { case "ghost" -> "background:transparent;border:0;box-shadow:none;border-radius:0;"; case "borderless" -> "background:transparent;border:0;box-shadow:none;border-radius:0;"; default -> "background:var(--lv-color-popover);border:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);border-radius:var(--lv-radius-md);box-shadow:var(--lv-shadow-xs);overflow:hidden;"; };}
${content}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui accordion-item partial -- one disclosure panel in an accordion (or standalone). TIER: PARTIAL presentational. Native
/ -- zero JS, CSP-clean. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Accordion (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/accordion/): a native
/ pair maps precisely to the APG disclosure pattern. The browser exposes aria-expanded on automatically, and Enter/Space/Tab are all platform- supplied. The panel body carries role="region" + aria-labelledby, forming an a11y landmark for substantial content (APG recommendation; suppressed via panelRole="" for trivial content). No enhancer, no roving tabindex, no focus trap. Uncontrolled: the caller omits open=; the browser manages expand/collapse state. Controlled: the caller binds open="${serverFact}" -- when the server holds which panels are expanded (e.g. a WIRE parent re-renders this template on each action), the open attribute reflects that truth. The item itself never declares a @Wire field. Single-open grouping: pass the same name= to sibling items;
gives the platform single-open exclusivity for free (modern browsers, 2024+). Multiple-open: omit name=. Disabled: the summary button is rendered `disabled` (aria-disabled + pointer-events-none); the trigger blocks Enter/Space; the item remains visible but inert. Chevron animation: a span.lv-accordion-chevron inside the summary rotates 180deg when the
is open. The rotation is CSS-driven via the details[open] selector -- no JS, no inline style. The adopter provides the rule (or the accordion.css companion delivers it). Transition timing reads --lv-duration-fast + --lv-ease. Inventory reference: Ant Design Collapse item (disabled, collapsible header, custom header content). Original generation; no Ant Design code copied. Styling: inspired by Tailwind UI disclosure; original generation over --lv-* tokens. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Accordion): -
: native disclosure element; browser supplies aria-expanded on the child automatically. - : the trigger; native role=button + Enter/Space from the platform; list-style:none suppresses the default disclosure marker (the chevron is our own icon). - aria-controls="panel-${id}" on the : points to the content region. - Panel content
: landmark for screen reader navigation (when panelRole is non-empty, default "region"). - Disabled state: aria-disabled="true" + pointer-events-none + tabindex="-1" on summary; the native
cannot be disabled itself, so we suppress the trigger. - Divider: a top border on the item (suppressed via noDivider=true for the first item or in borderless context). - Focus: no trap, no roving tabindex. Tab order = standard page sequence. - No live region (expand/collapse is visible in-place). Params: String label the trigger label (the header text, HTML-escaped by JTE). gg.jte.Content content the panel body markup (the caller's owned Content slot). String id = "lv-acc" id stem; summary gets id="header-btn-${id}", panel gets id="panel-${id}". String name = null
grouping name; siblings with the same name get single-open exclusivity via the platform. boolean open = false render this item expanded (
); the controlled hook -- bind to your WIRE field: open="${field.contains(id)}". boolean disabled = false suppress trigger activation; aria-disabled + tabindex=-1. String panelRole = "region" role on the panel content div; pass "" to suppress (for trivial content where landmark clutter is a concern). boolean noDivider = false suppress the top border divider (use on the first item, or when the parent variant handles borders differently). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the
root. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw attribute string ($unsafe) -- STATIC, author-typed strings only (data-testid, wire directives). Usage: Uncontrolled (browser manages state): @@template.lievit.accordion-item(label = "Section one", id = "s1", content = @@`

Body text.

`) Controlled (server fact drives open): @@template.lievit.accordion-item(label = "Section one", id = "s1", open = form.expandedPanels().contains("s1"), content = @@`

Body text.

`) Single-open group (platform handles exclusivity): @@template.lievit.accordion-item(label = "FAQ one", id = "faq1", name = "faq", content = @@`

Answer one.

`) @@template.lievit.accordion-item(label = "FAQ two", id = "faq2", name = "faq", content = @@`

Answer two.

`) Disabled item: @@template.lievit.accordion-item(label = "Locked section", id = "locked", disabled = true, content = @@`

Not accessible.

`) --%> @param String label @param gg.jte.Content content @param String id = "lv-acc" @param String name = null @param boolean open = false @param boolean disabled = false @param String panelRole = "region" @param boolean noDivider = false @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" !{var triggerId = "header-btn-" + id;} !{var panelId = "panel-" + id;} <%-- Top divider: present by default (separates sibling items); suppressed for first item or when the parent variant handles outer borders exclusively (borderless/ghost). --%> !{var itemStyle = noDivider ? "display:block;" : "border-top:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);display:block;";} <%-- Header base style: flex row, full-width, transparent background (the outer container provides the surface colour), consistent padding from spacing tokens. --%> !{var summaryBase = "display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:var(--lv-space-4);width:100%;list-style:none;cursor:pointer;padding:var(--lv-space-3) var(--lv-space-4);font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);font-size:var(--lv-text-sm);font-weight:var(--lv-font-medium);text-align:left;line-height:var(--lv-leading-tight);color:var(--lv-color-popover-fg);background:transparent;border:0;";} Disabled modifier: pointer-events off, opacity dim, cursor blocked. !{var disabledStyle = disabled ? "pointer-events:none;opacity:var(--lv-opacity-disabled);cursor:not-allowed;" : "";}
<%-- Summary = the disclosure trigger. list-style:none removes the default marker (we supply our own chevron icon). ::-webkit-details-marker { display:none } is the WebKit equivalent; handled in accordion.css. aria-controls + role are redundant on native but harmless and improve AT compat. disabled: aria-disabled + tabindex=-1 block keyboard activation without hiding the item. --%> ${label} <%-- Chevron: decorative icon that rotates 180deg when the
is open. CSS rule in accordion.css: details[open] .lv-accordion-chevron { transform: rotate(180deg); } No inline style carries logic; transition timing reads existing motion tokens. --%> @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-down", size = "1rem")
<%-- Panel content: role="region" + aria-labelledby forms an a11y landmark (APG). panelRole="" suppresses the role when content is trivial and landmark clutter is a concern. No `hidden` attribute needed:
hides the non-summary content natively. --%>
${content}

items.docs: Copy accordion.jte + accordion/item.jte + accordion.css to your lievit/ JTE root. Use @template.lievit.accordion(variant=..., content=@`...`) as the container and @template.lievit.accordion-item(label=..., id=..., content=@`...`) for each panel. Uncontrolled: omit open= on items (browser persists state). Controlled: bind open="${serverField.contains(itemId)}" on each item (the server renders truth; no @Wire on the accordion itself -- the CALLER's WIRE component owns the state). Single-open mode: give all sibling items the same name= attribute -- the browser enforces at most one open. Disabled items: disabled=true on the item (aria-disabled + pointer-events-none on the summary). Suppress the panel landmark role via panelRole="" when content is trivial. Link accordion.css (or paste its rules) for chevron rotation; the chevron transition reads --lv-duration-fast + --lv-ease from your token sheet.

items.name: accordion
items.description: A disclosure group: several triggers each expand/collapse their own region, single or multiple open. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the open set lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-accordion> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG accordion (per-item button[aria-expanded][aria-controls] + region[aria-labelledby]).

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import dev.lievit.wire.AccordionComponent @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui accordion WIRE template -- a disclosure GROUP whose open set lives server-side in AccordionComponent (ADR-0012, Wave 2). Pairs with registry/wire/accordion/AccordionComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open set is server state (a @Wire list); each trigger ARMS its item via the `$set('toggleId', '')` magic (the canonical wire:click="$set(...)" idiom this repo already uses in listing-list.jte), the server re-renders this template, the client morphs it. There is no (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter); the panel BODIES are NOT a JTE Content slot supplied at call time -- they are OWNED markup inside each region. The adopter edits the body region per item directly (this is copy-in source). That is the server-first equivalent of "children": owned, server-rendered, cannot fail to project. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Accordion pattern, Radix Accordion as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - each trigger is a real
<%-- Owned body region for this item: replace with your panel markup (server-rendered). --%>

Panel content for ${label}.

@endfor

items.docs: Register AccordionComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `itemIds` + `labels` (index-aligned) and `mode` ('single' | 'multiple') as locked server config, then render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.accordion(...)`. Each trigger arms its item with `l:click="$set('toggleId','<id>')"`; the @LievitRender hook flips it in the open set and clears the arm (one click, fully server-side). The panel BODY per item is OWNED markup inside the `data-accordion-region` div: edit it in this copied-in template (there is no <slot>). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/accordion")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: alert
items.description:
A contextual message banner (info/success/warning/destructive) whose variant drives the ARIA live-region role by intent (info/success → role=status polite; warning/destructive → role=alert assertive) and the token-pair (surface tint + foreground + border). Static presentational (PARTIAL): no JS, no client state. Slots: icon (boolean, default true, overridable via iconName), title (String heading as

, not a heading element), content (gg.jte.Content body), action (optional inline action below the body), dismiss button when closable=true (a real

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui alert/title partial -- the heading line of an alert (server-first, no JS). The shadcn AlertTitle: col-start-2 line-clamp-1 min-h-4 font-medium tracking-tight. A standalone data-slot for callers composing an alert body by hand; the alert partial inlines the same markup when you pass its `title` slot. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure token-styled text. Params: gg.jte.Content content the title text. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the title. Usage: @@template.lievit.alert.title(content = @@`Could not save`) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""

Usage: @@template.lievit.alert.description(content = @@`The listing was not persisted.`) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui alert/action partial -- an optional control region pinned under the alert message (server-first, no JS). shadcn composes an AlertAction (a button / link to act on the alert, e.g. "Retry" / "Dismiss"); here it is a real OWNED control in column 2, below the description, so it is announced within the same live region. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The control itself (a real /

items.docs:
Copies the alert family to your JTE root (lievit/alert.jte + lievit/alert/{title,description,action}.jte), reached as @template.lievit.alert(variant=..., content=@`...`). The variant param (info|success|warning|destructive) determines the ARIA live-region role by intent (info/success → role=status/polite; warning/destructive → role=alert/assertive) and the colour token pair. Pass icon=true (default) to show the variant-default Lucide icon, or iconName="" to override it; icon=false suppresses the icon entirely. Set title="..." (String, renders as

) for an optional heading; the content slot carries the description below it. Set closable=true to render a dismiss

items.name: alert-dialog
items.description:
A headless controlled/uncontrolled alertdialog panel partial (role='alertdialog', WAI-ARIA APG Alertdialog + Modal Dialog). Renders INSIDE a consuming dialog WIRE component's owned template region; the parent's open @Wire field controls open-state. Provides a bounded confirm overlay: title (

, aria-labelledby) + description (

, aria-describedby, MANDATORY for alertdialog) + confirm/cancel action pair wired via l:click. Variant=destructive|warning|default drives the confirm button intent token pair + optional icon tint. Cancel button is always ghost + receives initial focus (least-destructive action, APG irreversible-step prescription). Focus trap, Tab/Shift+Tab cycling, Esc-to-cancel, body scroll lock, and focus restore are all owned by the shared focus-trap enhancer (data-lievit-focus-trap + data-lievit-escape-action on the panel root). No close-X, no scrim-click-to-close (data-closable='false' prevents it). confirmWireArgs + cancelWireArgs use the SAFE escaped channel (Escape.htmlAttribute per value). No @Wire field, no Java component. APG Alertdialog: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/alertdialog/

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui alert-dialog partial -- a headless controlled/uncontrolled confirm overlay panel (role="alertdialog") that renders INSIDE a consuming dialog WIRE component's owned template region. The open-state lives in the parent DialogComponent's @Wire boolean open field; this partial controls only the alertdialog surface: title, description, confirm button, cancel button. NO @Wire field, NO Java component. TIER: PARTIAL (composes the dialog WIRE component + the shared focus-trap enhancer it already owns). The parent dialog shell provides the scrim + positioning overlay; this partial renders the alertdialog panel that goes inside that shell. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - WAI-ARIA APG Alertdialog pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/alertdialog/) + APG Modal Dialog keyboard/focus spec (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/). role="alertdialog" is MANDATORY (not role="dialog"): it signals both alert (demands immediate attention) AND dialog (traps focus). Screen readers announce the name + description text immediately on open without user navigation. A plain dialog must not be used here. - Inventory reference: Ant Design Modal confirm() API (destructive + warning variants, confirm/cancel pair, no arbitrary body content). - Styling: ORIGINAL generation over --lv-* (OKLCH) tokens; visual look inspired by Tailwind UI (NO code copied). A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Alertdialog, Modal): - Panel root: role="alertdialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="{titleId}" aria-describedby="{descId}". aria-describedby is MANDATORY here -- a missing one is an axe-core violation for role="alertdialog". - Title: real

(not a div). Semantic heading at level 2 (page h1 is outer context; the dialog is a subordinate section). - Description: real

. Must NOT be a complex structure; use dialog for that. - Icon slot wrapper (when used): aria-hidden="true" -- icon is decorative; title+description carry the accessible content. - Confirm button: <%-- Confirm button: variant-driven fill, may be loading. SECOND in DOM. Hover: CSS [data-variant] attribute selector in alert-dialog.css drives the hover tint via --lv-* tokens (no inline on* handlers -- the strict CSP refuses them). --%>

items.docs: Renders the alertdialog panel inside a parent dialog WIRE component's owned template. Pass title (required, wires aria-labelledby) and description (required, wires aria-describedby -- mandatory for role='alertdialog'). Wire the two action buttons via confirmWireClick + cancelWireClick (l:click action names) and confirmWireArgs + cancelWireArgs (SAFE per-row escaped args). Set variant='destructive' (default) | 'warning' | 'default' for the confirm button intent. Use loading=true to show a spinner on the confirm button while the round-trip is in-flight (cancel stays active). Pass iconSlot with @template.lievit.icon(...) for a variant-tinted icon left of the title. The focus-trap enhancer handles initial focus (cancel button via data-initial-focus), Tab/Shift+Tab cycle, Esc-to-cancel (via data-cancel-action), scroll lock, and focus restore. The parent dialog shell provides the scrim and overlay positioning.

items.name: alert-dialog
items.description: A confirm modal: an interruptive prompt requiring a response (delete this? discard?). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012, Wave 3), the role=alertdialog specialization of the dialog wire: open-state + title/description/labels + the destructive flag live in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the destructive action + cancel are real l:click buttons, not slots. Renders the dialog wire's overlay/panel structure directly (no nested live instance, no <lv-dialog> island). WAI-ARIA APG alertdialog (role=alertdialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); the backdrop does NOT dismiss (only the explicit buttons do); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.

files.content: @import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui alert-dialog WIRE template -- a confirm modal whose open-state lives server-side in AlertDialogComponent (ADR-0012, Wave 3). Pairs with registry/wire/alert-dialog/AlertDialogComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `open` action and the destructive action + cancel buttons fire `confirm` / `cancel` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No , no : this renders the dialog wire's STRUCTURE (overlay + panel + hidden-when-closed) directly, specialized to a confirm prompt. A wire template is rendered with ONLY its own @Wire fields + _component (JteTemplateAdapter, the Wave 0 finding), so one wire component cannot nest another as a live instance; "composing the dialog" therefore means rendering its overlay/panel structure here, owned and server-rendered, which is what this template does. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Alert Dialog pattern, Radix AlertDialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit-island-backed partial cited): - the panel is role="alertdialog" + aria-modal="true" (NOT role="dialog": an alertdialog is an interruptive prompt requiring a response, so the backdrop does NOT dismiss it; the only exits are the explicit confirm / cancel buttons); - aria-labelledby -> the title id; aria-describedby -> the description id when set; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree; - the footer is two real

items.docs: Register AlertDialogComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.alert-dialog(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` from a trigger to show the prompt, `confirm` (the destructive/primary action) or `cancel` to close. Do the real destructive work server-side by overriding `confirm()` (with authz) before it closes, or read the `confirmed` wire flag on the host after the round-trip. An alertdialog is interruptive: the backdrop does NOT dismiss it, only the explicit buttons do. Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-alert-dialog-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the `cancel` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=cancel button, which is also the initial-focus element via data-lv-autofocus -- the focus-on-cancel default for an interruptive prompt), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/alert-dialog")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: aspect-ratio
items.description: A CSS layout wrapper that constrains its content to a fixed width-to-height ratio using the native CSS `aspect-ratio` property (Baseline 2022). Two-element pattern: outer div handles visual framing (border-radius, border, overflow); inner div enforces the ratio. Semantically transparent: no role or aria-* on the wrapper. Purely presentational PARTIAL; no JS, no enhancer.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui aspect-ratio partial -- a CSS layout wrapper that constrains its content to a fixed width-to-height ratio. Single responsibility: maintain the declared ratio regardless of available width. Composable for responsive images, embedded videos, maps, skeleton placeholders, or any block content that must not reflow into an unwanted aspect. TIER: PARTIAL -- purely presentational CSS layout wrapper. No client state, no enhancer, no wire round-trip, no JavaScript. The content defines the accessible semantics; the wrapper is transparent. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the CSS `aspect-ratio` property is the correct modern mechanism (Baseline in all evergreen browsers since 2022; verified against caniuse.com). The legacy padding-top hack (padding-top: 56.25% + absolutely-positioned child) required non-semantic markup and extra positioning context -- rejected on ergonomics and readability. shadcn/ui AspectRatio consulted as inventory reference (wrapper + ratio mechanism); output is original generation over --lv-* tokens (no code copied). Two-element pattern (outer: visual framing -- border-radius, border, overflow clip at the correct layer; inner: ratio enforcement via aspect-ratio + size-full fill) is the idiomatic approach used by every major CSS framework for this component: it gives the adopter a clean hook for overflow control without clipping the border-radius at the wrong element. A single element with aspect-ratio on it does not allow clean overflow:hidden at the frame while keeping the content unclipped. A11y: NONE on the wrapper (negative confirmation verified against WAI-ARIA APG patterns index at https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/). This wrapper is semantically transparent: screen readers and assistive technologies see through it to the content inside. Adding any role or aria-* attribute to the wrapper would interfere with the content's own landmark, role, or name. The outer div carries NO role, NO aria-*. The inner div carries NO role, NO aria-*. Accessible semantics belong entirely to the content: - an img inside carries alt (mandatory; supplied by the adopter's content slot); - a video inside carries its own accessible controls; - an iframe inside carries title; - a skeleton inside may carry aria-busy="true" (see the loading-section spec). The outer div MUST NOT carry role="img" even when the content is an image -- the img itself carries role=img and alt; adding it to the wrapper double-announces. CREDITS: - Inventory: shadcn/ui AspectRatio used as inventory reference (wrapper + padding-top trick vs modern aspect-ratio property baseline); Ant Design has no equivalent (delegates to CSS utils). - Styling: original generation over --lv-* tokens; no code copied from any source. - A11y: negative confirmation against WAI-ARIA APG patterns index + HTML spec transparent content model concept (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/). Params: int ratioX = 16 width part of the ratio (e.g. 16 in 16:9). Typed int: type-safe, no format validation needed, template owns formatting into CSS. int ratioY = 9 height part of the ratio (e.g. 9 in 16:9). Any positive integer pair valid. String overflow = "hidden" CSS overflow on the inner content element: "hidden" | "visible" | "auto". Emitted as an inline overflow style on the outer element (matching the layer that clips the border-radius). String rounded = "" token-driven corner radius: "" (none) | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "full" -- mapped to --lv-radius-* via inline style on the outer element. boolean border = false renders a 1px border using --lv-color-border on the outer element. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes appended to the outer element. String innerClass = "" extra utility classes appended to the inner content element. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only (id, data-testid). Never feed user-supplied or per-row DB values through this channel. gg.jte.Content content (required) the child content: image, video, iframe, skeleton, or any block. Usage: @@template.lievit.aspect-ratio(content = @@` `) @@template.lievit.aspect-ratio(ratioX = 1, ratioY = 1, rounded = "full", cssClass = "max-w-xs", content = @@``) --%> @param int ratioX = 16 @param int ratioY = 9 @param String overflow = "hidden" @param String rounded = "" @param boolean border = false @param String cssClass = "" @param String innerClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param gg.jte.Content content <%-- Compute the CSS custom property value. Java string concatenation, not ${} inside a Java string literal (only valid in HTML template position). The ratio is authored as two typed ints so the call-site is type-safe and the partial owns the CSS formatting. --%> !{var _ratio = ratioX + " / " + ratioY;} !{var _dataRatio = ratioX + ":" + ratioY;} <%-- border-radius: mapped from rounded param to --lv-radius-* token. Empty = no radius. --%> !{var _radiusStyle = switch (rounded) { case "sm" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-sm);"; case "md" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-md);"; case "lg" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-lg);"; case "xl" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-xl);"; case "full" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-full);"; default -> ""; };} <%-- border: 1px solid --lv-color-border when true; empty string otherwise. --%> !{var _borderStyle = border ? "border:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);" : "";} <%-- overflow: controls whether content clips at the outer framing element. --%> !{var _overflowStyle = "overflow:" + overflow + ";";} <%-- outer element inline style: --lv-ar-ratio CSS custom property + radius + border + overflow. --lv-ar-ratio is a per-instance runtime value (not a design-constant token in lievit-tokens.css) namespaced --lv- to stay within the library's CSS custom property namespace and to be overridable via CSS by an adopter who needs to dynamically change the ratio. --%> !{var _outerStyle = "--lv-ar-ratio:" + _ratio + ";" + _overflowStyle + _radiusStyle + _borderStyle;} <%-- inner element: enforces the numeric ratio; size-full fills the constrained box. --%> !{var _innerStyle = "aspect-ratio:var(--lv-ar-ratio);width:100%;height:100%;";}
${content}

items.docs: Copies the aspect-ratio partial to your JTE root at lievit/aspect-ratio.jte, reached as @template.lievit.aspect-ratio(content = @`...`). Default ratio is 16:9 (ratioX=16, ratioY=9). Pass typed int params for any ratio: @template.lievit.aspect-ratio(ratioX=1, ratioY=1, rounded="full", cssClass="max-w-xs", content=@``). Common ratios: 16:9 (video/hero), 4:3 (document), 1:1 (square/avatar), 3:2 (photo), 21:9 (cinematic), 9:16 (portrait/Reels). Use overflow="visible" when content inside needs to escape the clip (e.g. a tooltip anchor). Use border=true for a framing border. Add corner radius via rounded param (sm|md|lg|xl|full).

items.name: avatar
items.description: A circular (or square) frame displaying a person or entity: image, auto-derived initials, or icon fallback. Diameter-based size scale (xs/sm/md/lg/xl/2xl, architecture-contract §5.b D5 -- NOT toolbar-aligned), circle|square shape, six intent colors with deterministic auto-hash from name, optional status dot (online/away/busy/offline, suppressed at xs), interactive wrappers (<a href> or <button>), ariaHidden decorative mode. Static presentational, zero JS, CSP-clean. Params: src, name, alt, initials, icon, size, shape, color, status, href, hrefLabel, clickable, ariaLabel, ariaHidden, cssClass, attrs, dataAttrs.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui avatar/group-count partial -- the "+N" overflow tile that closes an overlapping avatar stack (shadcn new-york-v4 AvatarGroupCount, data-slot="avatar-group-count"). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The overflow count is a server fact (the server already knows how many avatars it did NOT render), so there is no client state: the whole tile is server-rendered, zero JS, CSP-clean. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn renders AvatarGroupCount as a muted, round tile the same diameter as the avatars it sits beside, carrying the same ring as the group's other tiles so it reads as a member of the overlapping stack. We reproduce that with the same muted-bg/muted-fg token pair avatar/kbd/empty use and the avatar size scale (sm/default/lg -> the same dims). A11y: the tile states the count in text (e.g. "+3"); it is not interactive and carries no role. If the count links to a "see all" view the ADOPTER wraps the call in an . Params: int count overflow count rendered as "+". String size = "default" one of: sm | default | lg (mirror the sibling avatars' size). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes (e.g. the group ring: ring-2 ring-[var(--lv-color-bg)]). Usage:
@@template.lievit.avatar(name = "A", initials = "A", cssClass = "ring-2 ring-[var(--lv-color-bg)]") @@template.lievit.avatar(name = "B", initials = "B", cssClass = "ring-2 ring-[var(--lv-color-bg)]") @@template.lievit.avatar.group-count(count = 3, cssClass = "ring-2 ring-[var(--lv-color-bg)]")
--%> @param int count @param String size = "default" @param String cssClass = "" !{var dim = size.equals("sm") ? "1.5rem" : size.equals("lg") ? "2.5rem" : "2rem";} +${count}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui avatar/badge partial -- the small status dot that overlaps an avatar's corner (shadcn new-york-v4 AvatarBadge, data-slot="avatar-badge"): an online/notification indicator. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The dot's presence + colour are a server fact (the user IS online, there ARE N notifications), so no client state: server-rendered, zero JS. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's AvatarBadge is an absolutely-positioned round tile in the avatar's bottom-right, filled with a token pair and ringed with the page surface so it reads off the photo. Because our avatar partial is overflow-hidden (it clips the photo to a circle), the badge cannot live INSIDE it; the adopter wraps the avatar in a `relative` span and drops this badge as a sibling, exactly the shadcn Root>Badge nesting expressed server-first. The fill defaults to the primary token (shadcn's bg-primary); an optional content slot carries a tiny glyph or count, else the dot is a plain status indicator. A11y: a status dot is decorative unless it carries meaning; pass a non-blank `label` to announce it (role="img" + aria-label, e.g. "Online"), else it is aria-hidden so it adds no noise. Params: String size = "default" one of: sm | default | lg (match the avatar it sits on). String label = "" accessible label (e.g. "Online"); "" => decorative (aria-hidden). gg.jte.Content content = null optional tiny glyph / count inside the dot. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes (e.g. a status fill: bg-[var(--lv-color-success)]). Usage: wrap the avatar in a relative span, drop the badge as a sibling. @@template.lievit.avatar(name = "Ada", initials = "A") @@template.lievit.avatar.badge(label = "Online", cssClass = "bg-[var(--lv-color-success)]") --%> @param String size = "default" @param String label = "" @param gg.jte.Content content = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var dim = size.equals("sm") ? "0.5rem" : size.equals("lg") ? "0.75rem" : "0.625rem";} !{var labelled = label != null && !label.isBlank();} @if(content != null)${content}@endif

items.docs: Copies avatar.jte (+ avatar/group-count.jte and avatar/badge.jte) to your JTE root. Reached as @template.lievit.avatar(name=..., src=..., size="md"). Size is diameter-based (xs=24px, sm=32px, md=40px, lg=48px, xl=64px, 2xl=80px -- NOT toolbar-aligned per architecture-contract D5). shape="circle" (default) or "square". color sets the intent background for initials/icon (primary/secondary/accent/success/warning/destructive/default); null auto-hashes from name so the same person always gets the same hue. status="online|away|busy|offline" overlays a colored dot (suppressed at xs). Set href for a link wrapper or clickable=true for a button wrapper (provide hrefLabel/ariaLabel for an accessible name). Set ariaHidden=true for a decorative avatar where adjacent text identifies the person. Dynamic per-row data goes through the SAFE dataAttrs=Map.of("user-id", row.id()) channel; static wire directives go through attrs (TRUSTED, $unsafe, author-typed only). Adds two additive tokens: --lv-space-20 (5rem, 2xl diameter) and --lv-text-2xs (0.625rem, xs initials). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: badge
items.description: A small status pill (inert <span> or asChild <a> link) mapping each variant to a token fill + foreground pair. Two variant families side by side: the shadcn taxonomy (default, secondary, destructive, outline, ghost, link) and the status family (neutral, success, warning, danger, info). Size scale (sm/md/lg) governs text size + padding (NOT height — badge is inline, not a form control). Optional dot (coloured circle, aria-hidden) and leading slot. Pass href for an asChild <a> link (else inert <span>). Extra attributes via two escaping channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, $unsafe, STATIC author strings only) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map<String,String>, each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute for per-row DB values). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 4): variant-to-colour is pure, no client state, no island. Replaces the <lv-badge> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies the badge partial to your JTE root at lievit/badge.jte, reached as @template.lievit.badge(variant=..., label="..."). Variant drives the fill + foreground (+ border for outline) tokens across the shadcn names (default/secondary/destructive/outline/ghost/link) and the status names (neutral/success/warning/danger/info). Size is sm/md/lg (text + padding scale; not height-based -- badges are inline elements, not form controls). Pass label for plain text or a content slot for rich children (content wins over label). Pass dot=true to render a coloured indicator dot before the label (aria-hidden; always pair with a non-empty label -- dot-only is colour-as-sole-signal). Pass a leading Content slot for an icon before the label. Pass href to render the pill as a real <a> link (asChild). Extra attributes via two channels by trust: attrs is a TRUSTED raw string ($unsafe, static author-typed strings ONLY such as target/_blank, rel/noopener, or wire directives -- NEVER per-row DB values); dataAttrs is a SAFE Map<String,String> of dynamic data-* attributes whose values are HTML-escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute (the only safe way to carry user/DB-derived data). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: breadcrumb
items.description: Secondary navigation trail showing the user's current location in a page hierarchy (<nav aria-label> landmark + ordered <ol>, real <a href> for non-current items, aria-current='page' on the current last item, aria-hidden separators). WAI-ARIA APG Breadcrumb (nav of links) pattern. Size param scales type (sm/md/lg), not height. Collapsed mode hides middle items behind a keyboard-reachable <button> ellipsis; expansion is a server-side controller decision (the PARTIAL is stateless). Href scheme allowlist guard (only /, #, http://, https:// trusted). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012), no enhancer, no JS.

files.content:
@import java.util.List @import java.util.Map <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui breadcrumb partial -- secondary navigation trail showing the user's current location in a page hierarchy (e.g. Home / Customers / Acme Corp / Edit). TIER: PARTIAL presentational (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). The breadcrumb is STATIC: the server already knows the route being rendered, so it emits the full
in one pass. Zero client state, no enhancer. The former Lit island is gone. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Breadcrumb (nav of links) https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/breadcrumb/ https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/breadcrumb/examples/breadcrumb/ A
landmark wraps an ordered
    ; the ordinal communicates root-to-current sequence to assistive technology. Each non-current item is a real (the browser navigates; middle-click + open-in-new-tab work; no JS). The current (last) item is when it has an href, or when href is null (correctly removed from tab order). Separators are dedicated
  1. nodes so screen readers count ONLY the crumb items. The collapsed ellipsis is a
    2. <%-- Separator before last --%>
  2. ${separator}
    3. <%-- Last (current) item --%> !{var _last = items.get(_n - 1);} !{var _lastHref = _last.get("href");} !{var _lastAllowedHref = (_lastHref != null && (_lastHref.startsWith("/") || _lastHref.startsWith("#") || _lastHref.startsWith("http://") || _lastHref.startsWith("https://"))) ? _lastHref : null;}
  3. @if(_lastAllowedHref != null) ${_last.getOrDefault("label", "")} @else ${_last.getOrDefault("label", "")} @endif
    4. @else <%-- Full trail: render every item with separators between them. --%> @for(int _i = 0; _i < _n; _i++) !{var _item = items.get(_i);} !{var _isCurrent = _i == _n - 1;} !{var _rawHref = _item.get("href");} !{var _safeHref = (_rawHref != null && (_rawHref.startsWith("/") || _rawHref.startsWith("#") || _rawHref.startsWith("http://") || _rawHref.startsWith("https://"))) ? _rawHref : null;} !{var _itemAttrs = _item.getOrDefault("attrs", "");} <%-- Separator before every item except the first --%> @if(_i > 0)
  4. ${separator}
    5. @endif @if(_isCurrent)
  5. @if(_safeHref != null) <%-- Current item has an href: still an (the adopter may link the current page to itself for reload); aria-current="page" marks it per APG. --%> ${_item.getOrDefault("label", "")} @else <%-- Current item without href: a . NOT in tab order (correct; the user is already here). --%> ${_item.getOrDefault("label", "")} @endif
    6. @else
  6. @if(_safeHref != null) ${_item.getOrDefault("label", "")} @else <%-- Non-current item without href: a , styled but not interactive. --%> ${_item.getOrDefault("label", "")} @endif
    7. @endif @endfor @endif

items.docs: Copies the breadcrumb partial to your JTE root at lievit/breadcrumb.jte, reached as @template.lievit.breadcrumb(items=...). Pass items as a List<Map<String,String>> root-to-current; each map carries label (required), href (optional; only /, #, http://, https:// allowed — others render as non-link span), and attrs (optional, TRUSTED raw author-controlled extras). The last item is always the current page (aria-current='page'). Set navLabel to override the nav landmark's aria-label (e.g. for i18n). Set separator to change the divider glyph (default '/'). Set size to 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' for type-scale sizing. Set collapsed=true with maxVisible (default 3) to hide the middle items behind an ellipsis button; the button is keyboard-reachable but inert until wired in a WIRE context. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: builder
items.description: A list of HETEROGENEOUS typed blocks (Filament's Builder): unlike the repeater (the same schema repeated), a builder mixes DIFFERENT block types in one ordered list (a page builder: heading | paragraph | image), chosen from an add-block menu. Each block carries a hidden `<name>[<i>][__type]` plus its type-specific fields named `<name>[<i>][...]`, POSTing as a typed indexed form-array the server rebuilds into an ordered list of typed records, with zero JS. The block bodies are RECURSIVE content slots the adopter authors (existing blocks via the builder.block sub-partial, one blank `<template data-builder-template=type>` per type for the clone). JS-OFF the add-block menu is a row of submit buttons one per type (name=`<name>__addblock`, value=type) and per-block remove is a submit (name=`<name>__remove`, value=index), both server round-trips -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer clones the matching per-type template on add and drops a card on remove, in place, then re-indexes every field name (the __type included) + label so indices stay contiguous. The native inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: labelled fieldset/legend, a labelled add-block menu of typed buttons, each card a labelled region, labelled remove, aria-live. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui builder partial -- a list of HETEROGENEOUS typed blocks (Filament's Builder): unlike the repeater (the SAME schema repeated), a builder mixes DIFFERENT block types in one ordered list (e.g. a page builder: "heading" | "paragraph" | "image"), chosen from an add-block menu. Each block carries its TYPE + its type-specific fields, POSTing as an indexed form-array the server rebuilds into an ordered list of typed records. TIER: PARTIAL (+ optional enhancer, ADR-0012 server-first). Each block is a card whose fields name themselves `[][...]` PLUS a hidden `[][__type]` carrying the block's type, so the server knows which record shape each index is. The block BODIES are RECURSIVE content slots the adopter authors -- existing blocks via `blocksContent`, and one blank `

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui builder.block sub-partial -- ONE typed block card inside @@template.lievit.builder. The adopter calls this per existing record, passing the field prefix, the block's index + type, and the block's body (the recursive content slot of that type's fields). It renders the card chrome (the type label + a remove submit) and the hidden `[][__type]` field that tells the server which record shape this index is, then drops in the adopter's body. TIER: PARTIAL fragment (no JS of its own; the builder enhancer wires add/remove + re-indexing). A11y: the card is a labelled region (" N"); remove is a real
${content}

items.docs: Copies the builder partial (lievit/builder.jte, reached as @template.lievit.builder(name=..., blockTypes=..., blocksContent=@`...`)), the builder.block sub-partial (lievit/builder/block.jte, one typed block card) and the CSP-clean enhancer (lievit/builder.enhancer.ts). Import the enhancer once and call enhanceAllBuilders() on load (and after a DOM swap). Pass `blockTypes` (type key -> menu label Map), `count`, render existing blocks in `blocksContent` (loop your records, each a @template.lievit.builder.block), and author one blank full block card per type in `templates` as `<template data-builder-template=type>` using the `__i__` index token. JS-OFF add is the per-type submit (`<name>__addblock`, value=type) and remove is `<name>__remove` (value=index); JS-ON the enhancer clones the matching template / drops a card in place and re-indexes (the hidden `__type` included). The enhancer is self-contained (it inlines the index-rewrite helper, no cross-file import). Requires the icon partial (plus, trash-2). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: button
items.description: A token-styled button (or link styled as a button) with Filament-grade height-based sizing (sm/md/lg, shadcn h-9 baseline) that aligns to the form controls in a toolbar, an iconOnly square mode for icon actions, and primary/secondary/destructive/destructive-outline/destructive-ghost/ghost/outline variants (the two destructive-* are the red-but-not-solid actions) with shadcn hover + aria-invalid ring states. Net-new (v-next): loading=true shows an inline spinner, sets aria-busy=true, and blocks activation (dims + inert) without the permanent 'not available' semantic of disabled. The click is wired by the consuming wire template via l:click; the partial renders only the styled control. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui button partial -- a token-styled button (or link styled as a button). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island only styled a native @endif

items.docs: Copies the button partial to your JTE root at lievit/button.jte, reached as @template.lievit.button(variant=..., size=..., content=@`...`). size is height-based (sm=h-8/32px, md=h-9/36px the shadcn baseline, lg=h-10/40px) so a button sits flush next to a form control in a toolbar; set iconOnly=true (with ariaLabel) for a square icon-only action button. Set loading=true to show an inline spinner, set aria-busy=true, and block activation: the button dims + becomes inert while a wire action is in flight, without the permanent 'not available' semantic of disabled. Wire a per-row action SAFELY with wireClick="revokeDevice" (emitted as l:click="...", escaped) plus wireArgs=Map.of("id", row.id()) (escaped data-* the handler reads) -- no hand-rolled button in a reactive list. Or pass href to render a real link. Extra attributes go through two channels by trust: attrs is a TRUSTED raw string ($unsafe, static author-typed strings ONLY -- wire directives, target/rel) and dataAttrs is a SAFE Map<String,String> of dynamic data-* attributes whose values are HTML-escaped (the only safe way to carry user/DB-derived data; keys are the data-attribute name without the data- prefix, simple identifiers; wireArgs merge into this escaped channel). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: button-group
items.description: Joins buttons (and/or nested groups, selects, input-groups) into a segmented control: a role=group flex box whose CSS collapses the shared inner rounding + borders between adjacent children (shadcn's ButtonGroup, horizontal or vertical). Pure layout over the children the caller passes in, no client state; focus-visible children raise their z-index so the ring is never clipped by a neighbour's border. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean. Accepts an `attrs` param (TRUSTED raw $unsafe) for static author-typed strings such as data-testid.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui button-group partial -- a layout wrapper that collapses inner border-radius and merges adjacent borders on any children (buttons, inputs, selects, icon buttons). TIER: PARTIAL presentational -- pure layout/styling, zero client state, zero JS. Server-first is trivial: a layout container has nothing interactive of its own. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Toolbar structural pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/toolbar/) -- button-group maps to the STRUCTURAL half: role="group" with an optional aria-label so AT announces the set as one labelled unit. No roving tabindex (the toolbar keyboard model) because this wraps HETEROGENEOUS controls; each child is an independent tab stop. Ant Design Button Group (border-collapse, shared radius reset) as inventory reference for the visual join trick. Tailwind UI inspired styling. A11y (WAI-ARIA role=group): Roles:
-- unlabelled group is valid (role=region requires a label; role=group does not). Children carry their own roles (native button/input/a). Keyboard: platform. Each child is an independent tab stop (Tab/Shift+Tab). No roving tabindex. Focus ring: [&>*:focus-visible]:relative [&>*:focus-visible]:z-10 raises focused children above their neighbours so the ring is never clipped by a collapsed border. No live region. No shared enhancer (layout wrapper; simplest tier). Params: gg.jte.Content content REQUIRED. Children to join. String orientation = "horizontal" "horizontal" | "vertical" -- flex axis + join axis. String label = null aria-label on the root div. null = unlabelled (valid). String cssClass = "" Extra utility classes on the root element. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe). STATIC author-typed strings only (e.g. data-testid="toolbar"). Never user/DB data. Usage: @@template.lievit.button-group(label = "Text alignment", content = @@` @@template.lievit.button(content = @@`Left`) @@template.lievit.button(content = @@`Center`) @@template.lievit.button(content = @@`Right`) `) @@template.lievit.button-group(orientation = "vertical", attrs = "data-testid=\"actions\"", content = @@` `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String orientation = "horizontal" @param String label = null @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" !{var horizontal = !"vertical".equals(orientation);} !{var axis = horizontal ? "flex-row" : "flex-col";} !{var joinReset = horizontal ? "[&>*:not(:first-child)]:rounded-l-none [&>*:not(:first-child)]:border-l-0 [&>*:not(:last-child)]:rounded-r-none" : "[&>*:not(:first-child)]:rounded-t-none [&>*:not(:first-child)]:border-t-0 [&>*:not(:last-child)]:rounded-b-none";}
${content}

items.docs: Copies the button-group partial to your JTE root at lievit/button-group.jte, reached as @template.lievit.button-group(content=@`...`). Pass the buttons (or nested groups / selects / input-groups) as the content slot; they join seamlessly with collapsed inner rounding and shared borders. Set orientation="vertical" to stack on the vertical axis, and label for the group's aria-label (announced as one labelled group). Use attrs (TRUSTED raw, static strings only) to pass additional HTML attributes such as data-testid. Keyboard behaviour stays each child's own (Tab moves between them); a button-group is not a radio/toolbar, so no roving tabindex is imposed. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: calendar
items.description: A full event calendar (month/week/day) rendered server-first (ADR-0012, R1 RESOLVED). The grid, the events placed in its cells, the view, the anchor date and the filter all live in typed Java; the server renders the grid as HTML and the client morphs each re-render. The wire optimization toolkit absorbs round-trip latency: l:init/skeleton for first paint, l:loading feedback, l:model.debounce filter, l:click navigation. The one irreducible client bit -- event drag onto a day cell -- is a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer that fires the moveEvent action on drop (no Lit, no @event-calendar). Retires the <lv-calendar> Lit island and gest's ht-calendar escape-hatch. WAI-ARIA APG grid pattern.

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import dev.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent @import dev.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent.Cell @import dev.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent.Event @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui calendar WIRE template -- a full event calendar (month/week/day) whose grid, the events placed in its cells, the view, the anchor and the filter all live SERVER-SIDE (ADR-0012, R1 RESOLVED). Pairs with registry/wire/calendar/CalendarComponent.java + the calendar.ts drag enhancer. This is the single heaviest piece of the refactor: it retires both the Lit island AND gest's ht-calendar (@event-calendar) escape-hatch. There is NO Lit, NO @event-calendar, NO . TIER: WIRE. The grid is server-rendered HTML from the typed model (CalendarComponent.weeks()); the view switch + prev/next/today are l:click actions (the server re-renders the grid, the client morphs it); the filter is a @Wire string bound l:model.debounce.250ms so there is NO round-trip per keystroke. The wire OPTIMIZATION toolkit absorbs the round-trip latency a server-rendered drag-grid would otherwise pay: - Initial grid: when not yet loaded the template ships a SKELETON whose l:init="load" fires the `load` action right after bind (the wire:init parity), so first paint is immediate and the real grid arrives on the next morph; l:loading regions give instant feedback while a call resolves. - Filter/typeahead: l:model.debounce.250ms -> server re-filters a few hundred ms after the last key. - Navigation: l:click prev/next/today/viewMonth/viewWeek/viewDay re-render the resolved period. The one irreducible client bit -- event drag-resize/drag-create -- is the calendar.ts enhancer: on drop it $set's the dragged event id + the target ISO date into the wire and $call's `moveEvent`; the server owns the event store, moves the event in Java and re-renders. addEventListener only, CSP-clean. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Grid pattern for the month layout (the same model the dropped island cited): the grid is a , weekday headers are
, each day is a carrying aria-selected; today carries aria-current="date". A server-rendered grid + a tiny drop enhancer beats a client grid: the events and the layout are both server-held, so nothing can fail to project (the slot bug) and the layout cannot drift; the only client code is the irreducible drop gesture. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Grid): table[role=grid][aria-label], th[role=columnheader], td[role=gridcell] [aria-selected], the today cell aria-current="date"; the toolbar buttons carry aria-labels; the filter input is a labelled searchbox; the loading region is aria-busy while a call is in flight. Params: view String the visible view ("month" | "week" | "day"; round-trips) anchor String the ISO date the visible range is built around (round-trips) filter String the live filter query (bound by l:model.debounce.250ms) selected String the clicked day ISO (server-owned; set via $set) loaded boolean false on mount (skeleton + l:init), true once `load` has fired dragEventId String the event id the drag enhancer arms before moveEvent (round-trips) dragToDate String the ISO day the dragged event is dropped onto (round-trips) _instance CalendarComponent the live instance (reads the server-built grid + the period label) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, seed allEvents in the component from real data keyed by the visible range, register the component as a PROTOTYPE @Bean, call enhanceCalendars(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() so a day-cell drop moves the event server-side): @template.lievit.calendar(...) -- mounted by the wire pipeline. --%> @param String view @param String anchor @param String filter @param String selected @param boolean loaded @param String dragEventId @param String dragToDate @param CalendarComponent _instance @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var label = _instance.periodLabel();} !{List headers = _instance.weekdayHeaders();} !{List> weeks = _instance.weeks();}
<%-- Toolbar: period label + nav + view switch + the debounced filter. --%>
${label} <%-- l:loading region: instant feedback while any wire call for this calendar is in flight. --%> Loading…
@if(!loaded) <%-- First-paint skeleton: l:init fires `load` right after bind so the real grid arrives on the next morph; the skeleton is what the user sees instantly (initial-latency toolkit). --%>
@for(int i = 0; i < 35; i++)
@endfor
@else @for(String h : headers) @endfor @for(List week : weeks) @for(Cell cell : week) @endfor @endfor
${h}
${cell.dayOfMonth()}
@for(Event ev : cell.events()) ${ev.title()} @endfor
@endif <%-- The drag enhancer arms these over the wire ($set) before $call('moveEvent'); kept as hidden mirrors so a hand-built POST (JS off) can still move an event, and so the fields are visibly part of the owned template, not magic. --%>

items.docs: Register CalendarComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `allEvents` from your repository keyed by the visible range (rangeStart()..rangeEnd()) in the constructor / a @LievitRender hook -- the seed list is example data. Render it via the wire mount / @template.lievit.calendar(...); the cells + events are OWNED template markup (NOT a slot). Call enhanceCalendars(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() so dragging an event chip onto a day cell moves it server-side; add server-side authz in moveEvent(). The l:init skeleton makes first paint immediate; the l:model.debounce filter and l:click navigation re-render server-side. The @LievitComponent(template="lievit/calendar") name + the dev.lievit.wire package are yours to change once copied.

items.name: card
items.description: A visual grouping surface (v-next). Five variants (default/outlined/elevated/ghost/destructive), three sizes (sm/md/lg), structured slots (leading/trailing/header/footer/content). The `as` param controls the root element (div/section/article) for correct landmark semantics; as=section with a title emits aria-labelledby for the region. No client state, no enhancer -- the card is a structural PARTIAL. The collapsible use-case is handled by composing the `accordion` WIRE component inside the content slot.
items.docs: Copies the card partial to your JTE root at lievit/card.jte, reached as @template.lievit.card(content=@`...`). Choose variant (default/outlined/elevated/ghost/destructive), size (sm/md/lg), and supply content + optional leading/trailing/header/footer slots. Set as="section" + title for a labelled region landmark (aria-labelledby wired automatically). bordered=false removes the border ring; noPadding=true strips body padding for full-bleed content; fullHeight=true adds h-full for equal-height grid rows. Static author-typed ARIA goes in attrs; per-row dynamic data-* goes in dataAttrs (escaped). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: chart
items.description: A server-rendered interactive-capable chart (bar, line, area, pie, donut, composed) as an accessible inline SVG. Typed @param data (multi-series List<Map<String,String>> or legacy String[]/int[] arrays), --lv-color-chart-N palette tokens, WAI-ARIA role="img" + <title>/<desc> + sr-only <table> fallback. Zero JS baseline; when interactive=true the colocated chart.enhancer.ts is mounted via data-lievit-enhancer="chart" for tooltip, keyboard navigation, series highlight, zoom/pan and lv-chart-mark-click events. No canvas, no client charting library, CSP-clean. PARTIAL (no WIRE Java component) -- the adopting controller builds the series model. v-next re-forge: `label` param renamed to `title`.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: chart.legend - a SERVER-rendered static legend for the chart partial. The shadcn-faithful equivalent of ChartLegendContent (data-slot="chart-legend"): one entry per series, each a coloured swatch + the series name. Here the entries are typed @param data, the swatch is a token-coloured square, and there is zero JS (no client legend, no toggle handlers). Single-series charts (bar / line / area) pass one name + one colour; the pie passes one entry per slice (the slice name + its --lv-color-chart-N token), so the legend is the slice key. A11y: the legend is decorative chrome over the chart's own role=img text alternative (every datum is already announced via the per-shape aria-label), so the swatches are aria-hidden and the list is a plain inline
    ; the names stay readable text. It adds visual key, never a second source of the data to a screen reader. Params: String[] names series / slice names, one per entry. String[] colors fill per entry, aligned positionally with names (each a --lv-color-* token). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the legend. Usage: @@template.lievit.chart.legend(names = labels, colors = palette) --%> @param String[] names @param String[] colors @param String cssClass = ""
      @for(int i = 0; i < names.length; i++) !{String swatch = i < colors.length ? colors[i] : "var(--lv-color-chart-1)";}
    • ${names[i]}
      • @endfor

items.docs: Copies the chart partial family: lievit/chart.jte (multi-series server SVG) + chart/legend.jte + chart.css (shimmer, tooltip, mark states) + chart.enhancer.ts (optional interactive layer). Call as @template.lievit.chart(title="Revenue", series=seriesList, type="bar"). For simple single-series pass categories=String[] + values=int[] (legacy bridge). Set interactive=true to mount the enhancer; zoomable=true for brush-zoom. The chart body is always server-rendered SVG; the enhancer overlays only the interactive UX layer.

items.name: checkbox
items.description: A styled native input[type=checkbox] (two-state and tri-state / indeterminate). The native element carries role=checkbox, Space/Tab activation, checked/disabled, and form-submission for free. The `sr-only peer` pattern hides the input visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree; a peer-driven aria-hidden sibling renders the custom box with an inline SVG check and dash glyph. Three sizes (sm/md/lg) use --lv-space-4/5/6 (16/20/24 px) as the box dimension; checked, focus-visible, disabled, and aria-invalid states are expressed via Tailwind peer-* utilities over --lv-* tokens. Two attribute channels: `attrs` (TRUSTED raw $unsafe -- STATIC author-typed strings such as l:model) and `dataAttrs` (SAFE escaped Map for dynamic per-row data-* values). The indeterminate DOM property is set client-side by checkbox.enhancer.ts (component-specific enhancer; coordinator registers installCheckbox in index.ts).

files.content: @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui checkbox partial -- a styled native input[type=checkbox] (two-state + tri-state). TIER: PARTIAL presentational -> JTE partial. The native checkbox carries role=checkbox, Space/Tab activation, checked/disabled semantics, and form submission for free -- there is no irreducible client state in the control itself. The CHECKED state is rendered server-side via the `checked` param; the INDETERMINATE DOM property (write-only from JS, not an HTML attribute) is set by checkbox.enhancer.ts after hydration. All other styling and ARIA is server-rendered. Server-first works naturally: server renders `checked`; the one irreducible client bit is the `el.indeterminate = true` DOM property set by the enhancer. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - shadcn's Checkbox is a Radix

items.docs: Copies the checkbox partial to your JTE root at lievit/checkbox.jte, reached as @template.lievit.checkbox(...). Renders a native input[type=checkbox] (role=checkbox, Space/Tab, form-submit for free) styled with the sr-only-peer + custom-box pattern. Pass `checked=true` for the initial checked state; pass `indeterminate=true` to render data-indeterminate="true" (the enhancer sets el.indeterminate after hydration so the browser reflects aria-checked="mixed"). Wire to a WIRE field with `attrs="l:model=\"field\""` (TRUSTED raw channel, static string). Carry a SAFE dynamic data-* (filter key, row id) via `dataAttrs=java.util.Map.of("rowid", row.id())` -- each value HTML-escaped, never through attrs. Pass `ariaLabel` when no sibling label element is associated (a11y guard). The `id` param lets a sibling `<label for>` wire to this control; omitting it auto-generates a stable id. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: checkbox-list
items.description: A multi-select FORM control: a fieldset of native input[type=checkbox] options sharing one `name`, with an optional search box and a select-all / clear bulk toggle (Filament's CheckboxList; shadcn composes Checkbox + Command/search). Each option row composes the re-forged lievit checkbox primitive via @template.lievit.checkbox(...) with a SIBLING label[for] (the v-next checkbox has no label param). JS-OFF it is a fully accessible native multi-select that POSTs each checked value under the repeated `name` in a plain form (unchecked boxes are simply absent); the search + bulk-toggle UI stays hidden so there are no dead controls. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer reveals the tools and adds client-side filtering + a one-click select-all that fires native change so l:model and forms see it. The native checkboxes stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA APG Checkbox Group: native fieldset/legend group, aria-required on fieldset, role=searchbox on the filter, aria-pressed + dynamic label on the toggle. Plain HTML + Tailwind v4 + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui checkbox-list partial -- a MULTI-SELECT form control: a fieldset of native input[type=checkbox] options sharing one `name`, with an optional search box and a select-all / clear bulk toggle (Filament's CheckboxList; shadcn has no single equivalent, it composes Checkbox + Command/search). TIER: PARTIAL + component-specific enhancer (ADR-0012 server-first). The options, the checked state, and the legend are server-rendered HTML; each option is a real native checkbox sharing the `name`, so JS-OFF the control is a fully accessible multi-select that POSTs every checked value under the same repeated `name` in a plain form (native checkbox semantics: unchecked boxes are simply absent from FormData). The search and bulk-toggle rows are PURE ENHANCEMENTS layered on top by the CSP-clean enhancer; with JS off the search/select-all UI is hidden (no dead controls) and the user simply ticks boxes. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - A custom listbox would re-implement the checkbox role, Space activation, and the form POST in JS and break JS-off submit. - Native checkboxes give role + keyboard + disabled + form integration for free and POST natively. They are the base layer. - WAI-ARIA APG Checkbox Group: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/checkbox/examples/checkbox-group/ - Ant Design CheckboxGroup (columns, select-all, disabled-group) is the feature inventory reference; no code copied, original generation over --lv-* tokens. - shadcn has no native equivalent; Filament's CheckboxList is the direct inspiration for the search + bulk-toggle progressive-enhancement model. - The checkbox rows compose the re-forged lievit checkbox primitive via @template.lievit.checkbox(...) with a SIBLING label[for] (the v-next checkbox has NO label param by design -- two semantic elements stay separate). A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Checkbox Group): - native fieldset + legend is the APG group; each option is a real input[type=checkbox] + label[for]. The browser supplies role=group on fieldset automatically. - ariaLabel names the fieldset when there is no visible legend (aria-label on fieldset). - aria-required="true" marks the group required when `required=true` (the native `required` attribute on a fieldset is not supported; aria-required is the correct signal here). - invalid sets aria-invalid="true" + data-invalid="true" on the fieldset; each checkbox in the group inherits the error context via ariaInvalid=true on the checkbox primitive. - describedBy points the group at an external hint/error element (aria-describedby). - disabled="true" on the fieldset disables the whole group natively; data-disabled mirrors it for CSS styling hooks. - The search input is a labelled input[role=searchbox] (aria-label = searchPlaceholder); it appears only when JS is active (rendered hidden, enhancer reveals it). - The select-all control is a real button[type=button] with aria-pressed reflecting the all-checked state (synced by the enhancer on every change). Also JS-only. - Colour is never the only signal: the checked state is the native checkbox control; the count/label always states the status in text. OPTIONS: pass an ordered Map of String to String via `options` (value to label) for the simple case, OR a `content` slot of hand-authored option markup for per-option disabled / rich labels. The `content` slot wins when both are supplied. Params: String name the shared checkbox name (POSTs each checked value under the repeated name in a plain form submit). java.util.Map options = null ordered value-to-label option map. gg.jte.Content content = null hand-authored option markup (wins over options). java.util.List values = null the initially-checked values. String label = null visible legend text. String ariaLabel = null aria-label on the fieldset when no visible legend. String describedBy = null id of an external hint/error element (aria-describedby). boolean searchable = false render the search box (JS-on filter; hidden JS-off). boolean bulkToggle = false render the select-all / clear toggle (JS-on). String searchPlaceholder = "Search..." placeholder text inside the search input. String searchAriaLabel = null aria-label on the search input; falls back to searchPlaceholder when null. String selectAllLabel = "Select all" text for the bulk toggle button. String clearLabel = null text when all are checked (toggles to "Clear all"); falls back to selectAllLabel when null. String columns = "1" option grid columns: "1" | "2" | "3". boolean disabled = false disables the whole group (native fieldset disabled). boolean required = false marks the group required (aria-required on fieldset). boolean invalid = false aria-invalid="true" + danger ring on each option. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the fieldset root. Usage: @@template.lievit.checkbox-list( name = "tipologie", label = "Tipologie", searchable = true, bulkToggle = true, columns = "2", options = java.util.Map.of("villa", "Villa", "appartamento", "Appartamento"), values = contatto.tipologie()) @@template.lievit.checkbox-list( name = "tags", ariaLabel = "Tag filtro", bulkToggle = true, options = tagMap, values = selectedTags, invalid = hasError, describedBy = "tags-error") --%> @import gg.jte.Content @param String name @param java.util.Map options = null @param gg.jte.Content content = null @param java.util.List values = null @param String label = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param String describedBy = null @param boolean searchable = false @param boolean bulkToggle = false @param String searchPlaceholder = "Search..." @param String searchAriaLabel = null @param String selectAllLabel = "Select all" @param String clearLabel = null @param String columns = "1" @param boolean disabled = false @param boolean required = false @param boolean invalid = false @param String cssClass = "" !{var checkedSet = values != null ? values : java.util.List.of();} !{var hasTools = searchable || bulkToggle;} !{var gridCols = switch (columns) { case "2" -> "sm:grid-cols-2"; case "3" -> "sm:grid-cols-3"; default -> "grid-cols-1"; };} !{var resolvedSearchAriaLabel = (searchAriaLabel != null && !searchAriaLabel.isBlank()) ? searchAriaLabel : searchPlaceholder;} !{var resolvedClearLabel = (clearLabel != null && !clearLabel.isBlank()) ? clearLabel : selectAllLabel;}
@if(label != null && !label.isBlank()) ${label} @endif @if(hasTools)
@if(searchable)
@template.lievit.icon(name = "search", size = "0.875rem")
@endif @if(bulkToggle) @endif
@endif
@if(content != null) ${content} @elseif(options != null) @for(java.util.Map.Entry opt : options.entrySet()) !{var optId = name + "-" + opt.getKey();}
@template.lievit.checkbox(name = name, id = optId, value = opt.getKey(), checked = checkedSet.contains(opt.getKey()), disabled = disabled, ariaInvalid = invalid)
@endfor @endif

items.docs: Copies the checkbox-list partial to your JTE root at lievit/checkbox-list.jte (reached as @template.lievit.checkbox-list(name=..., options=...)) and the CSP-clean enhancer to lievit/checkbox-list.enhancer.ts. Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllCheckboxLists() on load (and after any DOM swap). JS-OFF the native checkboxes POST every checked value under the repeated name; the search + select-all controls stay hidden. JS-ON the enhancer reveals them and adds client-side filtering + a bulk toggle whose label swaps between selectAllLabel and clearLabel to reflect the all-checked state. Pass `options` (ordered Map of String to String, value to label) or a `content` slot of hand-authored option markup; `values` lists the initially-checked values; `searchable` / `bulkToggle` opt into the progressive tools; `columns` (1 / 2 / 3) sets the grid; `label` gives a visible legend, `ariaLabel` names the group when no legend is visible; `describedBy` points to a hint/error element; `disabled` / `required` / `invalid` manage group state. Requires the checkbox primitive (each option row) and the icon partial (the search glyph). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: chip
items.description: A small rounded tag, optionally removable: the Filament active-filter pill / shadcn Badge-with-dismiss. Same variant token vocabulary as badge (default/secondary/destructive/outline/ghost/link/neutral/success/warning/danger/info).
