Size scale (sm/md/lg) governs text size + padding (NOT height -- chip is inline, not a form control). Optional leading slot for an icon before the label. Pass content for rich children (wins over label). Pass removeHref for a trailing remove x rendered as a real <a href> (server-first removal, plain GET, no JS) and removeLabel for the remove link's aria-label (REQUIRED when removeHref is set; absence is an axe-core link-name violation). Extra attributes via two escaping channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, $unsafe, STATIC author strings only) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map<String,String>, each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute for per-row DB values). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012): no client state, no island, zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies the chip partial to your JTE root at lievit/chip.jte, reached as @template.lievit.chip(label="..."). A generic removable tag: variant drives the fill + foreground tokens (same family as badge: default/secondary/destructive/outline/ghost/link/neutral/success/warning/danger/info); size is sm/md/lg (text + padding scale; not height-based -- chips are inline elements, not form controls). Pass label for plain text or a content slot for rich children (content wins). Pass a leading Content slot for an icon before the label. Pass removeHref to render a trailing x as a real <a href> (server-first removal, plain GET to the URL the server builds; no JS) plus removeLabel for its aria-label (REQUIRED when removeHref is set -- a missing label is an axe-core link-name violation). The template emits a dev warning comment when removeHref is set and removeLabel is absent. Extra attributes via two channels by trust: attrs is a TRUSTED raw string ($unsafe, static author-typed strings ONLY -- NEVER per-row DB values); dataAttrs is a SAFE Map<String,String> of dynamic data-* attributes whose values are HTML-escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute. Domain meaning (which filter/entity) lives in your call, not the component. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: code-editor
items.description: A server-first code form field (Filament code-field / shadcn code parity). The canonical control is a plain monospace <textarea name=...> that POSTs its source with the surrounding <form> JS-off; a CSP-clean island progressively enhances it into a CodeMirror 6 editor (syntax highlight + line numbers + language) whose document is written back into the SAME textarea on every change, so the form keeps POSTing the field with or without JS. CodeMirror 6 is CSP-clean (no eval; theming injects a <style> element via style-mod, not inline style attributes). The CodeMirror engine + language packs are supplied by the adopter through an injectable factory so the version is pinned downstream and the island orchestration stays unit-testable.

files.content: @import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui code-editor partial (Filament code-field / shadcn code parity) -- a SERVER-FIRST code form field. The canonical control is a plain monospace

items.docs: Render the partial with a `name` (the form field) + a `language` (e.g. javascript, sql, json, html). With JS off the monospace <textarea> is the editor and POSTs its source. To enhance: `npm i codemirror @codemirror/state @codemirror/view` + the `@codemirror/lang-*` packs you need, then call enhanceCodeEditors({ factory }) once from main.ts after startLievit(), passing a factory that builds an EditorView (doc, parent=element, basicSetup for line numbers + highlight, an updateListener that calls onUpdate(state.doc.toString()) on docChanged, and the language() extension chosen from the `language` arg). The factory keeps the CodeMirror version + language packs pinned in YOUR app. On submit you receive the field as raw source text. Adjust the template name freely once copied.

items.name: collapsible
items.description: A single trigger that expands/collapses one content region. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): open state lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-collapsible> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG disclosure (button[aria-expanded] + region[aria-labelledby]).

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui collapsible WIRE template -- a single disclosure trigger + region whose open state lives server-side in CollapsibleComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/collapsible/CollapsibleComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `toggle` action over the wire and the server re-renders this template, the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the panel body is NOT a JTE Content slot supplied at call time: it is OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body region directly (this is copy-in source), or drives it from a @Wire field / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children": owned, server-rendered, cannot fail to project. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern, Radix Collapsible as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the trigger is a real
<%-- Owned body region: replace this with your panel markup (server-rendered). --%>

Collapsible content.

items.docs: Register CollapsibleComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount/`@template.lievit.collapsible(...)`; pass the panel body as the `content` slot (an @`...` block). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/collapsible")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: color-picker
items.description: A composite color-selection form control (v-next, PARTIAL+ENH). A trigger button (colored preview swatch + current hex label) opens a non-modal popover containing hex/RGB/HSL channel spinbuttons, an optional alpha slider, a preset swatch grid, and an optional eyedropper button. The committed color is a single hex string in a hidden native input, submitted with the form. A11y: WAI-ARIA APG Spinbutton (channel inputs) + APG Toolbar (swatch grid roving-tabindex) + APG Button (trigger), assembled as a BUILT composite widget (no single APG pattern covers a color picker). The swatch grid composes the shared collection-nav roving-tabindex enhancer. Strictly CSP-clean: no inline script, no on* handlers. The typed-TS enhancer owns channel sync (hex/RGB/HSL derivation), popover open/close + focus management, swatch application, spinbutton Home/End/PageUp/PageDown, format toggle, and eyedropper API integration.

files.content: @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui color-picker partial -- a composite color-selection form control: a trigger button (colored preview swatch + current value label) opens a popover containing hex/RGB/HSL channel spinbuttons, an optional alpha slider, a preset swatch grid, and an optional eyedropper button. The committed color is a single hex string in a hidden native input, submitted with the form. TIER: PARTIAL (shell) + ENH (color-picker.enhancer.ts). The shell is pure server-rendered JTE; the enhancer owns exactly two irreducible client responsibilities: (a) keeping the channel spinbuttons, hex input, alpha slider, and native input in sync during editing (pure-client derived-state: hex <-> RGB <-> HSL, sub-keystroke latency required); (b) swatch-grid roving-tabindex keyboard navigation (APG Toolbar). Everything else -- form submission, the value in the hidden input, popover open/close lifecycle -- is handled by the platform (popover API) or the enhancer closing the popover on confirm. Server-first works because the VALUE is a single String (the committed hex color) that the server holds, validates, and reflects into the template on submit. Only the in-picker ephemeral edit state earns a typed-TS enhancer (it cannot be expressed as a server round-trip per keystroke). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): A11y sub-patterns (assembled per the BUILT case; no single APG pattern covers a composite picker): 1. APG Button (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/) -- the trigger

params.name: name
params.description: Form field name submitted with the committed hex value.

params.name: value
params.description: Current committed hex color string (#rrggbb).

params.name: alpha
params.description: When true, renders the alpha channel slider.

params.name: alphaValue
params.description: Alpha percentage 0-100, used when alpha=true.

params.name: format
params.description: Initial display format: hex | rgb | hsl.

params.name: swatches
params.description: Preset hex values rendered as clickable swatch chips.

params.name: swatchColumns
params.description: Swatch grid column count.

params.name: eyedropper
params.description: Renders the eyedropper button; degrades gracefully when EyeDropper API is absent.

params.name: disabled
params.description: Dims and blocks all interaction.

params.name: size
params.description: sm | md | lg -- trigger height, toolbar-aligned.

params.name: ariaLabel
params.description: Accessible name for the composite group.

params.name: ariaLabelledby
params.description: Overrides ariaLabel when a visible label element exists.

params.name: id
params.description: Base id; derived from name if null.

params.name: cssClass
params.description: Extra utility classes on the root wrapper.

params.name: attrs
params.description: TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only.

params.name: dataAttrs
params.description: SAFE dynamic data-* values (each via Escape.htmlAttribute).

enhancer.notes: The swatch role='toolbar' is wired with data-lievit-collection + data-lievit-collection-roving-tabindex='true' + data-manual-activation='true'. The shared collection-nav enhancer (installCollectionNav) must also be installed to handle swatch ArrowLeft/ArrowRight/Home/End navigation. The color-picker enhancer handles swatch click semantics (apply color without confirming).

tokensNeeded: --lv-color-swatch-border: oklch(0.80 0.00 0) light / oklch(0.40 0.00 0) dark -- swatch chip border distinct from --lv-color-border
--lv-cp-swatch-size: var(--lv-space-5) -- swatch chip square size alias (20x20px); adopter-overridable
--lv-cp-preview-size: calc(var(--lv-space-9) - 4px) -- trigger preview swatch square size alias
items.docs: Copies color-picker.jte to lievit/color-picker.jte and the typed-TS enhancer to lievit/color-picker.enhancer.ts. Call enhanceAllColorPickers() on load and after any DOM swap. The shared collection-nav enhancer (installCollectionNav) must also be installed for swatch keyboard navigation. Params: name (required), value (#rrggbb), format (hex|rgb|hsl), swatches (List of hex strings), alpha (boolean), alphaValue (0-100), eyedropper (boolean), disabled, size (sm|md|lg), ariaLabel, ariaLabelledby, id, cssClass, attrs, dataAttrs.

items.name: combobox
items.description: A searchable select with a progressive-enhancement contract (ADR-0012, v-next): JS-OFF it IS a real <input role="combobox"> + <ul role="listbox"> + <input type="hidden"> — fully accessible and form-functional with zero JavaScript. JS-ON the colocated combobox.enhancer.ts activates the WAI-ARIA APG Combobox/listbox pattern (editable text input, client-side option filtering, popover seam for open/close, write-back to the hidden form field) while delegating listbox keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down/Home/End/Enter via aria-activedescendant, typeahead) to the shared collection-nav.enhancer.ts. Free-type mode commits any typed text on blur/Enter; select-only mode reverts to the last committed label. Optionally clearable. Sizes sm/md/lg (toolbar-aligned). Groups render as WAI-ARIA role="group" with aria-labelledby. Loading state: aria-busy + spinner before options. No-results state: aria-disabled option. Listbox always in DOM (aria-controls must resolve per APG); visibility via native popover + CSS Anchor Positioning.
items.docs: Copies combobox.jte to your JTE root (lievit/combobox.jte) and combobox.enhancer.ts to your alias root. Import the enhancer from your main entry module and call enhanceAllComboboxes() on load and after DOM swaps. JS-OFF the server-rendered <input role="combobox"> + <ul role="listbox"> + <input type="hidden"> are fully accessible and the hidden field POSTs the committed value; JS-ON the enhancer activates filtering, open/close, write-back, and blur commits, delegating listbox arrow-key nav to the shared collection-nav.enhancer.ts. Requires collection-nav.enhancer.ts and popover-anchor.enhancer.ts from the lievit runtime. Pass options (List), optionGroups (Map of group label to List), or a hand-authored content slot. Set mode="free-type" to allow committing values not in the list. Use clearable=true for an x button. Sizes sm/md/lg. The NET-NEW token --lv-combobox-listbox-max-h (260px default) controls the listbox scroll height; override in :root for dense UIs.

items.name: command
items.description: A command palette: a modal overlay with a search input and a grouped listbox of actions, filtered client-side by the colocated command.enhancer.ts. CONTROLLED/UNCONTROLLED: uncontrolled by default (the enhancer fires openPalette/close wire actions on the caller's component); controlled via the plain open @param. WAI-ARIA APG Combobox + Listbox (aria-activedescendant model). Composed with focus-trap.enhancer.ts (modal trap) and collection-nav.enhancer.ts (ArrowUp/Down/Home/End/PageUp/PageDown). No Java component (the caller's own Wire component owns the open flag + command registry). Items and groups arrive via @param List<String> columns.

files.content:
@import java.util.List @import java.util.HashSet <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui command partial -- a CONTROLLED/UNCONTROLLED modal command palette overlay. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (command.enhancer.ts + focus-trap.enhancer.ts + collection-nav.enhancer.ts). No Java component, no @Wire field, no _component/_instance params. The open-state belongs to the CALLER's own Wire component. CONTROLLED/UNCONTROLLED doctrine: UNCONTROLLED (default, open=false): the overlay is entirely absent from the DOM. The caller places its own trigger (e.g. a button with l:click="openPalette"). The enhancer also wires a global Cmd+K / Ctrl+K shortcut on the panel (data-shortcut) while the panel is in the DOM, to close it on second press. Opening from keyboard when closed is the caller's responsibility (the trigger button handles it). CONTROLLED (open=true): the full overlay renders -- scrim + centered panel. The panel carries data-lievit-focus-trap (traps Tab cycling, fires closeAction on Escape via focus-trap.enhancer.ts), data-lievit-collection on the listbox (ArrowUp/Down/Home/End navigation via collection-nav.enhancer.ts), and data-lievit-command (client-side filtering + Enter/Backspace/ shortcut dispatch via command.enhancer.ts). STRUCTURE (WAI-ARIA APG Combobox + Listbox, aria-activedescendant model): - Overlay: role="presentation" scrim + panel wrapper, position:fixed, z-index:--lv-z-overlay. - Scrim: aria-hidden="true", l:click="${closeAction}" (click-outside closes). - Panel: role="presentation", position:relative, z-index:--lv-z-modal, focus-trap host. - Breadcrumb nav: shown when page is non-empty (nested command page). Back button fires backToRootAction. A11y: nav[aria-label="Command palette navigation"]. - Search row: input[role=combobox][aria-controls][aria-haspopup=listbox][aria-autocomplete=list] [aria-expanded=true][aria-activedescendant] driven by collection-nav.enhancer.ts via data-lievit-collection-activedescendant-target on the listbox. - Listbox: ul[role=listbox][id] with data-lievit-collection + data-lievit-collection-orientation vertical. Each item: li[role=option][id="opt-{id}"][data-lievit-item]. - Groups: outer li[role=presentation][data-slot=group] + span[id=grp-N-label] + ul[role=group] [aria-labelledby=grp-N-label] for items in that group. - Empty state: li[role=presentation][data-slot=empty] + nested div[role=status][aria-live=polite]. - Items NOT in any group are rendered after the grouped blocks. Enhancer wiring (data-attribute contract -- do NOT edit the shared enhancers): data-lievit-focus-trap on the panel -- activates trap (Tab cycling, scroll-lock). data-lievit-escape-action on the panel -- fires closeAction on Escape. data-lievit-command on the panel -- activates the command enhancer. data-shortcut on the panel -- global shortcut (e.g. "mod+k") to close. data-page on the panel -- current nested page name (empty = root). data-lievit-collection on the listbox -- activates collection-nav.enhancer.ts. data-lievit-collection-orientation "vertical" -- ArrowUp/Down navigation. data-lievit-collection-activedescendant-target "#${inputId}" -- names the input to update. data-lievit-item on each li[role=option] -- collection item. MISSING tokens (flagged for token audit): --lv-space-96 (24rem), --lv-space-128 (32rem), --lv-space-192 (48rem): used as listbox max-height and panel widths. Fallbacks inline: 24rem / 32rem / 48rem. --lv-motion-fast: use --lv-duration-fast instead. Params (all @param, no Wire fields): boolean open = false List commands label per command (the filter target). List commandIds stable key per command; defaults to index. List commandGroups group name per command; blank = no group. List commandIcons Lucide icon name per command; blank = no icon. List commandShortcuts keyboard hint per command (display only); blank = none. List commandIntents "default" | "destructive"; blank = "default". List commandHrefs navigation href per command; blank = action command. List commandPageIds nested page id per command; blank = action command. List commandDisabled whether the command is disabled; null or false = enabled. List groups ordered list of group names to render (determines order). List recentCommandIds command ids to mark as recently used (data-recent). String page current nested page name; empty = root palette. String placeholder search input placeholder text. String emptyText text shown when no commands match the query. String size "sm" (24rem) | "md" (32rem, default) | "lg" (48rem). String shortcut global keyboard shortcut expressed as "mod+k". String closeAction wire action name to close the palette. String executeAction wire action name fired on a plain command item. String openPageAction wire action name fired when a page-command is activated. String backToRootAction wire action name fired by the breadcrumb Back button. String openAction wire action name fired by the global shortcut to open. String id root id; input and listbox ids derive from it. String cssClass extra classes on the overlay root. Usage: @@template.lievit.command( open = myCmd.open(), commands = myCmd.commandLabels(), commandIds = myCmd.commandIds(), commandGroups = myCmd.commandGroups(), commandIcons = myCmd.commandIcons(), commandShortcuts = myCmd.commandShortcuts(), commandIntents = myCmd.commandIntents(), commandHrefs = myCmd.commandHrefs(), commandDisabled = myCmd.commandDisabled(), groups = myCmd.groups(), recentCommandIds = myCmd.recentCommandIds(), page = myCmd.page(), closeAction = "closeCommand", executeAction = "executeCommand", openPageAction = "openPage", backToRootAction = "backToRoot", openAction = "openCommand" ) --%> @param boolean open = false @param List commands = java.util.List.of() @param List commandIds = java.util.List.of() @param List commandGroups = java.util.List.of() @param List commandIcons = java.util.List.of() @param List commandShortcuts = java.util.List.of() @param List commandIntents = java.util.List.of() @param List commandHrefs = java.util.List.of() @param List commandPageIds = java.util.List.of() @param List commandDisabled = java.util.List.of() @param List groups = java.util.List.of() @param List recentCommandIds = java.util.List.of() @param String page = "" @param String placeholder = "Search commands…" @param String emptyText = "No commands found." @param String size = "md" @param String shortcut = "mod+k" @param String closeAction = "close" @param String executeAction = "executeCommand" @param String openPageAction = "openPage" @param String backToRootAction = "backToRoot" @param String openAction = "openPalette" @param String id = "lv-cmd" @param String cssClass = "" !{var listboxId = id + "-listbox";} !{var inputId = id + "-input";} !{var recentSet = new HashSet(recentCommandIds);} !{var sizeWidth = "lg".equals(size) ? "48rem" : "md".equals(size) ? "32rem" : "24rem";} !{var hasGroups = !groups.isEmpty();} @if(open)

<%-- Panel: the modal box centered over the scrim. Focus-trap host. --%>

@endif <%-- Search row: combobox input + decorative search icon. --%>

items.docs: Render @template.lievit.command(open = myComponent.open(), commands = myComponent.commandLabels(), ...) in your Wire template. The palette appears when open=true. The colocated command.enhancer.ts mounts on the panel, delegates focus-trapping to focus-trap.enhancer.ts and listbox roving to collection-nav.enhancer.ts, drives client-side filtering, and fires wire actions (openPalette, close, executeCommand, openPage, backToRoot) on user interaction. Pass the action name params to match your Wire component's action names. Install all three enhancers via installFocusTrap + installCollectionNav + installCommandEnhancer on your runtime.

items.name: command
items.description: A searchable command palette whose grouped items are filtered SERVER-SIDE: the query binds l:model.debounce, the server re-filters + re-groups the catalog and re-renders the list (no client filtering). The chosen value lives server-side and is set via the $set magic action. Often composed inside a dialog wire (a command-dialog). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-command> Lit island. WAI-ARIA combobox + listbox with grouped options.

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import dev.lievit.wire.CommandComponent @import dev.lievit.wire.CommandComponent.Group @import dev.lievit.wire.CommandComponent.Item @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui command WIRE template -- a searchable command palette whose items are filtered SERVER-SIDE (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/command/CommandComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The query is a @Wire string bound with l:model.debounce: typing re-queries the server, which re-filters + re-groups the catalog (CommandComponent.render) and re-renders this list, the client morphing it. NO client-side filtering, the catalog never ships to the browser. The chosen value lives server-side (a @Wire string) and is set with the $set magic action on click. No . Often opened inside a dialog (a command-dialog): render this component inside a dialog wire component's OWNED body region (the dialog owns the open state, this owns the query + selection). If no dialog wire is present, render it inline. The body is owned server-rendered markup either way. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA combobox + listbox (the model the dropped island cited): the search with aria-controls -> the list; the list is
    ; groups are role="group" with an aria-label; items are
  • . Server-rendered grouped options + debounced re-query beats a client-filtered list: the catalog and selection are both server-held, so nothing can fail to project. A11y: input[role=combobox][aria-controls], ul[role=listbox], div[role=group][aria-label], li[role=option][aria-selected]. Params: query String the live search query (bound by l:model.debounce) selected String the chosen command value (server-owned; set via $set) placeholder String placeholder for the search box emptyText String text shown when nothing matches label String accessible label for the palette _instance CommandComponent the live instance (reads the server-filtered groups) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, seed allItems in the component from real commands): register CommandComponent as a PROTOTYPE @Bean; mount it (optionally inside a dialog wire); read the `selected` field in a @LievitRender hook to act on the chosen command. --%> @param String query @param String selected @param String placeholder @param String emptyText @param String label @param CommandComponent _instance @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var listId = "lv-command-list";} !{List groups = _instance.groups();}
    @template.icon(name = "search", size = "1rem")

items.docs: Register CommandComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `allItems` from your available commands (the seed list is placeholder data). The query filters server-side via l:model.debounce; selecting an item sets the `selected` field via $set('selected', '<value>'). Read `selected` in a @LievitRender hook (or a dedicated action) to act on the choice. For a command-dialog, render the component inside a dialog wire's owned body region. Adjust the template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: command-palette
items.description: A searchable command list (shadcn Command), STATIC with the WHOLE small/preloaded catalog rendered server-side as real <a href> / <button formaction> items, plus a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (command.enhancer.ts) for in-browser filtering + keyboard nav. WAI-ARIA combobox + listbox: input[role=combobox][aria-controls] -> ul[role=listbox] of li[role=option], group-captioned headings, each option a real focusable control. JS-off it is a plain navigable list; the enhancer filters by query (hide non-matches, no re-render, no server round-trip) and wires ArrowUp/Down + Enter + Escape. Items come in via index-aligned List<String> columns (the tabs-static convention), so the partial needs no custom record. The static sibling of the server-filtered wire command (registry/wire/command/), serving the kit dev.lievit.kit.Command render path; use the wire command for a large/lazy/server-owned catalog. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, no inline script.

files.content:
@import java.util.List @import java.util.HashSet <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui command partial -- a CONTROLLED/UNCONTROLLED modal command palette overlay. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (command.enhancer.ts + focus-trap.enhancer.ts + collection-nav.enhancer.ts). No Java component, no @Wire field, no _component/_instance params. The open-state belongs to the CALLER's own Wire component. CONTROLLED/UNCONTROLLED doctrine: UNCONTROLLED (default, open=false): the overlay is entirely absent from the DOM. The caller places its own trigger (e.g. a button with l:click="openPalette"). The enhancer also wires a global Cmd+K / Ctrl+K shortcut on the panel (data-shortcut) while the panel is in the DOM, to close it on second press. Opening from keyboard when closed is the caller's responsibility (the trigger button handles it). CONTROLLED (open=true): the full overlay renders -- scrim + centered panel. The panel carries data-lievit-focus-trap (traps Tab cycling, fires closeAction on Escape via focus-trap.enhancer.ts), data-lievit-collection on the listbox (ArrowUp/Down/Home/End navigation via collection-nav.enhancer.ts), and data-lievit-command (client-side filtering + Enter/Backspace/ shortcut dispatch via command.enhancer.ts). STRUCTURE (WAI-ARIA APG Combobox + Listbox, aria-activedescendant model): - Overlay: role="presentation" scrim + panel wrapper, position:fixed, z-index:--lv-z-overlay. - Scrim: aria-hidden="true", l:click="${closeAction}" (click-outside closes). - Panel: role="presentation", position:relative, z-index:--lv-z-modal, focus-trap host. - Breadcrumb nav: shown when page is non-empty (nested command page). Back button fires backToRootAction. A11y: nav[aria-label="Command palette navigation"]. - Search row: input[role=combobox][aria-controls][aria-haspopup=listbox][aria-autocomplete=list] [aria-expanded=true][aria-activedescendant] driven by collection-nav.enhancer.ts via data-lievit-collection-activedescendant-target on the listbox. - Listbox: ul[role=listbox][id] with data-lievit-collection + data-lievit-collection-orientation vertical. Each item: li[role=option][id="opt-{id}"][data-lievit-item]. - Groups: outer li[role=presentation][data-slot=group] + span[id=grp-N-label] + ul[role=group] [aria-labelledby=grp-N-label] for items in that group. - Empty state: li[role=presentation][data-slot=empty] + nested div[role=status][aria-live=polite]. - Items NOT in any group are rendered after the grouped blocks. Enhancer wiring (data-attribute contract -- do NOT edit the shared enhancers): data-lievit-focus-trap on the panel -- activates trap (Tab cycling, scroll-lock). data-lievit-escape-action on the panel -- fires closeAction on Escape. data-lievit-command on the panel -- activates the command enhancer. data-shortcut on the panel -- global shortcut (e.g. "mod+k") to close. data-page on the panel -- current nested page name (empty = root). data-lievit-collection on the listbox -- activates collection-nav.enhancer.ts. data-lievit-collection-orientation "vertical" -- ArrowUp/Down navigation. data-lievit-collection-activedescendant-target "#${inputId}" -- names the input to update. data-lievit-item on each li[role=option] -- collection item. MISSING tokens (flagged for token audit): --lv-space-96 (24rem), --lv-space-128 (32rem), --lv-space-192 (48rem): used as listbox max-height and panel widths. Fallbacks inline: 24rem / 32rem / 48rem. --lv-motion-fast: use --lv-duration-fast instead. Params (all @param, no Wire fields): boolean open = false List commands label per command (the filter target). List commandIds stable key per command; defaults to index. List commandGroups group name per command; blank = no group. List commandIcons Lucide icon name per command; blank = no icon. List commandShortcuts keyboard hint per command (display only); blank = none. List commandIntents "default" | "destructive"; blank = "default". List commandHrefs navigation href per command; blank = action command. List commandPageIds nested page id per command; blank = action command. List commandDisabled whether the command is disabled; null or false = enabled. List groups ordered list of group names to render (determines order). List recentCommandIds command ids to mark as recently used (data-recent). String page current nested page name; empty = root palette. String placeholder search input placeholder text. String emptyText text shown when no commands match the query. String size "sm" (24rem) | "md" (32rem, default) | "lg" (48rem). String shortcut global keyboard shortcut expressed as "mod+k". String closeAction wire action name to close the palette. String executeAction wire action name fired on a plain command item. String openPageAction wire action name fired when a page-command is activated. String backToRootAction wire action name fired by the breadcrumb Back button. String openAction wire action name fired by the global shortcut to open. String id root id; input and listbox ids derive from it. String cssClass extra classes on the overlay root. items.docs: Copies the static command palette to your JTE root at lievit/command.jte (+ the lievit/command.enhancer.ts enhancer), reached as @template.lievit.command(...). Pass a unique `id` and the index-aligned List<String> columns: `labels` (required, also the filter target) plus the optional `values`/`hrefs`/`formActions`/`formIds`/`icons`/`shortcuts`/`groups` (a blank cell means absent; an item with a non-blank href renders a real <a>, else a real <button>). Tune `placeholder`, `emptyText`, `label`. JS-off the list is a plain navigable menu; import the enhancer and call enhanceAllCommands() after the runtime starts to add live filtering + ArrowUp/Down/Enter/Escape keyboard nav (it hides non-matching items and their now-empty group headings, toggles the empty state, drives aria-activedescendant). The pure commandFilter(labels, query) is exported for unit tests. For a large/lazy/server-owned catalog that filters per keystroke server-side, use the wire command component instead.

items.name: context-menu
items.description: A right-click context menu (WAI-ARIA APG Menu pattern, context-menu variant). UNCONTROLLED PARTIAL: the open state is managed client-side by context-menu-trigger.enhancer.ts (intercepts the contextmenu DOM event, positions the panel via data-menu-x/data-menu-y CSS attr(), manages outside-click dismiss and Escape). Keyboard roving (arrow keys, Home/End, typeahead, Enter/Space commit) is delegated to the shared collection-nav.enhancer.ts via data-lievit-collection on the panel. CONTROLLED opt-in: bind open to the caller's own @Wire field. Items composed from context-menu/item, context-menu/separator, context-menu/group sub-partials. No Java component, no server round-trip just to open.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui context-menu partial -- a right-click floating role="menu" panel that opens at the pointer position. UNCONTROLLED by default: the context-menu-trigger.enhancer.ts intercepts the contextmenu DOM event, records the pointer coordinates, writes data-menu-x + data-menu-y onto the panel, and manages the open/closed lifecycle entirely client-side. CONTROLLED opt-in: pass open=true (bound to the CALLER's own @Wire field) to keep the panel in the DOM across wire round-trips; the enhancer still owns positioning and keyboard handling. TIER: PARTIAL (registry:jte). The open-state is NOT server state here -- no @Wire field, no Java component, no _component/_instance params. Same tier decision as dropdown-menu: a menu whose actions do server work (via l:click / href on each item) is fundamentally a PARTIAL + one irreducible gesture enhancer for the contextmenu event capture. The BUSINESS component that needs controlled open passes open="${myComp.contextMenuOpen()}" from its own @Wire field. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Menu pattern (context-menu variant, https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menu/). Panel = role="menu" aria-orientation="vertical". Items = real

<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui context-menu/separator partial -- a non-selectable visual divider between groups of
  context-menu items. Pure CSS, no JS.

  TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Menu): role="separator" inside the role="menu" panel. aria-hidden="true" on
  the visual element so AT does not announce it redundantly (the role="separator" on the <hr> is
  sufficient; the aria-hidden is on the wrapper that carries the visual styling).

  Params: (none)

  Usage:
    @template.lievit.context-menu.separator()
--%>
<hr
  role="separator"
  data-slot="context-menu-separator"
  style="height:1px; border:none; background:var(--lv-color-border); margin:var(--lv-space-1) 0;"
/>

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui context-menu/group partial -- a labelled group of related context-menu items. Wraps its items in a role="group" element with aria-labelledby pointing to a header span. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. STRUCTURE (WAI-ARIA APG Menu, group pattern): role="group" aria-labelledby="". The header is role="presentation" (visual label only, not a menu item) so AT reads the label as the group name, not as a menu item. The header is not focusable and collection-nav skips it (no data-lievit-item attribute). A11y (WAI-ARIA APG): role="group" aria-labelledby; group header role="presentation" with a unique id; items inside are standard role="menuitem" (composed from context-menu/item partial). Params: String groupId = "" unique id suffix for the group header element. When empty a random suffix is not feasible in a static template; callers MUST supply a unique id when more than one group exists on a page. String label = "" visible group header text (also the a11y group name). gg.jte.Content content the group's items composed from context-menu/item partial. Usage: @template.lievit.context-menu.group( groupId = "edit-actions", label = "Edit", content = @` @template.lievit.context-menu.item(label = "Cut", icon = "scissors", attrs = "l:click=\"cut\"") @template.lievit.context-menu.item(label = "Copy", icon = "copy", attrs = "l:click=\"copy\"") @template.lievit.context-menu.item(label = "Paste", icon = "clipboard", attrs = "l:click=\"paste\"") ` ) --%> @param String groupId = "" @param String label = "" @param gg.jte.Content content !{var headerId = "ctx-grp-" + (!groupId.isEmpty() ? groupId : "default");}
${label} ${content}

items.docs: Copies the context-menu family to your JTE root (lievit/context-menu.jte + lievit/context-menu/{item,separator,group}.jte) and the component CSS (lievit/context-menu.css). Call @template.lievit.context-menu(id, trigger, content) -- compose items from @template.lievit.context-menu.item (role menuitem/checkbox/radio; variant default/destructive; wireClick+wireArgs for per-row safe actions), @template.lievit.context-menu.separator, and @template.lievit.context-menu.group (role group, aria-labelledby). Install the enhancers once: installContextMenuTrigger() from context-menu-trigger.enhancer.ts (handles right-click interception, coordinate positioning, outside-click/Escape dismiss, focus restore); installCollectionNav() from collection-nav.enhancer.ts (arrow key roving, Home/End, typeahead, Enter/Space commit). Import context-menu.css. For controlled open (panel survives a wire round-trip), bind open to your own @Wire boolean field.

items.name: context-menu
items.description: A right-click context menu opened at the pointer. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012, Wave 2): open-state, pointer position, items and selection live in typed Java; the server re-renders, the client morphs. A tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer captures the contextmenu gesture (no Lit, no @floating-ui/dom). Replaces the <lv-context-menu> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG menu pattern (role=menu of role=menuitem).

files.content: @import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import dev.lievit.wire.ContextMenuComponent @import dev.lievit.wire.ContextMenuComponent.Entry @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui context-menu WIRE template -- a right-click menu whose open-state, pointer position and selection live server-side in ContextMenuComponent (ADR-0012, Wave 2). Pairs with registry/wire/context-menu/ContextMenuComponent.java + the context-menu.ts enhancer. TIER: WIRE. The open-state + the pointer coordinates + the last selection are server state (@Wire fields); the items are server-owned data read off _instance (a record list cannot round-trip the generic-Map snapshot codec, the listing-list pattern). Right-click opens the menu via a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (context-menu.ts) that prevents the browser menu, writes x/y into the wire fields and fires the `openAt` action; the server re-renders open at those coordinates and the client morphs it. No : the right-clickable region is OWNED markup inside this template (the server-first equivalent of children), never a projected slot (the light-DOM slot bug the pivot exists to kill). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Menu pattern, Radix ContextMenu as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the panel is role="menu"; - items are role="menuitem" / "menuitemcheckbox" / "menuitemradio", a real @endif @endfor @endif

items.docs: Register ContextMenuComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call) and supply the real `items` list (override the default in your copy). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.context-menu(...)`; put your right-clickable content in the owned `data-context-menu-trigger` region (NOT a slot). Call `enhanceContextMenus(runtime)` once from main.ts after `startLievit()` so a right-click opens the menu at the pointer. A selection arms `selectedKey` via $set; read it (or add server authz on a `closeAfterSelect`) in your copy. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/context-menu")` name + the dev.lievit.wire package are yours to change once copied.

items.name: data-list
items.description: A divided list surface: a real <ul role="list"> whose <li> rows (data-list.row) are ruled by a hairline divide and split into a leading media slot + a left content column + a right actions cluster. The container ships the optional bordered card chrome + the divide rule; data-list.row ships the row (role=listitem, leading/content/actions slots, optional whole-tile href link, hoverHighlight tint, SAFE dataAttrs channel). The read-only LIST display primitive for the kit's infolist / RepeatableEntry and panelled record lists, distinct from item (a single composable row) and data-table (a sortable/selectable grid). Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui data-list partial -- a divided list surface: a real
    whose
  • rows (data-list.row) are separated by a hairline rule (the shadcn "divide-y" list pattern). Each row is a left content column + an optional right actions cluster. This is the read-only LIST display primitive the kit's infolist / RepeatableEntry and gest's profile lists need: distinct from `item` (a single composable card-like row) and from `data-table` (a sortable/selectable grid). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout + styling; any actionable row puts its own /

items.docs: Copies the data-list partial (+ its data-list/row child) to your JTE root at lievit/data-list.jte, reached as @template.lievit.data-list(content=@`...`). Stack data-list.row rows inside it (each a real <li role="listitem" with leading / content / actions slots). Pass ariaLabel to label the list for assistive tech. Pass bordered=false for a flush, inline list with no card chrome. On the row: pass href for a whole-tile <a> link (block-display inside the <li>); hoverHighlight=false for non-interactive rows; dataAttrs for SAFE escaped data-* (per-row DB values). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: data-table
items.description: A server-sorted, server-paginated data table: a real <table> whose sort and page are SERVER facts (sortable headers are real <a href> re-sort links carrying aria-sort, paging via the pagination partial). Rows come in via a Content slot (caller owns the cells). Empty state. Plus the shadcn data-table feature set as server-first slots: a leading checkbox column (select-all + per-row, setting data-state=selected), a toolbar region (search/filter + column-visibility dropdown), and a selected-count footer with a bulk-action slot. Zero JS, zero client sort, CSP-clean. The presentational primitive the kit's admin list composes.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table (static JTE partial - server-sorted/paginated table) The SERVER-FIRST data table: a real whose sort and page are SERVER facts. Sortable column headers are real re-sort links (the server produces the next sort URL per column); clicking one re-sorts on the server and re-renders. Pagination is the windowed pagination partial (real page links). The empty state renders a single spanning row when there are no rows. ZERO client JavaScript, ZERO client sort. TIER (why this is a partial, not a Lit island / wire component): the genuinely STATEFUL server-sorted/paginated table is OWNED by lievit-kit's admin list -- the ListingListComponent wire holds the page/sort/search/filter as @Wire fields, AdminListView.of(...) reads one bounded page, and l:click="toggleSort/prev/next" actions re-render it. This partial is NOT that engine; it is the presentational PRIMITIVE the kit (and any plain Spring controller) composes to RENDER one page. The kit's listing-list.jte hand-rolls this exact
///pagination markup; this partial generalises it so the markup, the aria-sort contract, and the sort-link affordance live in one owned place instead of being copy-pasted per resource. The old Lit island (client sort + client pagination, fully in the browser) is removed by ADR-0012: the table is server-first, the kit holds the state, this partial draws it. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule). shadcn's Data Table is a TanStack-Table client grid (sort / paginate / select all in JS); the WAI-ARIA APG "sortable table" pattern is a plain
whose sorted
carries aria-sort and whose header is an in-cell control that re-sorts. For a server-first kit the APG sortable-table pattern wins on (1) a11y (real table semantics, the sort control is a real link that works with JS off) and (5) robustness (the sort and page survive a reload because they are URL state, not in-memory client state). shadcn's grid is rejected: it puts the sort/page truth in the browser, the exact silent-client-state class the pivot removes. So: a real // (composing the table primitive's look),
headers, aria-sort on the active column, the sort affordance as a real (the kit emits the next-sort URL), and the pagination partial for paging. ROWS via a Content slot (data down, the caller owns the cells). The caller renders one @template.lievit.data-table.row(...) per data item inside `rows`, each holding @template.lievit.data-table.cell blocks (or composes the existing table.row / table.cell primitives). This mirrors the kit's typed Cell switch: the partial owns the chrome + the sort/page contract, the caller owns what a cell shows (a badge, a link, an icon, escaped text). No row data is hardcoded here; it arrives via the slot from the controller's typed model. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG, sortable table): real /// rows (caller owns them; one row per data item). int rowCount = -1 number of data rows; <= 0 renders the empty state. -1 = unknown (trust the slot; never render the empty row). String emptyText = "No data" text of the empty-state row. String caption = null optional
; the active sort column's carries aria-sort="ascending|descending" (others "none"); the sort control is a real so it is keyboard reachable and works with JS off; a visible sort indicator glyph (chevron-up/down for asc/desc, chevrons-up-down for unsorted) is aria-hidden (the aria-sort state is the accessible signal); the optional
is the table's accessible name; the empty state is one row spanning all columns. The pagination partial owns its own nav landmark + aria-current. Params: List headers column header labels, in order (REQUIRED). List sortUrls = null per-column next-sort URL; "" / null = that column is not sortable (no link). Same length / order as `headers`. List sortStates = null per-column active sort: "asc" | "desc" | "" (none). Same length / order as `headers`. null = nothing sorted. Content rows the (accessible name). String ariaLabel = null aria-label when there is no visible caption. int page = 0 current page (1-based); a page < 1 hides the pager. int totalPages = 0 total pages; <= 1 hides the pager. String pageBaseUrl = null URL prefix for page links (passed directly to @template.lievit.pagination as baseUrl). null hides the pager. String klass = "" extra classes on the . Usage: @@template.lievit.data-table( headers = view.headers(), sortUrls = sortUrls, // controller builds the next-sort href per column sortStates= sortStates, // "asc"/"desc"/"" per column from the active Sort caption = "Listings", rowCount = view.rows().size(), page = view.pagination().page(), totalPages= view.pagination().totalPages(), pageBaseUrl = "/admin/listings", rows = @@` @@for(var row : view.rows()) @@template.lievit.data-table.row(content = @@` @@template.lievit.data-table.cell(content = @@`${row.id()}`) `) @@endfor `) --%> @param java.util.List headers @param java.util.List sortUrls = null @param java.util.List sortStates = null @param gg.jte.Content rows @param int rowCount = -1 @param String emptyText = "No data" @param String caption = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param int page = 0 @param int totalPages = 0 @param String pageBaseUrl = null @param String klass = "" !{var colCount = headers.size();} !{var showPager = pageBaseUrl != null && page >= 1 && totalPages > 1;}
@if(caption != null && !caption.isBlank()) @endif @for(int i = 0; i < colCount; i++) !{var label = headers.get(i);} !{var url = (sortUrls != null && i < sortUrls.size()) ? sortUrls.get(i) : null;} !{var sortable = url != null && !url.isBlank();} !{var state = (sortStates != null && i < sortStates.size()) ? sortStates.get(i) : "";} !{var sorted = state != null && (state.equals("asc") || state.equals("desc"));} !{var ariaSort = !sortable ? null : (state.equals("asc") ? "ascending" : (state.equals("desc") ? "descending" : "none"));} @endfor @if(rowCount == 0) @else ${rows} @endif
${caption}
@if(sortable) ${label} @if(state.equals("asc")) @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-up", size = "0.85em") @elseif(state.equals("desc")) @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-down", size = "0.85em") @else @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevrons-up-down", size = "0.85em") @endif @else ${label} @endif
${emptyText}
@if(showPager)
@template.lievit.pagination(currentPage = page, totalPages = totalPages, baseUrl = pageBaseUrl)
@endif

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui :: data-table.row - one <tr> in the server-first data table.

  A bottom-bordered, hover-highlighted row; a selected row gets a steadier muted fill via the
  data-state attribute (shadcn parity). The caller renders one of these per data item inside the
  data-table `rows` slot, filling it with @template.lievit.data-table.cell blocks. No row data lives here;
  it arrives via the `content` slot from the controller's typed model.

  Params:
    state   - "selected" to mark the row selected, else "" (default).
    id      - optional row id, stamped as data-row-id for the caller's action wiring.
    klass   - extra classes for the <tr>.
    content - the row's @@template.lievit.data-table.cell blocks.
--%>
@param String state = ""
@param String id = null
@param String klass = ""
@param gg.jte.Content content
<tr
  data-slot="data-table-row"
  data-row-id="${id == null || id.isBlank() ? null : id}"
  data-state="${state.isEmpty() ? null : state}"
  class="lv-table-row border-b transition-colors hover:bg-[color-mix(in_srgb,var(--lv-color-surface)_60%,var(--lv-color-bg))] data-[state=selected]:bg-[color-mix(in_srgb,var(--lv-color-surface)_85%,var(--lv-color-bg))] ${klass}"
  style="border-color:var(--lv-color-border);"
>
  ${content}
</tr>

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table.cell - one in the server-first data table. A padded, bottom-bordered data cell. Pass scope="row" + as a for a row-header cell (the first column of a row identifying it); the default is a plain . The cell content is the caller's typed projection (escaped text, a badge, a real , an icon), mirroring the kit's typed Cell switch: this partial owns the chrome, the caller owns what the cell shows. Params: align - "left" (default) | "center" | "right". header - true to render a (row-header cell) instead of a . klass - extra classes for the cell. content - the cell's rendered content. --%> @param String align = "left" @param boolean header = false @param String klass = "" @param gg.jte.Content content !{var alignClass = align.equals("right") ? "text-right" : (align.equals("center") ? "text-center" : "text-left");} @if(header) ${content} @else ${content} @endif

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table.selection-cell - the leading checkbox column of the server-first data table. shadcn's Data Table prepends a checkbox column (id="select"): a select-all checkbox in the header (checked when the whole page is selected, indeterminate when some are), and a per-row checkbox that toggles row.getIsSelected() -- the same boolean the row's data-state="selected" tint already reads. This partial renders THAT column's one cell: a header cell () for the select-all box, or a body cell () for a per-row box. The checkbox itself is the lievit checkbox partial (native , JS-off submit, indeterminate via aria-checked="mixed"). SERVER-FIRST wiring: selection is a SERVER fact the kit holds (which row ids are selected this page), exactly like sort and page. The partial renders the STRUCTURE + a wire hook; the adopter / lievit-kit wires the toggle. Pass `model` for an l:model binding (the checkbox partial binds it to the native change event, the documented checkbox wire idiom). With JS off the checkbox still POSTs its `value` under `name`, so a plain form-based bulk action (a surrounding
+ submit) works unchanged. The checkbox-column spacing rule (shadcn table.tsx): a cell that HOLDS a [role=checkbox] drops its right padding (`[&:has([role=checkbox])]:pr-0`) so the box sits flush; this partial bakes pr-0 in. A11y: the select-all box gets aria-label "Select all" (no visible label fits a header checkbox); a row box gets aria-label "Select row". `indeterminate` renders the tri-state "mixed" glyph for the "some rows selected" header state. A real / keeps table semantics intact. Params: boolean header = false true => the select-all header cell (); else a row cell (). String name = null the checkbox name (POSTs `value` when checked); e.g. "selectAll" / "selected". String value = "on" the submitted value when checked (a row box usually passes the row id). boolean checked = false initial checked state (select-all: whole page selected; row: this row selected). boolean indeterminate = false header-only: "some but not all" rows selected (tri-state "mixed"). String ariaLabel = null accessible name; defaults to "Select all" (header) / "Select row" (row). String model = null l:model expression binding the checkbox to a wire field. String klass = "" extra classes on the cell. Usage (header): @@template.lievit.data-table.selection-cell(header = true, name = "selectAll", checked = view.allSelected(), indeterminate = view.someSelected(), model = "selectAll") Usage (row): @@template.lievit.data-table.selection-cell(name = "selected", value = row.id(), checked = view.isSelected(row.id()), model = "selected") --%> @param boolean header = false @param String name = null @param String value = "on" @param boolean checked = false @param boolean indeterminate = false @param String ariaLabel = null @param String model = null @param String klass = "" !{var label = (ariaLabel != null && !ariaLabel.isBlank()) ? ariaLabel : (header ? "Select all" : "Select row");} !{var modelAttr = (model != null && !model.isBlank()) ? "l:model=\"" + model + "\"" : "";} @if(header) style="color:var(--lv-color-fg);background-color:color-mix(in srgb, var(--lv-color-surface) 35%, var(--lv-color-bg));" > @template.lievit.checkbox(name = name, value = value, checked = checked, indeterminate = indeterminate, ariaLabel = label, attrs = modelAttr) @else @template.lievit.checkbox(name = name, value = value, checked = checked, ariaLabel = label, attrs = modelAttr) @endif

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table.toolbar - the controls row ABOVE the server-first data table. shadcn's Data Table puts a control row above the : a filter on the left and a column-visibility ("Columns") pushed to the right (ml-auto). This partial is that row as a slot/region: a `search` slot on the left, an `end` slot on the right (the column-visibility toggle and any bulk-action buttons), composed in one flex row that matches shadcn's `flex items-center py-4`. Both are SLOTS (the caller owns the wired controls) so the partial stays presentational. SERVER-FIRST: the search/filter and column-visibility are SERVER facts the kit holds (a filter query, a set of hidden columns), like sort and page. The partial renders the affordance + the wire hook seam; lievit-kit (or a plain controller) wires the actual round-trip. The search input is the lievit input partial type="search" with a `model` for the wire field (and a real `name` so it POSTs JS-off inside a surrounding
); the column-visibility dropdown is the sibling data-table.column-visibility partial. Params: Content search = null the left slot: the search / filter affordance (e.g. @template.lievit.input type="search"). null renders nothing on the left (the `end` slot still right-aligns). Content end = null the right slot: the column-visibility toggle + bulk-action buttons. Pushed to the right edge (ml-auto) exactly like shadcn's "Columns" button. String klass = "" extra classes on the toolbar row. Usage: @@template.lievit.data-table.toolbar( search = @@`@@template.lievit.input(name = "q", type = "search", size = "sm", placeholder = "Filter...", model = "query", cssClass = "max-w-[20rem]")`, end = @@`@@template.lievit.data-table.column-visibility(content = @@`...`)` ) --%> @param gg.jte.Content search = null @param gg.jte.Content end = null @param String klass = ""
@if(search != null)
${search}
@endif @if(end != null)
${end}
@endif

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table.column-visibility - the "Columns" show/hide toggle of the data table. shadcn's Data Table renders a with a "Columns" trigger and one DropdownMenuCheckboxItem per hideable column (checked = column visible, toggles column.toggleVisibility). This partial is that dropdown: it composes the sibling dropdown-menu partial with a "Columns" trigger + a trailing chevron-down (the lievit dropdown idiom; shadcn uses a plain outline "Columns" button), and the caller fills the `content` slot with one @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(type = "checkbox", ...) per column. SERVER-FIRST: which columns are visible is a SERVER fact the kit holds (a set of hidden column ids), like sort/page/selection. The partial renders the STRUCTURE; each checkbox item carries its checked state from the server and a wire hook (a form action / l:click the adopter wires) to toggle it. The menu itself is the native-popover dropdown (zero JS, CSP-clean); with JS off the toggles are real

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table.selection-footer - the "N of M row(s) selected" region below the data table. shadcn's Data Table shows a muted line under the table: "{selected} of {total} row(s) selected." (table.getFilteredSelectedRowModel().rows.length of table.getFilteredRowModel().rows.length). This partial is that region. The counts are SERVER facts the kit passes in (how many rows are selected, how many are in the current filtered set), like the page/total the pager reads. An optional `actions` slot holds bulk-action controls (Delete selected, Export selected) the adopter wires; it renders only when there IS a selection, so the bulk bar appears on demand. A11y: the count line is aria-live="polite" so a screen reader announces the running selected count as rows are toggled (the selection changes without a navigation). The text is plain, muted, shadcn-styled. Params: int selected = 0 number of selected rows (server fact). int total = 0 number of rows in the current filtered set (server fact). String noun = "row" the singular noun ("row" -> "row(s) selected"); for a typed list pass e.g. "listing". Used ONLY by the English fallback; ignored when `label` is supplied. String label = null i18n seam: the fully-rendered localized count sentence (e.g. "5 di 12 attività selezionate."). Non-blank -> replaces the English line; null/blank -> the legacy "{selected} of {total} {noun}(s) selected." fallback. Content actions = null optional bulk-action slot; rendered only when selected > 0. String klass = "" extra classes on the footer row. Usage: @@template.lievit.data-table.selection-footer( selected = view.selectedCount(), total = view.rows().size(), actions = @@`@@template.lievit.button(variant = "destructive", size = "sm", attrs = "l:click=\"deleteSelected\"", content = @@`Delete selected`)`) --%> @param int selected = 0 @param int total = 0 @param String noun = "row" @param String label = null @param gg.jte.Content actions = null @param String klass = "" <%-- i18n seam: the count sentence is NOT hardcoded English. A host passes the fully-rendered, localized `label` (e.g. "5 di 12 attività selezionate."); when null/blank the partial falls back to the legacy English "{selected} of {total} {noun}(s) selected." for back-compat. The counts stay params so a host that wants the default plural still gets the server facts. --%> !{var _line = (label != null && !label.isBlank()) ? label : (selected + " of " + total + " " + noun + "(s) selected.");}
${_line}
@if(actions != null && selected > 0)
${actions}
@endif

items.docs: Copies the data-table partial family to your JTE root: lievit/data-table.jte (+ data-table/{row,cell,selection-cell,toolbar,column-visibility,selection-footer}.jte), reached as @template.lievit.data-table(...). It is server-first: sort and page are URL state your controller produces, not client state. Pass `headers` (labels), `sortUrls` (per-column next-sort href; blank = not sortable), `sortStates` ("asc"/"desc"/"" per column), and the `rows` slot (one @template.lievit.data-table.row per item, holding @template.lievit.data-table.cell blocks). Pass `page`/`totalPages`/`pageBaseUrl` for the pager and `rowCount`=0 to render the empty state. SELECTION: add @template.lievit.data-table.selection-cell(header=true,...) as the leading header cell and one per row (a native checkbox; check the row sets data-state=selected), and render the running count with @template.lievit.data-table.selection-footer. FILTER + COLUMNS: put @template.lievit.data-table.toolbar above the table with a search input slot and @template.lievit.data-table.column-visibility (a Columns dropdown of per-column checkbox items) in its `end` slot. Selection / filter / column-visibility are server facts the kit holds; these partials render the structure + the wire hook (l:model / form action), so they work with JS off. The pager composes the sibling `pagination` partial, so copy `lievit/pagination.jte` in too. The lievit-kit admin list (AdminListView/ListRequest) is the stateful engine that drives this primitive via l:click sort/page/select actions; this is the markup it renders. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: date-picker
items.description: A token-styled native <input type="date"> with its <label>: the browser owns the accessible, locale-aware calendar popup, keyboard grid and validation. @param for name/id/value/min/max/required/label (+ disabled/readonly/invalid/describedBy/model). `mode="range"` renders a paired from/to control (two bound native inputs, role=group, native from<=to constraint) under name/nameTo. Zero JS, no @floating-ui, CSP-clean, form-submits the ISO date under `name`. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-date-picker> Lit island. A richer multi-month / custom-grid picker is the calendar wire or an escape-hatch, not this component.

items.docs: Copies the date-picker partial to your JTE root at lievit/date-picker.jte, reached as @template.lievit.date-picker(name=..., label=..., value="2026-06-19"). It renders a native <input type=date>: the browser supplies the calendar, keyboard navigation, locale formatting and min/max/required enforcement with zero JS. The value POSTs under `name` as ISO YYYY-MM-DD, or bind it to a wire field with `model`. Set `mode="range"` for a paired from/to control: two bound native inputs POSTing under `name`/`nameTo` (set `valueTo`/`modelTo`), grouped as role=group with the shared label, where the browser's native min/max keeps from<=to. The source is yours: edit freely. The richer multi-month / custom-calendar-grid picker is the calendar wire (a separate component) or a documented escape-hatch typed-TS micro-enhancement; lievit-ui's date-picker is the native input(s).

items.name: description-list
items.description: A read-only label->value list rendered as a native <dl> grid: each row a <dt> term + <dd> definition (description-list.item), laid out in N columns via the --lv-dl-cols custom property, with per-item columnSpan. size (sm/md/lg) controls row gap + base text density. bordered wraps the <dl> in card chrome. title + extra slot render a header row above the <dl>. description-list.item supports a colon param to append ':' to the term. The detail-page primitive the kit's AdminViewView.Section and Filament's infolist section grid map onto. Native dt/dd association (no extra ARIA). Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui description-list partial -- a read-only label->value list rendered as a real
grid: each row a
term +
definition (description-list.item). This is the detail-page primitive the kit's AdminViewView.Section (its ordered label-to-display map) and Filament's infolist section grid need: a structured READ of a record, N columns, terms left, values right. NOT data-table (no sorting/selection), NOT a form (read-only, no inputs). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout; the values are already-projected strings the controller resolved (the kit's "silent-slot" lesson: the template iterates resolved display, never re-runs a closure). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the semantic HTML for label->value is
/
/
(shadcn has no dedicated export; its dashboards compose a grid of dt/dd). Filament's infolist section is an N-column grid of entry-wrappers (label column + value column). We ship the
grid container here and the
+
pair in description-list/item.jte (mirroring item.jte + item/* sub-parts). `columns` drives the grid via the inline --lv-dl-cols custom property (no inline stylesheet element, CSP-friendly even under a style-nonce policy); an item may span more than one column (description-list.item columnSpan via a grid-column span), mirroring the kit's Entry.columnSpan / ResolvedNode.Field.columnSpan. The grid collapses to one column on small screens by default; pass a Tailwind sm: utility through cssClass to tune the breakpoint. TOKEN FIDELITY: terms read --lv-color-muted-fg, values --lv-color-fg; the grid gap reads the space scale, so a theme retints + respaces the whole list. size controls the row gap and base text size (sm/md/lg scale) so a caller can match the surrounding page density without overriding individual rows. bordered wraps the
in a card chrome (border + radius + bg) for use inside panel-like surfaces; off by default (lists are usually flush in an existing card/section). title + extra render a header row above the
when the list needs a visible label. A11y: a native
already associates each
with its
for assistive tech (a description list); no extra ARIA needed. The list is read-only content, not a control. Params: gg.jte.Content content the rows (description-list.item partials). int columns = 1 the column count from the sm: breakpoint (1..N); 1 => a stacked list. boolean bordered = false wrap the
in a card chrome (border + radius + bg). String size = "md" row-gap + base text size: "sm" | "md" | "lg". String title = null optional section heading rendered above the
; omitted when null. gg.jte.Content extra = null optional slot beside the title (actions, a badge, a link). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the
root. Usage: @@template.lievit.description-list(columns = 2, content = @@` @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Nome", content = @@`Mario Rossi`) @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Email", content = @@`mario@@example.com`) @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Note", columnSpan = 2, content = @@`Cliente storico.`) `) @@template.lievit.description-list(columns = 2, title = "Dettagli contatto", bordered = true, content = @@` @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Nome", content = @@`Mario Rossi`) `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param int columns = 1 @param boolean bordered = false @param String size = "md" @param String title = null @param gg.jte.Content extra = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var cols = Math.max(1, columns);} !{var gapYClass = "sm".equals(size) ? "gap-y-[var(--lv-space-2)]" : "lg".equals(size) ? "gap-y-[var(--lv-space-7)]" : "gap-y-[var(--lv-space-4)]";} !{var textSizeClass = "sm".equals(size) ? "text-[length:var(--lv-text-xs)]" : "lg".equals(size) ? "text-[length:var(--lv-text-base)]" : "text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)]";} !{var borderedClass = bordered ? "border border-[var(--lv-color-border)] rounded-[var(--lv-radius-md)] bg-[var(--lv-color-card)] p-[var(--lv-space-4)]" : "";} @if(title != null)

${title}

@if(extra != null)
${extra}
@endif
@endif
${content}

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui description-list/item partial -- one label->value row of a description-list: a <dt>
  term + a <dd> definition, stacked. The unit the kit's AdminViewView.Section entries
  (label -> display string) and Filament's infolist entry-wrapper map onto.

  TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout; `term` is plain text (a humanized
  attribute label), `content` is the already-projected value markup (a string, a badge, a swatch).

  STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the semantic pair is <dt> (term) + <dd> (definition); both
  must be direct children of the <dl> for the native association, so this partial emits them as a
  sibling pair (NO wrapping element between the <dl> and its dt/dd). The term sits above the value
  (Filament's stacked entry-wrapper); use the parent description-list `columns` for the multi-column
  grid. An entry may span more columns via columnSpan (the kit Entry.columnSpan), applied to BOTH
  the dt and dd so the pair stays a grid cell-pair.

  Text size is NOT set on dt/dd: it is inherited from the parent <dl> container, which drives it
  via `size` (sm/md/lg). This keeps the pair's text density consistent with the container.

  colon: when true (default) appends ":" to the rendered term text so authors write plain labels
  ("Nome") and the colon is a formatting concern, not data. Set false for contexts that supply
  their own punctuation or where a colon is semantically wrong.

  A11y: native <dt>/<dd> association; the term labels the value with no extra ARIA. `content` may be
  an infolist-entry (a badge / swatch / icon), which carries its own semantics.

  Params:
    String term                   the label (a humanized attribute name).
    gg.jte.Content content        the value markup (string, badge, infolist-entry...).
    int columnSpan  = 1           parent grid columns this pair spans (the kit Entry.columnSpan).
    boolean colon   = true        append ":" to the term text; set false to suppress.
    String cssClass = ""          extra utility classes on the <dd> value.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Email", content = @@`mario@@example.com`)
    @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Stato", content = @@`@@template.lievit.badge(label = "Attivo", variant = "success")`)
    @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Note", columnSpan = 2, content = @@`Cliente storico.`)
    @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Nome", colon = false, content = @@`Mario Rossi`)
--%>
@param String term
@param gg.jte.Content content
@param int columnSpan = 1
@param boolean colon = true
@param String cssClass = ""

!{var span = Math.max(1, columnSpan);}
!{var spanStyle = span > 1 ? "grid-column:span " + span + " / span " + span + ";" : "";}
!{var termText = colon ? term + ":" : term;}

<dt
  data-slot="description-list-term"
  class="font-[var(--lv-font-medium)] text-[var(--lv-color-muted-fg)]"
  style="${spanStyle}"
>${termText}</dt>
<dd
  data-slot="description-list-value"
  class="m-0 mb-[var(--lv-space-1)] font-[var(--lv-font-normal)] text-[var(--lv-color-fg)] ${cssClass}"
  style="${spanStyle}"
>${content}</dd>

items.docs: Copies the description-list partial (+ its description-list/item child) to your JTE root at lievit/description-list.jte, reached as @template.lievit.description-list(columns=2, content=@`...`). Fill it with description-list.item rows (term + content value); the value content can be a plain string or an infolist-entry (badge / swatch / icon). columns drives the grid; an item may span more columns via columnSpan. size=sm|md|lg tunes the row gap and text density. bordered adds card chrome. title + extra render a header row. colon=false on an item suppresses the auto-appended ':'. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: dialog
items.description: An accessible modal dialog. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): open state lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the body + footer are owned template regions, not slots. Replaces the <lv-dialog> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dialog WIRE template -- an accessible modal dialog whose open state lives server-side in DialogComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/dialog/DialogComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `open` action and the backdrop / close button fire `close` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the body + footer are NOT JTE Content slots supplied at call time: they are OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body + footer regions directly (this is copy-in source), or drives them from @Wire fields / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children": owned, server-rendered, cannot fail to project. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal) pattern, Radix Dialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the panel is role="dialog" + aria-modal="true"; - aria-labelledby -> the heading id when a heading is set, else aria-label="Dialog"; - aria-describedby -> the description id when a description is set; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree. Open-state is SERVER (wire); focus-trap + Escape are a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement (data-lv-dialog hooks): zero open-state in the client, only the focus mechanics. Escape + the backdrop dispatch the `close` action; the close button does too. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Dialog/Modal): div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true] labelled by the heading + described by the description; closed overlay removed from the a11y tree via `hidden`. Params: open boolean modal open state (server-owned) heading String dialog title; wired to aria-labelledby (empty hides the header) description String supporting line; wired to aria-describedby (empty omits it) dismissible boolean when true the backdrop + Escape route to `close` (locked server-side) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, then put YOUR body + footer markup in the owned regions):

Your owned, server-rendered body here.

--%> @param boolean open @param String heading @param String description @param boolean dismissible @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var headingId = "lv-dialog-heading";} !{var descId = "lv-dialog-desc";} !{var hasHeading = heading != null && !heading.isEmpty();} !{var hasDesc = description != null && !description.isEmpty();}
<%-- Backdrop: a sibling button covering the overlay so a click on the empty area closes (dismissible). Keeping it a real button keeps the close path CSP-clean + keyboard-safe. --%> @if(dismissible) @endif
@if(hasHeading)

${heading}

@if(hasDesc)

${description}

@endif
@endif <%-- Owned body region: replace this with your dialog body markup (server-rendered). --%>

Dialog content.

<%-- Owned footer region: replace this with your action buttons (server-rendered). --%>

items.docs: Register DialogComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.dialog(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` to show it and `close` (backdrop / Escape / close button) to hide it. Put your dialog body + footer markup directly in the data-lv-dialog-body / data-lv-dialog-footer regions of the copied template (they are owned source, NOT slots). Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-dialog-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the server `close` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=close button, so Escape and the button take the same wire path), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/dialog")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: drawer
items.description: A side-panel overlay that slides in from a viewport edge, headless CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED (architecture contract overlay doctrine). UNCONTROLLED (default): the trigger is a real <button command="show-modal" commandfor> (browser invoker command API); the browser opens the <dialog> as a native modal (top-layer, ::backdrop, focus-trap, Escape) with zero JS. CONTROLLED: pass open (from the caller's @Wire boolean) and closeAction (wire action name); when open=true, a positioned panel + scrim are rendered; data-lievit-focus-trap activates the shared focus-trap enhancer (Tab cycle, scroll-lock, Esc fires closeAction, inert on behind-content); when open=false, the panel is hidden (absent from a11y tree). Placement: right (default) | left | top | bottom. Sizes: sm (320px) | md (480px, default) | lg (640px) | xl (800px) | full. modal=false renders the APG non-modal dialog variant (no scrim, no trap, page interactive). push=true (non-modal only) shifts page content via CSS sibling selector. destroyOnClose=true removes the panel subtree from the DOM when closed.
