Closable controls X + Esc + scrim-click together. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal/Non-modal): role=dialog, aria-modal (modal mode only), aria-labelledby/aria-label, icon-only close button aria-label. Content: owned body (never a slot -- the projection contract), optional footer. Replaces the old static slide-over.jte (which used native <dialog> top-layer only) with the full headless WIRE-ready surface. No Java component, no @Wire field.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui drawer partial -- headless CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED side-panel overlay. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (focus-trap.enhancer.ts). No Java component, no @Wire field, no _component/_instance/_componentSnapshot params. The open-state belongs to the CALLER. CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED doctrine (architecture contract, "Overlay & stateful primitives"): UNCONTROLLED (default; open=false, closeAction=null, no trigger rendered externally): Pass a `trigger` Content slot: a real ` ) Controlled (server-owned open, caller @Wire drives open state): @@template.lievit.drawer( id = "detail-panel", placement = "right", title = "Record detail", content = @@`
...infolist...
`, size = "lg", closable = true, open = myComponent.drawerOpen(), closeAction = "closeDrawer" ) --%> @import gg.jte.Content @param String id = "lv-drawer" @param String title = null @param String placement = "right" @param String size = "md" @param boolean modal = true @param boolean closable = true @param boolean push = false @param boolean destroyOnClose = false @param boolean open = false @param String closeAction = null @param gg.jte.Content trigger = null @param gg.jte.Content content @param gg.jte.Content footer = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var titleId = id + "-title";} !{var hasTitle = title != null && !title.isBlank();} !{var isControlled = closeAction != null && !closeAction.isEmpty();} !{var trapModal = isControlled && modal;} !{var showPanel = open || !destroyOnClose;} <%-- Resolve placement to one of the four canonical edges. --%> !{var edge = switch (placement) { case "left", "top", "bottom" -> placement; default -> "right"; };} <%-- Resolve size to the five canonical values. --%> !{var sizeVal = switch (size) { case "sm", "lg", "xl", "full" -> size; default -> "md"; };} <%-- Panel dimension token: left/right = width; top/bottom = height. Net-new --lv-space-drawer-* tokens are TOKENS_NEEDED (not yet in lievit-tokens.css); meanwhile fall back to fixed inline values so the component renders correctly. Replace the inline values with var(--lv-space-drawer-*) once the tokens are added. --lv-motion-slide is also TOKENS_NEEDED; fall back to --lv-duration-slow meanwhile. --%> !{var panelDim = switch (sizeVal) { case "sm" -> "320px"; case "lg" -> "640px"; case "xl" -> "800px"; case "full" -> ("left".equals(edge) || "right".equals(edge)) ? "100vw" : "100dvh"; default -> "480px"; };} <%-- Push flag: only meaningful when modal=false; ignored (server validates) for modal=true. --%> !{var pushActive = push && !modal;} @if(trigger != null) <%-- Trigger button: uncontrolled path -- browser opens the as a modal (no JS). --%> @endif @if(isControlled && modal && open) <%-- Controlled-mode scrim: full-viewport cover behind the panel when modal=true + open=true. --%>
@endif <%-- The panel element. UNCONTROLLED mode (isControlled=false): - No hidden attribute (browser manages show/hide via showModal()). - No data-lievit-focus-trap (browser native modal trap handles Tab cycling). - No data-lievit-escape-action (browser cancels on Esc natively). - Panel is a element (native top-layer, native backdrop). CONTROLLED mode (isControlled=true): - Panel is a positioned
(not a : the controlled open/hidden cycle uses the HTML hidden attribute, not the dialog open attribute, so a
is correct -- the element's showModal() / close() API is not called). - hidden="${!open}" when destroyOnClose=false (smart attr: bare hidden when !open). - Subtree absent from output when destroyOnClose=true + !open (the @if guard). - data-lievit-focus-trap="" (activates enhancer trap + scroll-lock) when modal=true. - data-lievit-escape-action="${closeAction}" when modal=true + closable=true. - data-push="${pushActive}" when push=true + modal=false (layout-shift CSS hook). - Positioned at the chosen edge via CSS [data-placement] + [data-size] in drawer.css. --%> @if(isControlled) @if(showPanel)
<%-- Header: title + optional close X button. --%>

${hasTitle ? title : ""}

@if(closable) <%-- Controlled close button: l:click fires the wire round-trip. Icon-only: aria-label mandatory. --%> @endif
<%-- Body: OWNED server-rendered content (never a native slot; the projection contract). --%>
${content}
@if(footer != null) <%-- Footer: caller-provided action buttons. --%>
${footer}
@endif
@endif @else <%-- Uncontrolled mode: native element (browser owns top-layer + focus-trap + Esc). --%> @if(closable && modal) <%-- Backdrop-dismiss (uncontrolled): a full-bleed button behind the panel. --%> @endif <%-- Header. --%>

${hasTitle ? title : ""}

@if(closable) <%-- Uncontrolled close button:
native close (no JS). --%>
@endif
<%-- Body: OWNED server-rendered content. --%>
${content}
@if(footer != null)
${footer}
@endif @endif

items.docs: Copies drawer.jte + drawer.css to your JTE root at lievit/drawer.jte and lievit/drawer.css, reached as @template.lievit.drawer(...). UNCONTROLLED (default): pass a trigger Content slot and a unique id; the browser opens and closes the panel natively with zero JS (command="show-modal"). CONTROLLED: pass open (your @Wire boolean) and closeAction (the wire action name) to let the server own the open-state; the focus-trap.enhancer.ts handles the focus cycle + scroll-lock + Esc. Both modes support: title (panel accessible name -> aria-labelledby), placement (right|left|top|bottom), size (sm|md|lg|xl|full), modal (default true; false = non-modal variant), closable (X + Esc + scrim, default true), push (layout-shift for non-modal; requires a [data-slot=page-content] or <main> sibling), destroyOnClose (remove subtree when closed, default false). Install the focus-trap enhancer when using controlled+modal mode. TOKENS_NEEDED: --lv-space-drawer-sm/md/lg/xl (panel dimensions) and --lv-motion-slide (slide transition duration) -- the CSS falls back to fixed values + --lv-duration-slow until the coordinator adds these tokens.

items.name: drawer
items.description: A modal side panel anchored to a viewport edge (right/left/top/bottom). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): open state lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the body is an owned template region, not a slot. Replaces the <lv-drawer> Lit island. Same WAI-ARIA APG dialog semantics as the dialog wire (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui drawer WIRE template -- a modal side panel anchored to one viewport edge whose open state lives server-side in DrawerComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/drawer/DrawerComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires `open` and the backdrop / close button fire `close` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the body is NOT a JTE Content slot supplied at call time: it is OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body region directly (this is copy-in source), or drives it from a @Wire field / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children". STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal) pattern, Radix Dialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited), presented as a side sheet: - the panel is role="dialog" + aria-modal="true"; - aria-labelledby -> the heading id when set, else aria-label="Drawer"; - the `side` field positions the panel against one edge (right/left/top/bottom); - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree. Open-state is SERVER (wire); focus-trap + Escape are a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement (data-lv-drawer hooks). Escape + the backdrop dispatch the `close` action. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Dialog/Modal): div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true] labelled by the heading; closed overlay removed from the a11y tree via `hidden`. Params: open boolean modal open state (server-owned) side String edge to anchor: "right" | "left" | "top" | "bottom" heading String panel title; wired to aria-labelledby (empty hides the header) dismissible boolean when true the backdrop + Escape route to `close` (locked server-side) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, then put YOUR body markup in the data-lv-drawer-body region):

Your owned, server-rendered body here.

--%> @param boolean open @param String side @param String heading @param boolean dismissible @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var headingId = "lv-drawer-heading";} !{var hasHeading = heading != null && !heading.isEmpty();} !{ var edge = side == null ? "right" : side; String panelPos; switch (edge) { case "left" -> panelPos = "top: 0; left: 0; bottom: 0; width: min(90vw, 24rem); border-right: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; case "top" -> panelPos = "top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; max-height: 85vh; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; case "bottom" -> panelPos = "bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; max-height: 85vh; border-top: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; default -> panelPos = "top: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0; width: min(90vw, 24rem); border-left: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; } }
@if(dismissible) @endif
@if(hasHeading)

${heading}

@endif <%-- Owned body region: replace this with your drawer body markup (server-rendered). --%>

Drawer content.

items.docs: Register DrawerComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.drawer(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` to show it and `close` (backdrop / Escape / close button) to hide it; set `side` to 'right' (default) | 'left' | 'top' | 'bottom'. Put your drawer body markup directly in the data-lv-drawer-body region of the copied template (owned source, NOT a slot). Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-drawer-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the server `close` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=close button), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). For the richer header/description/footer four-side panel use the sheet wire instead. Adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: dropdown-menu
items.description: Controlled/uncontrolled headless menu-button dropdown (v-next). A trigger + a floating role='menu' panel built on the native HTML popover API + CSS Anchor Positioning. UNCONTROLLED by default: the browser owns open/close via popovertarget (zero JS round-trip, top-layer render, light-dismiss, Tab exits). CONTROLLED opt-in: pass open={wire.open()} to bind panel presence to a Wire field. Keyboard navigation (Arrow/Home/End/Esc/Tab/typeahead) via the shared collection-nav.enhancer.ts in roving-tabindex mode. Focus returns to trigger on close via popover-anchor.enhancer.ts. Items are real <a href> (navigation) or <button> (action), role=menuitem/menuitemcheckbox/menuitemradio. Groups, labels, separator, submenu, shortcut composed from sub-partials. No Lit island, CSP-clean, zero inline script.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu (v-next) -- a trigger + a floating role="menu" panel. TIER: PARTIAL (headless controlled/uncontrolled overlay, REFORGE-AGENT-BRIEF tier doctrine). CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED model: - UNCONTROLLED (default, open=false): the panel is always in the DOM; the native HTML popover attribute controls its visibility (top-layer, light-dismiss, browser-managed aria-expanded on the trigger). Zero server round-trip to open. The trigger carries popovertarget to toggle the panel. Tab/Esc close via the platform + collection-nav. - CONTROLLED (open=true): the same panel element is rendered with the HTML popover "open" state forced, so it is visible. The caller binds `open` to its own Wire field. The caller's Wire component exposes an action (default name "close", or override via `escapeAction`) that flips open=false; the popover-anchor enhancer calls it on light-dismiss. In controlled mode the caller should NOT pass popovertarget on the trigger (it wires l:click to its own action instead); the trigger in this template omits popovertarget when open=true. - The open param distinguishes the TWO states: open=false (uncontrolled default -- panel in DOM, browser hides it) vs open=true (controlled -- panel in DOM, forced visible by adding the "open" attribute to the popover element). A controlled-but-closed state (panel absent from DOM) is expressed by the caller NOT rendering this template at all, or by using a JTE @if wrapping the whole @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu call in the caller's template. KEYBOARD (collection-nav.enhancer.ts, roving-tabindex + data-manual-activation="true"): - ArrowDown / ArrowUp: roving focus between enabled items; wraps at ends. - Home / End: jump to first / last enabled item. - Enter / Space: fires the item's own l:click action (manual-activation mode). - Esc: in CONTROLLED mode (open=true) fires data-lievit-collection-escape-action on the menu root (default "close") to sync server open-state; in UNCONTROLLED mode (open=false) the attr is absent, so Esc closes the native popover purely client-side (no wire round-trip). - Tab: exits the menu, focus leaves; non-modal (no focus trap, APG Menu + WCAG SC 2.1.2). ENHANCER_NEEDED -- roving-mode typeahead: the current collection-nav enhancer only runs typeahead in aria-activedescendant mode (the `else` branch in handleTypeahead); roving mode needs the same typeahead path gated on isRovingMode. ENHANCER_NEEDED -- submenu ArrowRight/Left: collection-nav does not yet support data-lievit-collection-submenu-action or ArrowRight-to-open / ArrowLeft-to-close. POPOVER SEAM (lv-popover Stimulus controller, converted from popover-anchor.enhancer.ts): - Panel carries data-controller="lv-popover" so the shared lv-popover controller owns the native-popover seam: opener recording, focus-return on light-dismiss, aria-expanded mirror, and the controlled/uncontrolled wire-close doctrine (via the DismissableController base). Stimulus connects/disconnects the controller across the wire morph + idiomorph + Turbo Drive, so there is no WeakSet/afterCall bookkeeping and no leaked listener. popover-anchor.enhancer.ts stays installed for not-yet-converted overlays and SKIPS this panel (its data-controller~="lv-popover" guard), so the close never fires twice while both paths coexist. - Panel carries popover="auto" + data-lv-opener="${triggerId}" for focus-return on close. - CONTROLLED/UNCONTROLLED wire-close: data-lv-wire-close="${escapeAction}" is stamped ONLY when open=true (controlled). On light-dismiss the controller reads that attribute and calls the named action on the component owning the panel, syncing server open-state. In UNCONTROLLED mode (open=false) the attribute is absent and the controller fires NO wire call -- the browser owns the open state and the close is purely client-side. This is the controlled/uncontrolled doctrine: an uncontrolled overlay must never round-trip a "close" to a host that may have no such @LievitAction (that mapped to 410 UNKNOWN_COMPONENT / "page expired" on data tables). - In controlled mode the caller's Wire component exposes the named action (default "close", override via escapeAction); the configurable name needs no controller patch. OVERLAY: CSS Anchor Positioning. - The trigger sets anchor-name; the panel sets position-anchor + position-area. - placement: "bottom-start" (default) | "bottom-end" | "top-start" | "top-end". - position-try-fallbacks:flip-block flips to the opposite block side when cramped. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Menu Button): - Trigger: native <%-- MENU PANEL: popover="auto" in both modes. Uncontrolled mode (open=false): the panel is always in the DOM; the native popover hides it until the trigger fires. top-layer render escapes overflow:hidden ancestors. Controlled mode (open=true): the "open" attribute on the popover element forces the panel visible (the HTML popover open state); the caller's Wire field drives re-render. data-lv-opener links the popover-anchor enhancer so focus returns to the trigger on close. data-lievit-collection activates collection-nav in roving-tabindex mode for keyboard nav. --%>
${content}

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/separator partial -- a non-selectable divider between dropdown-menu groups (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). Pure CSS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A token-styled hairline carrying role="separator" so assistive tech announces the group boundary. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG): role="separator" inside the role="menu" panel. Params: (none) Usage: @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.separator() --%>

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/label partial -- a non-interactive caption for a group of menu items (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). The shadcn DropdownMenuLabel: a small medium-weight heading that titles a DropdownMenuGroup; it is NOT a menuitem (no role, not focusable), so assistive tech reads it as static text within the menu, not as a selectable entry. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure token-styled text. STRUCTURE (shadcn DropdownMenuLabel, new-york-v4): px-2 py-1.5 text-sm font-medium, with an `inset` variant (data-[inset]:pl-8) that aligns the label text under the items' icon column when the group has checkable/icon items. A11y: a plain text caption (no role). Paired with dropdown-menu/group it can name the group via aria-labelledby (pass a matching `id`). Params: String label the caption text. String id = "" optional id, so a dropdown-menu/group can aria-labelledby it. boolean inset = false indents the label under the items' leading-icon column (shadcn inset). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the label. Usage: @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.label(label = "My account") --%> @param String label @param String id = "" @param boolean inset = false @param String cssClass = ""
${label}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/group partial -- a semantic grouping of related menu items inside a dropdown-menu (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). The shadcn DropdownMenuGroup: it bundles items that belong together (separated from other groups by a dropdown-menu/separator), and carries role="group" so assistive tech announces the boundary; pass a `labelId` that matches a dropdown-menu/label `id` to name the group (aria-labelledby). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A wrapper; the items are OWNED, server-rendered. STRUCTURE (shadcn DropdownMenuGroup, Radix Group): a plain wrapper element. shadcn's div has no role; we add role="group" (WAI-ARIA APG menu grouping) so the group boundary is announced, the accessible-faithful choice for a labelled group of menuitems. A11y: role="group" inside the role="menu" panel; aria-labelledby -> a dropdown-menu/label id. Params: gg.jte.Content content the group's items (dropdown-menu/item entries), OWNED. String labelId = "" id of a dropdown-menu/label that names this group. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the group. Usage: @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.label(label = "Account", id = "acct-lbl") @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.group(labelId = "acct-lbl", content = @@` @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(href = "/profile", label = "Profile") @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(href = "/settings", label = "Settings") `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String labelId = "" @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/shortcut partial -- the right-aligned keyboard hint of a menu item (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). The shadcn DropdownMenuShortcut: ml-auto text-xs tracking-widest text-muted-foreground -- decorative text pinned to the item's end showing the accelerator (e.g. "Cmd+S"). It is announced as decoration, not as an action. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure token-styled text. Back-compat: dropdown-menu/item still accepts a `shortcut` String and renders the same visual inline; this partial is the standalone shadcn-faithful data-slot for callers composing an item's trailing region by hand (e.g. inside a custom item body). A11y: aria-hidden="true" -- the glyph is a visual hint, the real binding lives in the platform. Params: String shortcut the accelerator text (e.g. "Cmd+S", "Del"). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the shortcut. Usage: @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.shortcut(shortcut = "Cmd+S") --%> @param String shortcut @param String cssClass = "" ${shortcut}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/sub partial -- a NESTED submenu inside a dropdown-menu (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). Bundles the shadcn DropdownMenuSub + DropdownMenuSubTrigger + DropdownMenuSubContent triple into one server-pure disclosure: a native
whose is the SubTrigger (role="menuitem", aria-haspopup="menu", a trailing chevron-right) and whose body is the SubContent (role="menu") holding the nested items. TIER DECISION (why this shape, server-first): a true Radix flyout submenu reveals on hover / arrow-right and floats beside the parent -- that hover/arrow reveal + repositioning is the ONE piece that genuinely needs JS. The zero-JS, CSP-clean equivalent is the native
disclosure: the SubTrigger toggles the nested menu OPEN INLINE (the browser owns the open state, no script), the items are real /

items.docs: Copies the dropdown-menu family to your JTE root. UNCONTROLLED (default): pass id + trigger + content; the browser toggles the panel. CONTROLLED: also pass open={wire.open()} to bind to a Wire field; expose a 'close' action on the Wire component (the popover-anchor enhancer calls it on light-dismiss). Install collection-nav.enhancer.ts + popover-anchor.enhancer.ts for keyboard nav and focus return. Compose content from: @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item (give href for navigation, formId/formAction for form POST, wireClick+wireArgs for per-row wire action; type=checkbox/radio for checkable items), @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.label, @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.group, @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.separator, @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.shortcut, @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.sub.

items.name: empty
items.description:
A full-width blank-slate placeholder for surfaces with no data (empty list, empty search, empty inbox). Composes an illustration (default Lucide icon per variant, a custom imageUrl, or a caller-owned image Content slot) + optional title

+ optional description

+ optional action Content slot (CTA). Four variants drive the default icon: default (inbox), search (search-x), error (triangle-alert), offline (wifi-off). Three sizes scale the illustration, title, and description proportionally: sm (fits inside a combobox listbox), md (default, inline section), lg (full-page blank-slate). iconOnly=true suppresses text + action (canonical for sm inside small containers). No hardcoded copy: title=null omits the title element; the controller supplies all strings from its own message source. Purely presentational PARTIAL (ADR-0012): no client state, no enhancer, no wire action. Extra attributes via two escaping channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, $unsafe, STATIC author strings only such as aria-label or id) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map of String to String, each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute). The custom imageUrl is also safe-escaped before emission as an src attribute value.
items.docs: Copies the empty partial to your JTE root at lievit/empty.jte, reached as @template.lievit.empty(). Call with no params for a zero-config placeholder. Pass variant to pick the default icon intent (default/search/error/offline). Pass size to scale the illustration (sm/md/lg). Pass title and description as strings from your controller's message source -- the partial never bakes in default copy. Pass imageUrl for a custom illustration URL (safe-escaped as an HTML attribute). Pass imageAlt with a non-empty string to mark the image as semantic (otherwise aria-hidden decorative treatment applies). Pass iconOnly=true to suppress all text and action (canonical empty state inside a combobox dropdown or a small table cell). Pass an action Content slot for a CTA button or link. Pass an image Content slot to fully replace the illustration layer with your own markup. Extra attributes go through two channels: attrs is TRUSTED raw ($unsafe, static author-typed strings only -- aria-label, id, data-testid, wire directives -- NEVER per-row DB values); dataAttrs is SAFE (Map of String to String, each value HTML-escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute, the only safe channel for dynamic data). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: field
items.description:
v-next FormField layout wrapper: label + control + hint + message regions with full accessible wiring auto-derived from the control id. Adds layout (vertical/horizontal/inline), size (sm/md/lg), status (error/warning/success), a v-next aria-live message slot always pre-registered in the DOM, hint/labelTooltip regions, labelWidth for horizontal layouts, cssClass/labelCssClass/attrs/dataAttrs, leading and extra slots. Back-compat preserved: forId alias for controlId, orientation alias for layout, description/hint aliases, single-String error + List errors multi-error paths (deduped
    ), control slot alongside the v-next content slot, field-content sub-slot for horizontal helper text. The root cascades --lv-color-destructive to its labels via data-invalid; the message

    is always in DOM so aria-live is pre-registered. Ships with field/group, field/set, field/title and field/separator sub-partials.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui field/group partial -- shadcn's FieldGroup: the vertical stack that holds a set of @@template.lievit.field calls (and FieldSeparators), and establishes the container-query context that drives a field's "responsive" orientation. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. It is the `@container/field-group` parent shadcn uses so an inner Field with orientation="responsive" flips from stacked to side-by-side at the `@md` breakpoint of THIS group's width (not the viewport), so a field group in a narrow column stays stacked. STRUCTURE: shadcn's FieldGroup is a `gap`-ed flex column marked `@container/field-group`; the responsive Field reads it via `@md/field-group:`. We reproduce that exactly with tokens. A11y: a plain grouping wrapper; the grouped controls carry their own labels. For a semantic group with a single legend, use @@template.lievit.field.set instead. Params: gg.jte.Content content the fields (and separators) in this group. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the group. Usage: @@template.lievit.field.group(content = @@` @@template.lievit.field(forId = "first", label = "First name", control = @@`...`) @@template.lievit.field.separator() @@template.lievit.field(forId = "last", label = "Last name", control = @@`...`) `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui field/set partial -- shadcn's FieldSet + FieldLegend: a semantic
grouping several related fields (or radio/checkbox options) under one , with an optional muted description. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): a real native
+ is the platform group primitive (the same call radio-group makes). shadcn renders FieldSet as a
with a FieldLegend and a FieldGroup of inner fields; we reproduce that with the native elements so the group name is announced for free and the disabled attribute cascades to every nested control. A11y (WAI-ARIA grouping): native
with a names the group; assistive tech announces the legend with each contained control. The optional description sits below the legend and is referenced by the controls' own descriptions, not the fieldset. LEGEND VARIANT (shadcn's FieldLegend variant): `legendVariant` picks the legend size, mirroring shadcn's FieldLegend variant="legend" (text-base, the default) vs variant="label" (text-sm, a compact legend used when the group reads like a single labelled field rather than a section). Params: String legend = null visible text naming the group. String legendVariant = "legend" "legend" (text-base, default) | "label" (text-sm, compact). String description = null muted helper text below the legend. boolean disabled = false native fieldset disable: cascades to every nested control. gg.jte.Content content the grouped fields (usually wrapped in @@template.lievit.field.group). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the fieldset. Usage: @@template.lievit.field.set(legend = "Address", description = "Where do we ship?", content = @@` @@template.lievit.field.group(content = @@` @@template.lievit.field(forId = "street", label = "Street", control = @@`...`) @@template.lievit.field(forId = "city", label = "City", control = @@`...`) `) `) --%> @param String legend = null @param String legendVariant = "legend" @param String description = null @param boolean disabled = false @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = "" !{var hasDesc = description != null && !description.isBlank();}
@if(legend != null && !legend.isBlank()) ${legend} @endif @if(hasDesc)

${description}

@endif ${content}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui field/title partial -- shadcn's FieldTitle: a small medium-weight title for a field block (e.g. the heading of a switch/checkbox row, or a labelled group inside a Field), distinct from the

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui field/separator partial -- shadcn's FieldSeparator: a divider between fields in a FieldGroup, optionally with a centred inline label ("or", "and"). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. STRUCTURE: shadcn's FieldSeparator is a Separator with optional centred children. We render the same role="separator" rule (token-coloured) and, when a label is given, overlay it centred on a background chip so the line reads as broken by the word. A11y (WAI-ARIA separator): role="separator" + aria-orientation="horizontal"; when a label is shown it is decorative text, the separator role still carries the grouping semantics. Params: String label = null optional centred inline text (e.g. "or"). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wrapper. Usage: @@template.lievit.field.separator() @@template.lievit.field.separator(label = "or") --%> @param String label = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var hasLabel = label != null && !label.isBlank();}
@if(hasLabel) ${label} @endif

items.docs:
Copies the field partial (+ its field/group, field/set, field/title and field/separator children) to your JTE root at lievit/field.jte, reached as @template.lievit.field(controlId=..., label=..., content=@`...`). v-next: use `controlId` + `content` slot + `status`/`message` for the accessible status pattern; the message

is always in the DOM so aria-live is pre-registered (no reflow on first message). For horizontal layouts pass `labelWidth` to control the label column width via --lv-field-label-width. Back-compat: old callers using `forId`, `control` slot, `error`/`errors`, `description`, `orientation` continue to work unchanged. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: file-upload
items.description: A server-rendered drag-and-drop dropzone + file list whose upload runs over the wire upload path: a native <input type=file l:upload> POSTs the bytes out-of-band, the endpoint returns a signed temp-file reference, and the wire sets the server-held file list (the bytes never ride the snapshot). Remove drops a file from the server-held list. A CSP-clean island progressively enhances the dropzone with drag-and-drop, local thumbnail previews + progress, remove and reorder, driven by the runtime upload events (the temp-upload wire protocol seam); JS-off the native multiple <input type=file> still POSTs. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-file-upload> Lit island. WAI-ARIA dropzone-as-button + labelled file list. The runtime upload directive (l:upload) is ready (runtime/features/uploads.ts).

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import dev.lievit.wire.FileUploadComponent @import dev.lievit.wire.FileUploadComponent.UploadedFile @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui file-upload WIRE template -- a server-rendered dropzone + file list whose upload runs over the wire upload path (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/file-upload/FileUploadComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The dropzone + the selected-file list are server-rendered HTML. The upload itself is the wire runtime's upload directive: the native POSTs the chosen bytes out-of-band to the upload endpoint (runtime features/uploads.ts), which returns a signed temp-file reference; the wire sets the bound `files` field to that reference and re-renders this list. The bytes never ride the snapshot (state-never-code), only the reference does. A remove arms the file name via $set then a confirm action drops it from the server-held list. No . Runtime upload directive: READY (runtime/features/uploads.ts installs l:upload). The adopter wires the SERVER half (the /lievit/upload controller that stores to temp + returns the signed ref); that is application-specific. If you prefer a plain multipart POST instead of the wire upload, swap the l:upload input for inside a
and handle the upload in a controller (the partial recipe). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG dropzone-as-button + a labelled file list (the model the dropped island cited): the zone is role="button" tabindex=0 with aria-describedby -> the hint; the native is visually hidden but reachable; each file row has a labelled remove button. Native input + l:upload beats a custom uploader: the platform owns the file picker and drag-drop, the wire owns the transport, the server owns the list. A11y: div[role=button][aria-label][aria-describedby], visually-hidden input[type=file], ul[aria-label="Selected files"] with per-row aria-label="Remove ". Params: accept String comma-separated MIME types / extensions for the native input multiple boolean allow multiple files (locked) disabled boolean disable the control (locked) dropLabel String primary label inside the dropzone hint String constraint hint below the label label String accessible label for the dropzone button pendingRemove String the file name armed for removal (set via $set, confirmed by removePending) _instance FileUploadComponent the live instance (reads the server-held file list) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, point l:upload at your endpoint, persist refs on submit): register FileUploadComponent as a PROTOTYPE @Bean; install the wire upload runtime; mount it. --%> @param String accept @param boolean multiple @param boolean disabled @param String dropLabel @param String hint @param String label @param String pendingRemove @param FileUploadComponent _instance @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var inputId = "lv-file-input";} !{var hintId = "lv-file-hint";} !{List files = _instance.files();}
@if(!files.isEmpty())
    @for(UploadedFile file : files)
  • ${file.name()} ${FileUploadComponent.formatSize(file.size())} @if(pendingRemove.equals(file.name())) @else @endif
    • @endfor
@endif

items.docs: Register FileUploadComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). The native input carries l:upload="files": install the wire upload runtime (installUploads from runtime/features/uploads.ts) and wire the SERVER half (the /lievit/upload controller that validates + stores to temp + returns the signed ref). The dropzone + file list render server-side from the `files` field; a remove arms the file name via $set then the removePending action drops it. If you prefer a plain multipart POST, swap the l:upload input for <input type=file name=...> inside <form method=post enctype=multipart/form-data>. Call enhanceFileUploads() once from main.ts after startLievit() for the drag-and-drop dropzone + local thumbnail previews + progress (driven by the runtime lievit:upload-progress events) + reorder/remove of pending previews; JS-off the native multiple input still POSTs. The temp-upload wire protocol (l:upload -> /lievit/upload -> signed ref) is the seam the adopter wires server-side. Adjust the template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: form
items.description: v-next server-first Form: a native <form> wrapper that owns a form-level error summary region (always in DOM for aria-live pre-registration) and stacks its field children. v-next adds: errors list (List<Map<String,String>> with fieldId+message for anchor links), errorSummaryHeading, focusOnError (data-lv-autofocus for enhancer focus management), layout (stacked/inline/grid), size + labelWidth propagated via CSS custom properties (--lv-form-size, --lv-form-label-width), autocomplete/enctype/novalidate/ariaLabel/headingId HTML controls, and a footer slot. Back-compat preserved: single-String error path, data-slot=form-error slot name, id/${errorId} derivation, aria-describedby conditional wiring, method/action/cssClass params. Pairs with the field partial for per-field wiring.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui form partial (v-next) -- the server-first Form: a native
wrapper that owns a form-level validation summary region and stacks its field children. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. No client state: validation runs server-side, the controller passes `errors` (form-level list) down, and this partial does the aria wiring. JS-off the form POSTs natively; with the wire runtime present the same markup submits over the wire. STRUCTURE:
[heading when non-empty] [error list with fieldId anchor links or plain text]
${content} [
${footer}
when footer != null]
A11y: - Native
landmark: no extra role needed. - Error summary: `role="alert"` carries implicit aria-live="assertive" aria-atomic="true" per the ARIA spec (WAI-ARIA APG Alert pattern). `aria-live="assertive"` is also explicit for engine compatibility. The summary is always in the DOM so AT registers it early. - `hidden` attribute gates visibility without removing the element (preserves aria-live registration on page load; the AT hears the update when `hidden` is removed). - `focusOnError` + `data-lv-autofocus`: a JS enhancer focuses the summary after a failed submit, so keyboard/SR users land on the error list immediately. - `novalidate`: suppresses native browser validation UI (server validates; native popups disrupt the custom error pattern). Default true per WAI-ARIA form best practice. - `aria-describedby` on the form references the error summary while errors are present (removed when no errors to avoid spurious SR description). Params: String id = null form id (derives the error summary id -errors). String action = null form action URL. String method = "post" native method. String layout = "stacked" "stacked" | "inline" | "grid" form layout. String size = "md" size tier propagated via CSS custom property. String labelWidth = null CSS value for --lv-form-label-width cascade. List> errors = [] error list; each entry carries "fieldId" + "message". String errorSummaryHeading = "Correggi gli errori prima di procedere" heading text. String autocomplete = null native autocomplete attribute. boolean novalidate = true suppress native browser validation UI. String enctype = null native enctype attribute. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the . String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- static author-typed strings only. String headingId = null id for the error summary heading (for aria-labelledby). String ariaLabel = null aria-label on the . boolean focusOnError = false adds data-lv-autofocus to the error summary when errors. gg.jte.Content content REQUIRED the form body (fields, actions). gg.jte.Content header = null optional header rendered above the error summary. gg.jte.Content footer = null optional footer rendered below the content. Usage: @@template.lievit.form(id = "login", action = "/login", errors = formErrors, focusOnError = true, content = @@` @@template.lievit.field(controlId = "email", label = "Email", status = "error", message = errors.email(), content = @@`@@template.lievit.input(name="email",id="email")`) @@template.lievit.button(type = "submit", content = @@`Sign in`) `) --%> @param String id = null @param String action = null @param String method = "post" @param String layout = "stacked" @param String size = "md" @param String labelWidth = null @param java.util.List> errors = java.util.List.of() @param String errorSummaryHeading = "Correggi gli errori prima di procedere" @param String autocomplete = null @param boolean novalidate = true @param String enctype = null @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param String headingId = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param boolean focusOnError = false @param gg.jte.Content content @param gg.jte.Content header = null @param gg.jte.Content footer = null <%-- Resolve error state: list drives hasError. --%> !{var hasError = errors != null && !errors.isEmpty();} !{var formId = (id != null && !id.isBlank()) ? id : null;} !{var errorId = formId != null ? formId + "-errors" : "form-errors";} @if(header != null)${header}@endif <%-- Error summary: always in DOM for aria-live pre-registration. `hidden` attr gates visibility; aria-live still registered by the browser. --%>
@if(hasError) @if(errorSummaryHeading != null && !errorSummaryHeading.isBlank())

${errorSummaryHeading}

@endif
    @for(java.util.Map err : errors)
  • @if(err.get("fieldId") != null && !err.get("fieldId").isBlank()) ${err.get("message")} @else ${err.get("message")} @endif
    • @endfor
@endif
${content} @if(footer != null)
${footer}
@endif

items.docs: Copies the form partial to your JTE root at lievit/form.jte, reached as @template.lievit.form(id=..., action=..., errors=formErrors, content=@`...`). v-next: pass `errors` (List<Map<String,String>> with fieldId+message keys) for an anchored error summary; set focusOnError=true to have a JS enhancer auto-focus the summary after failed submit. Size and labelWidth propagate via --lv-form-size / --lv-form-label-width CSS vars. Back-compat: old callers using `error` (single string) continue to work unchanged. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: hover-card
items.description: A hover/focus-triggered content-preview popover (WAI-ARIA APG Tooltip pattern). TWO cooperating partials: hover-card-trigger.jte wraps the trigger element; hover-card.jte is the floating panel. The panel content is fully server-rendered (no round-trip on open); hover-card.enhancer.ts owns show/hide timing, hover-grace delay, focus-open path, and Esc dismiss. role="tooltip" + aria-describedby + popover="manual" + CSS Anchor Positioning. No interactive children inside the card. CSP-clean, zero inline script.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui hover-card-trigger partial -- the trigger wrapper for a hover-card panel. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (hover-card.enhancer.ts). This wrapper emits the trigger element plus the ARIA and data-attribute wiring the enhancer needs to show/hide the matching hover-card panel. The trigger wrapper is a separate partial from hover-card.jte because the two may appear in different parts of the page template (e.g. a table row trigger + a card panel rendered once per row), so they cannot be co-located in one template call. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Tooltip pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tooltip/). The trigger wrapper carries aria-describedby pointing at the card panel id -- emitted unconditionally by the server so keyboard/SR users receive the description on focus without requiring JS. The wrapper itself has no role or tabindex; the semantic interaction affordances belong to the trigger content the adopter supplies (an , ` ) --%> @param String cardId @param int delay = 300 @param int closeDelay = 150 @param boolean openOnFocus = true @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param java.util.Map dataAttrs = java.util.Map.of() @param gg.jte.Content content <%-- CSS Anchor Positioning: anchor-name links this wrapper to the card panel's position-anchor. The ident is derived from cardId (server-authored, not user-supplied). --%> !{var anchorName = "--hc-" + cardId;} <%-- SAFE dynamic data-* attributes: each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute. JTE's HTML parser forbids a raw @for in attribute-NAME position, so we build the fragment once here. Keys must be simple identifiers ([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*); a non-identifier key is dropped. --%> !{var dataAttrs_ = new StringOutput();} !{for (var e : dataAttrs.entrySet()) { if (e.getKey() != null && e.getKey().matches("[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*")) { dataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-" + e.getKey() + "=\""); Escape.htmlAttribute(e.getValue() == null ? "" : e.getValue(), dataAttrs_); dataAttrs_.writeContent("\""); } }} !{var dataAttrsMarkup = dataAttrs_.toString();} ${content}

items.docs: Copies two JTE partials and a CSS token file into your project. Call hover-card-trigger.jte around the trigger element and hover-card.jte for the card panel with matching cardId/id values. Install hover-card.enhancer.ts via installHoverCard(runtime) in your main.ts (or wire it through installAllFeatures once the coordinator registers it in index.ts). The card content must contain NO interactive children (links, buttons, form controls) — the APG Tooltip pattern is the a11y foundation and prohibits focusable elements inside the panel. For cards needing clickable children, use the popover component instead.

items.name: icon
items.description: Inline Lucide <svg> by name, sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens. Zero JS, CSP-clean. The root PARTIAL: every partial/wire template that needs a glyph composes it via @template.lievit.icon(name=...).

files.content:
@import static dev.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui icon partial -- renders an inline Lucide <svg> by name.

  DELIVERY DECISION (scientific, issue #428): inline-per-name, NOT an SVG sprite.
  Compared against MUI (font/SVG components), Radix (per-icon SVG components), shadcn
  (lucide-react, per-icon, tree-shaken) and Web Awesome (<wa-icon>, font/registry):
    (1) tree-shakeability -- inline-per-name wins: only the icons vendored into
        registry/icons/ exist in the body map, so an adopter ships exactly what they use.
        A sprite ships the whole <symbol> sheet (or needs a build to assemble a per-page
        subset) and an extra request / external <use href> with its CSP+cross-doc quirks.
    (2) ergonomics for a JTE adopter -- "add an icon" = drop a .svg in registry/icons/ and
        re-run generate-icon-map.mjs. No sheet to maintain, no <use> id registry.
    (3) robustness -- no <use href>, no external ref, CSP-clean, zero JS.
  The sprite's only edge (repeated-icon byte savings) is marginal in a gestionale and costs
  the copy-in simplicity, so inline-per-name is objectively best here.

  The uniform <svg> wrapper lives HERE once (sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens); the per-icon
  inner body (paths/circles/...) comes from dev.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body(name) -- a lievit-OWNED
  facade over an IconResolver SPI, defaulting to the bundled, tree-shaken Lucide set
  (LucideIconResolver). The partial names NO adopter class, so it renders standalone out of the box.
  To ship a different icon set, implement dev.lievit.ui.IconResolver and call
  LievitIcons.setResolver(...) once at startup; this call site never changes. The Lucide SVGs
  stroke with currentColor, so colour follows the surrounding text colour or any Tailwind `text-*`
  utility / --lv-color-* override on a wrapping element.

  Usage:
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-down")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "trash-2", cssClass = "text-[var(--lv-color-danger)]")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "search", size = "1.25rem")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "settings", label = "Open settings")   // labelled (img role)

  By default the icon is decorative: aria-hidden="true" + focusable="false". Pass a `label`
  to expose it to assistive tech (role="img" + aria-label); then it is NOT aria-hidden.

  Add an icon: drop registry/icons/<name>.svg (any Lucide SVG), run
  `node registry/icons/generate-icon-map.mjs`, commit the regenerated map.
--%>
@param String name
@param String size = "var(--lv-icon-size)"
@param String cssClass = ""
@param String label = null
<svg
  xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  viewBox="0 0 24 24"
  width="${size}"
  height="${size}"
  fill="none"
  stroke="currentColor"
  stroke-width="var(--lv-icon-stroke)"
  stroke-linecap="round"
  stroke-linejoin="round"
  class="lv-icon ${cssClass}"
  role="${label == null ? null : "img"}"
  aria-hidden="${label == null ? "true" : null}"
  aria-label="${label}"
  focusable="false"
  data-lucide="${name}"
>$unsafe{body(name)}</svg>

items.docs: Copies the icon partial to your JTE root at lievit/icon.jte, reached as @template.lievit.icon(name=...). It renders standalone out of the box: the partial imports dev.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body(name), a lievit-OWNED facade over an IconResolver SPI defaulting to the bundled, tree-shaken Lucide set (LucideIconResolver) -- all three Java files (IconResolver, LucideIconResolver, LievitIcons) are copied to your dev/lievit/ui/ source root, so NO adopter class is needed. Add an icon: drop registry/icons/<name>.svg and re-run registry/icons/generate-icon-map.mjs (regenerates LucideIconResolver). Ship a different icon set: implement dev.lievit.ui.IconResolver and call LievitIcons.setResolver(...) once at startup; the partial's @import never changes. The source is yours: edit the package/import freely.

items.name: icons
items.description: Lucide icon body map for Lit islands: `iconBody(name)` returns the inner SVG markup for a vendored Lucide icon, inlined into the component's own <svg> wrapper and tinted via --lv-* tokens + currentColor. A registry dependency, not a standalone component.
items.docs: Internal helper; pulled in automatically as a dependency of any lievit-ui component that renders an inline Lucide icon (e.g. calendar, carousel). Import { iconBody } and inline unsafeSVG(iconBody(name)) inside an <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24"> wrapper.

items.name: infolist-entry
items.description: A single labelled key-value row (<dt>+<dd>) used inside a description list (<dl>). Renders a read-only fact with a label and a value region — the infolist / descriptions pattern for a record detail view (name, status, address, date). Five variants (default/highlight/destructive/success/warning) control the value ink + tinted background. Three sizes (sm/md/lg) control label font, value font, and block padding-y. Orientation: vertical (label stacked above value) or horizontal (label column left, responsive collapse). Bordered mode adds a --lv-color-border cell ring. Colon after label via CSS ::after (not in DOM; screen-reader safe). Colspan emitted as data-colspan for the parent CSS grid. Optional leading slot for an icon or avatar in <dt>. Optional value slot (gg.jte.Content) for any server-rendered markup; absent -> empty fallback string. Loading state: skeleton pulse + aria-busy. Copyable: native <button> wired via data-lievit-action='copy' runtime directive (Clipboard API, CSP-clean). Safe escaping via two channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, static strings) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map, Escape.htmlAttribute). v-next re-forge of the kit-facing display row: replaces the old type-switch (color/icon/image/code) with the spec-conformant label+value+slot API.
items.docs: Copies the infolist-entry partial to your JTE root at lievit/infolist-entry.jte, reached as @template.lievit.infolist-entry(label="...", value=...). Place it inside a <dl> container; the partial renders one <dt>+<dd> pair per call. Pick a variant (default/highlight/destructive/success/warning) to colour the value region. Use the leading slot for an icon or avatar in the label. Pass value=null or omit it to show the empty fallback (default '—', overridable via the empty param). Set loading=true for a skeleton pulse while the parent WIRE fetches. Set copyable=true for a runtime-wired copy-to-clipboard button (uses the native Clipboard API via data-lievit-action='copy'). Set colspan=N for entries that span multiple grid columns in a CSS-grid <dl>. Set orientation='horizontal' for a side-by-side label+value layout (label column width controlled by --lv-infolist-label-w, default 8rem). Extra attributes via two channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw $unsafe, static author-typed strings only) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map<String,String> of dynamic data-* attributes, each value HTML-escaped). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: input
items.description: Foundational single-line text input partial. A native <input> inside a styled flex-row container that supports leading/trailing adornment slots, optional clearable button, optional live character-count badge, four semantic states (default/focused/invalid/disabled), three toolbar-aligned sizes (sm/md/lg), and a borderless layout variant for inline-edit surfaces. The native element supplies role=textbox, keyboard, and focus for free; no JS is needed unless showCount or clearable are enabled (input-count.enhancer.ts). Binds via l:model or a plain form name; server-renders the current value into value='' with no client round-trip.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input partial -- the foundational single-line text input control. TIER: PARTIAL (+ENH for showCount/clearable). Pure presentation: the native owns role=textbox, keyboard interaction, focus management, and form binding for free. The only irreducible client behaviour (live count, clearable button visibility) is handled by input-count.enhancer.ts; all other wiring (l:model, l:keydown.enter) passes through attrs. Server-first works trivially: the server renders the current value into value="${...}" and the user sees it immediately with no client round-trip. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - WAI-ARIA 1.2 textbox role: https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-1.2/#textbox (native element mapping; no APG pattern page for textbox -- the platform supplies role, keyboard, and focus). - APG "Providing Accessible Names and Descriptions": https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/names-and-descriptions/ (labeling rule). - Inventory: Ant Design Input (prefix/suffix adornments, clearable, character count, status, size, borderless); the search-compound composes this partial inside input-group. - Styling: ORIGINAL generation over --lv-* (OKLCH) tokens; look inspired by Tailwind UI. - The outer container is a flex row; the native flexes to fill; leading/trailing zones receive adornment slots. The container carries :focus-within for the focus ring so the ring wraps the entire compound control, not just the bare input. A11y (WAI-ARIA 1.2 textbox, platform-native): - The native implicitly carries role=textbox (+ specialised roles for type=search etc.); do NOT add a redundant role="textbox" attribute. - aria-invalid="true" when invalid=true. - aria-readonly="true" when readonly=true (native readonly also present). - aria-required="true" when required=true (native required also present). - aria-disabled NOT emitted: native disabled already removes the element from the tab order and the accessibility tree's interactive node set. - aria-placeholder NOT emitted: the native placeholder maps to aria-placeholder automatically. - Labeling: a visible

items.docs: Copies input.jte + input-count.enhancer.ts to your JTE root at lievit/. Reached as @template.lievit.input(...). The native <input> POSTs under name= with zero JS; bind to a wire field with attrs='l:model="field"'. size= (sm/md/lg) is height-based so the input lines up flush with a button or native-select of the same size in a toolbar row. The enhancer (input-count.enhancer.ts) is optional: fields without showCount or clearable need no JS. Call enhanceAllInputs() on DOMContentLoaded and after WIRE morphs.

items.name: input-group
items.description: A fused-border group wrapper that composes one CORE form control (input, native-select, or any element) with optional leading/trailing addons and peer elements into one visually cohesive unit. The group is purely presentational: layout shell + shared border + focus-within ring; all state lives in the inner controls. Slots: content (required, the core control), leadingElement / trailingElement (full-peer controls with shared border), leadingAddon / trailingAddon (decorative or lightweight-interactive pieces on muted background). size is height-based (sm/md/lg) so the group aligns flush with buttons and inputs in a toolbar row. role="group" is emitted only when ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy is set (WAI-ARIA grouping rule). Invalid state: aria-invalid + destructive ring. No enhancer, no client state.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input-group partial -- a fused-border group wrapper that composes one CORE form control (input, native-select, or any element) with optional leading/trailing addons and peer elements into one visually cohesive unit. The group is PURELY presentational: all state and value management lives in the inner controls; the group provides only the layout shell, the shared border frame, and the focus-within ring. TIER: PARTIAL -- pure server-rendered presentation. No enhancer, no client state, no data-lievit-component. The consuming template places the core control in the `content` slot and optional addons/elements in the four flanking slots; the server renders the HTML in one pass. This is the simplest non-trivial layout primitive in the library. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - WAI-ARIA spec role="group" (https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-1.2/#group): a named group is emitted ONLY when ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy is set -- a gratuitous role="group" without an accessible name is a WAI-ARIA error. When the inner control has its own label (via the surrounding field partial) the root is a plain
(no role). - APG Grouping + Landmark guidance: the "group" role marks a cluster as a named unit; reserve it for compound groups where a shared label genuinely adds information (e.g. "Date range"). - Inventory reference: Ant Design Input.Group + Input affix model (prefix/suffix text, icon prefix/suffix, before/after select addons) -- slot semantics adapted, no code copied. - Styling: ORIGINAL generation over --lv-* tokens; visual look inspired by Tailwind UI Input with addons (fused borders, shared radius, muted addon background) -- no code copied. ELEMENT vs ADDON (the two slot kinds): - leadingElement / trailingElement: a full-peer control (select, button) that shares the group border. Gets its own --lv-color-input background; separated from the core control by a 1px internal divider (border-r / border-l at --lv-color-border). Receives the group's outer border-radius on the outer side (rounded-l-* for leading, rounded-r-* for trailing). - leadingAddon / trailingAddon: a decoration or lightweight interactive piece (text label, icon, small icon-button). Gets --lv-color-muted-bg background, sits inside the group border frame, no own border. Purely decorative addons should have aria-hidden="true" on the addon container OR on the icon partial (the icon partial already emits aria-hidden by default). An addon containing an interactive child (button) must NOT have aria-hidden on the container. INNER CONTROL BORDER STRIPPING: inner and

items.docs: Copies the input-group partial to your JTE root at lievit/input-group.jte, reached as @template.lievit.input-group(...). The `content` slot (required) holds the core form control; `leadingAddon` / `trailingAddon` take decorative pieces (icons, text labels) with a muted background; `leadingElement` / `trailingElement` take full-peer controls (selects, buttons) that share the group border. size (sm/md/lg) is height-based so the group lines up with form controls in a toolbar row. Set ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy when the group has a shared semantic label (triggers role="group"); omit for single-field usage where the inner input has its own label. Set invalid=true for the destructive border + ring treatment.

items.name: input-otp
items.description: A fixed-length one-time-password / PIN entry field (v-next CLIENT-ENHANCER): N native single-character inputs in a role="group" + a hidden name mirror that submits the joined value in a plain POST form (zero wire round-trips mid-entry). Auto-advance / paste-fill / backspace-retreat / roving-tabindex choreography lives in the colocated input-otp.enhancer.ts (CSP-clean, no inline on*). APG Spinbutton group a11y (role=spinbutton per slot, aria-valuenow/text, roving tabindex, role=alert error region). Sizes sm/md/lg; optional separator positions; optional mask toggle; optional lievit:otp-complete CustomEvent or form submit on completion.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input-otp partial -- a fixed-length one-time-password / PIN entry field: a horizontal row of N individual digit slots (each a real single-character input) enclosed in a role="group" container. The assembled value lives in a hidden mirror input (name="...") so the code POSTs with a plain HTML form, zero round-trips mid-entry. The one irreducible client behaviour (auto-advance, paste-fill, backspace-retreat, roving tabindex) lives in the colocated input-otp.enhancer.ts; the server renders the truth every time. TIER: PARTIAL (+ enhancer). The OTP value is NOT server state mid-entry: there is nothing for the server to decide between keystrokes, so no wire round-trip per character. The hidden mirror submits the joined value with the enclosing form (zero JS at POST time). The enhancer runs only for the focus choreography and optional completion callback. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Spinbutton pattern applied to a group of per-slot spinbuttons, following the "Date Picker Spin Buttons" example (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/spinbutton/examples/datepicker-spinbuttons/) as the canonical model for multiple role="spinbutton" elements in a role="group". APG has no dedicated OTP pattern so this is a BUILT implementation. The enhancer owns roving tabindex (only the active slot is tabindex="0"); Tab exits freely (non-modal). Each slot receives real DOM focus -- NOT aria-activedescendant. collection-nav is NOT composed (OTP focus model differs from listbox/menu). A11y (APG Spinbutton + group): - group container: role="group" aria-label from groupLabel; aria-describedby points to the error region when errorMessage is set; aria-disabled when disabled. - each slot input: role="spinbutton", aria-valuenow (numeric mode, the digit or omitted when empty), aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="9" (numeric only), aria-valuetext (the char or "blank"), aria-label per slotLabel template ({n}/{total} substituted), aria-invalid when error. - roving tabindex: slot 0 has tabindex="0" on first render (enhancer manages subsequent roving). - error region: role="alert" id=errorId, always present (empty when no error) so screen readers track it; the assertive live region announces automatically when errorMessage is set. - separators: span[aria-hidden=true] between slots -- decorative, no semantic weight. - autocomplete="one-time-code" on slot 0 enables platform SMS autofill. - mask toggle (optional): button[type=button] with aria-label toggled by masked state. - no focus trap; no collection-nav; Tab exits freely. Slot list param (slots): each element is a Map of String to String with keys: index 0-based slot position (numeric string) position 1-based (for aria-label substitution) slotId stable HTML id: "{name}-slot-{index}" (or caller-supplied id prefix) value single char or empty string (pre-filled from the value param) inputType "text" or "password" when masked inputmode "numeric" (mode=numeric) or "text" (mode=alphanumeric/any) tabIndex "0" for slot 0, "-1" for all others (enhancer takes over after mount) ariaValueNow numeric value 0-9 in numeric mode when filled, else empty ariaValueMin "0" in numeric mode, else empty ariaValueMax "9" in numeric mode, else empty ariaValueText the char value if filled, "blank" if empty ariaLabel substituted slot label e.g. "Digit 1 of 6" Params: String name submit field name; the joined value POSTs under it via mirror. java.util.List> slots per-slot data (see Slot list above). java.util.List separatorAfter 0-based slot indices AFTER which a separator is shown. String groupLabel accessible label for the role="group" container; REQUIRED. String errorId stable id for the role="alert" error region. String errorMessage non-blank = error state; null/blank = no error. boolean hasError true when errorMessage is non-blank. boolean disabled all slots inert. boolean masked slots rendered as type="password". String mode "numeric" | "alphanumeric" | "any" -- drives data-otp-mode. int length number of slots -- drives data-otp-length. boolean autoSubmit fire completeAction on N-th char (true) vs wait for Enter. String value pre-filled assembled value (distributed across slots). String size "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md"). String completeAction optional: "submit" submits the enclosing form; any non-blank value fires a lievit:otp-complete CustomEvent on the root. boolean showMaskToggle when true, renders a mask toggle button after the group. Usage: The slots list is typically built server-side from the length + value + mode + masked params. See the companion InputOtpModel helper or build manually. @@template.lievit.input-otp(name = "otp", slots = slots, groupLabel = "One-time code", errorId = "otp-error", length = 6, mode = "numeric") @@template.lievit.input-otp(name = "pin", slots = slots, groupLabel = "PIN", errorId = "pin-error", length = 4, size = "lg", completeAction = "submit") Import the enhancer once from your entry module: import { enhanceAllInputOtp } from "lievit/input-otp.enhancer"; --%> @param String name @param java.util.List> slots @param java.util.List separatorAfter = java.util.List.of() @param String groupLabel @param String errorId @param String errorMessage = null @param boolean hasError = false @param boolean disabled = false @param boolean masked = false @param String mode = "numeric" @param int length = 6 @param boolean autoSubmit = true @param String value = "" @param String size = "md" @param String completeAction = null @param boolean showMaskToggle = false <%-- Slot dimension class: data-[size=N] attr selectors drive width + height + font. All strings are COMPLETE and static so Tailwind's scanner picks them up. --%> !{var slotDimClass = "data-[size=sm]:w-[var(--lv-space-8)] data-[size=sm]:h-[var(--lv-space-8)] data-[size=sm]:text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)] data-[size=md]:w-[var(--lv-space-9)] data-[size=md]:h-[var(--lv-space-9)] data-[size=md]:text-[length:var(--lv-text-base)] data-[size=lg]:w-[var(--lv-space-10)] data-[size=lg]:h-[var(--lv-space-10)] data-[size=lg]:text-[length:var(--lv-text-lg)]";} <%-- Slot border + ring: destructive ring on error, muted border on disabled, default otherwise. focus-visible uses the standard --lv-ring token (all interactive controls share it). --%> !{var slotBorderClass = hasError ? "border-[var(--lv-color-destructive)] focus-visible:shadow-[var(--lv-ring-destructive)]" : disabled ? "border-[var(--lv-color-muted)]" : "border-[var(--lv-color-border)] focus-visible:border-[var(--lv-color-primary)] focus-visible:shadow-[var(--lv-ring)]";} <%-- aria-describedby: only point to the error region when in error state. Smart attr: null omits. --%> !{var groupDescribedBy = hasError ? errorId : null;} <%-- SAFE-escaped root data-* fragment. Builds one string, emits $unsafe (safe: all values escaped). JTE forbids @for in an attribute NAME position, so we pre-build the fragment in a !{} block. data-otp-mode value is user-typed but locked (only 3 values); still escape for belt-and-braces. --%> !{var rootDataAttrs_ = new StringOutput();} !{var escapedMode_ = new StringOutput();} !{Escape.htmlAttribute(mode, escapedMode_);} !{rootDataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-otp-mode=\"" + escapedMode_ + "\"");} !{rootDataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-otp-length=\"" + length + "\"");} !{rootDataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-otp-autosubmit=\"" + (autoSubmit ? "true" : "false") + "\"");} !{var hasCompleteAction = completeAction != null && !completeAction.isBlank();} !{if (hasCompleteAction) { var escapedComplete_ = new StringOutput(); Escape.htmlAttribute(completeAction, escapedComplete_); rootDataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-otp-complete=\"" + escapedComplete_ + "\""); }} !{var rootDataAttrsMarkup = rootDataAttrs_.toString();} <%-- Component root: data-slot + data-size for test selectors and styling hooks. --%>
<%-- OTP group row: role="group" + the sequence of slot inputs with optional separators. aria-disabled is a smart attribute: emits the attribute when disabled, omitted when false. (Native disabled on each removes slots from the tab order; the group aria-disabled informs AT of the group-level semantics without overriding the native per-slot attribute.) --%>
@for(var slot : slots) !{var slotIndex = Integer.parseInt(slot.get("index"));} <%-- Decorative separator BEFORE this slot when separatorAfter contains the previous index. aria-hidden: separators are purely visual; the slot labels carry positional meaning. --%> @if(separatorAfter.contains(slotIndex - 1)) – @endif <%-- Per-slot spinbutton input. Smart attributes: JTE omits the attribute when the value is null (the canonical way to conditionally emit an attribute -- never split an element across @if/@else for this). aria-valuenow / aria-valuemin / aria-valuemax: non-blank in numeric mode only. disabled: boolean smart attr emits the bare attribute when true, omits when false. tabindex: string from the slot map ("0" for slot 0, "-1" for all others). --%> !{var ariaValueNow_ = slot.get("ariaValueNow");} !{var ariaValueMin_ = slot.get("ariaValueMin");} !{var ariaValueMax_ = slot.get("ariaValueMax");} !{var ariaValueNowAttr = (ariaValueNow_ != null && !ariaValueNow_.isBlank()) ? ariaValueNow_ : null;} !{var ariaValueMinAttr = (ariaValueMin_ != null && !ariaValueMin_.isBlank()) ? ariaValueMin_ : null;} !{var ariaValueMaxAttr = (ariaValueMax_ != null && !ariaValueMax_.isBlank()) ? ariaValueMax_ : null;} @endfor
<%-- Mask toggle button (optional): only rendered when showMaskToggle=true. Icon-only button: aria-label is mandatory (no visible text); toggled by masked state. type="button" prevents form submission. The masked state is driven by the caller -- the caller re-renders the template (e.g. via htmx swap or a server round-trip) with the new masked value, not by the enhancer (the enhancer never manages input type). --%> @if(showMaskToggle) @endif <%-- Error region: role="alert" always present (empty when no error). role="alert" implies aria-live="assertive" + aria-atomic="true" per ARIA spec. The node must pre-exist in the DOM so AT tracks it from page load; populating it dynamically (via a server swap) fires the assertive announcement without navigation. --%>
${hasError ? errorMessage : ""}
<%-- Hidden value mirror: submits the assembled value with the enclosing form under `name`. The enhancer keeps this in sync on every keystroke / paste so FormData sees the joined value at submit time. aria-hidden + tabindex="-1" keep it out of the a11y tree + tab order. --%>

params.name: name
params.description: Submit field name; the joined value POSTs under it via the hidden mirror.

params.name: slots
params.description: Per-slot data list; each map has keys: index, position, slotId, value, inputType, inputmode, tabIndex, ariaValueNow, ariaValueMin, ariaValueMax, ariaValueText, ariaLabel.

params.name: groupLabel
params.description: Accessible label for the role=group container; REQUIRED (no default; a missing label is an a11y violation).

params.name: errorId
params.description: Stable id for the role=alert error region.

params.name: separatorAfter
params.description: 0-based slot indices AFTER which a decorative separator is rendered.

params.name: errorMessage
params.description: Non-blank = error state; null/blank = no error.

params.name: hasError
params.description: True when errorMessage is non-blank.

params.name: disabled
params.description: All slots inert; aria-disabled on group.

params.name: masked
params.description: Slots rendered as type=password.

params.name: mode
params.description: "numeric" | "alphanumeric" | "any" -- drives data-otp-mode and client-side validation.

params.name: length
params.description: Number of slots (N); drives data-otp-length.

params.name: autoSubmit
params.description: Fire completeAction on N-th char (true) vs wait for Enter (false).

params.name: value
params.description: Pre-filled assembled value distributed across slots (mirrors the server-side field value).

params.name: size
params.description: "sm" | "md" | "lg" -- slot height+width (32/36/40px, toolbar-aligned).

params.name: completeAction
params.description: Optional completion behaviour: "submit" also requestSubmit()-s the enclosing form; any non-blank fires a lievit:otp-complete CustomEvent. Null = plain form POST only.

params.name: showMaskToggle
params.description: When true, renders a mask toggle button (eye/eye-off) after the group row.

events.name: lievit:otp-complete
events.fired: when all N slots are filled and data-otp-complete is set

a11y.pattern: APG Spinbutton + group (BUILT, no dedicated OTP APG pattern)

items.docs: Copy input-otp.jte to your JTE root at lievit/input-otp.jte (called as @template.lievit.input-otp(...)) and the CSP-clean enhancer to your alias root at lievit/input-otp.enhancer.ts. Build a slots List<Map<String,String>> server-side (or use the companion InputOtpModel helper). Call enhanceAllInputOtp() on load and after any DOM swap. The assembled code POSTs under `name` via the hidden mirror at form submit; the completeAction param optionally fires a lievit:otp-complete event or auto-submits the form when all N slots are filled.

items.name: item
items.description: A composable list/menu row (v-next). The core item exposes leading (media) / content / trailing (actions) slots; element param selects the root tag (li|dd|dt|div); variant (default|active|danger|muted) and size (sm|md|lg) drive token-based colour + height; disabled dims the row. Extra attributes via trusted `attrs` + safe-escaped `dataAttrs`. The typed child partials mirror shadcn ItemTitle / ItemDescription / ItemHeader / ItemFooter / ItemGroup / ItemSeparator / ItemMedia. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui item/title partial -- shadcn's ItemTitle: the typed title sub-part of an item row. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout + typography, no client state. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn ships ItemTitle as a distinct data-slot="item-title" rather than a free content slot so the media variant CSS ("translate the media when a description is present") and the title typography are addressable by slot. We mirror the exact data-slot so those sibling-aware rules keep working. Sits inside the item's content column. A11y: a plain styled
; when the item is actionable the link/button lives elsewhere in the row and keeps its own semantics. The title text is content, not a control. Params: gg.jte.Content content the title text/markup. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the title. Usage: @@template.lievit.item(content = @@` @@template.lievit.item.title(content = @@`Contratti`) @@template.lievit.item.description(content = @@`24 documenti`) `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui item/description partial -- shadcn's ItemDescription: the typed muted secondary line of an item row. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout + typography, no client state. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn ships ItemDescription as data-slot="item-description" on a

(not a free content slot) precisely so the media variant can react to its presence (group-has-[[data-slot=item-description]]). We mirror the exact slot + the

element + the muted colour + the inline-link affordances (underline, primary on hover). line-clamp-2 keeps it to two lines like shadcn. A11y: a paragraph of secondary text; any inline keeps its own semantics and gets the underline/primary-on-hover cue. Params: gg.jte.Content content the description text/markup. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the description. Usage: @@template.lievit.item.description(content = @@`24 documenti`) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""

${content}
