@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui item/header partial -- shadcn's ItemHeader: a full-width band at the top of an item row, with content pushed to the edges (title left, action right). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. STRUCTURE: shadcn's ItemHeader is `flex basis-full items-center justify-between gap-2` so it spans the whole row (basis-full breaks the flex-wrap line) and edge-justifies its children. We reproduce that exactly with tokens for the gap. A11y: a plain layout band; its children carry their own semantics. Params: gg.jte.Content content the header children (e.g. a title slot + an actions slot). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the header. Usage: @@template.lievit.item.header(content = @@` @@template.lievit.item.title(content = @@`Recent files`) @@template.lievit.icon(name = "more-horizontal") `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui item/footer partial -- shadcn's ItemFooter: a full-width band at the bottom of an item row, with content pushed to the edges. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. STRUCTURE: shadcn's ItemFooter is `flex basis-full items-center justify-between gap-2` (the same shape as ItemHeader but read as the closing band). basis-full breaks the flex-wrap line so it spans the whole row under the content/actions. Reproduced verbatim with tokens for the gap. A11y: a plain layout band; its children carry their own semantics. Params: gg.jte.Content content the footer children. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the footer. Usage: @@template.lievit.item.footer(content = @@` Updated 2 days ago @@template.lievit.badge(label = "draft") `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui item/group partial -- shadcn's ItemGroup: the list wrapper that holds a set of item rows (and item separators). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. STRUCTURE: shadcn's ItemGroup is `role="list"` + `group/item-group flex flex-col`. The role makes the wrapped rows a real list for assistive tech (each child item should carry role="listitem"). We reproduce the role + the flex column exactly. A11y (WAI-ARIA list): role="list" names the container as a list; pass role="listitem" on each @@template.lievit.item inside so the count and position are announced. Without children rows carrying listitem, the list is empty to AT, so always pair them. Params: gg.jte.Content content the item rows (and item separators) in this group. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the group. Usage: @@template.lievit.item.group(content = @@` @@template.lievit.item(role = "listitem", content = @@`@@template.lievit.item.title(content = @@`A`)`) @@template.lievit.item.separator() @@template.lievit.item(role = "listitem", content = @@`@@template.lievit.item.title(content = @@`B`)`) `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui item/separator partial -- shadcn's ItemSeparator: a horizontal divider between item rows in an item group. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's ItemSeparator wraps the shared Separator (horizontal, decorative) and stamps data-slot="item-separator". We do the same shape inline (a role-less, aria-hidden token rule) rather than @@template.lievit.separator so the item-separator slot is the element itself, matching shadcn's addressable slot. A separator between rows is decorative (the list semantics already convey grouping), so it stays out of the a11y tree. A11y (WAI-ARIA): decorative divider -> aria-hidden, no role; the item-group's role="list" carries the grouping semantics. Params: String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the divider. Usage: @@template.lievit.item.separator() --%> @param String cssClass = ""

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui item/media partial -- shadcn's ItemMedia: the leading media region of an item row, with the three shadcn variants (default | icon | image). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the core item.jte renders an inline item-media wrapper for the simple case. This partial is the TYPED form: it carries data-variant so the icon/image chrome matches shadcn exactly: - default: transparent, just centres the slot (an inline icon/index). - icon: a size-8 rounded bordered muted tile around a centred svg (svg sized to 4 when unstyled). - image: a size-10 rounded overflow-clipped tile whose fills + object-covers. The base also self-starts + nudges down when the row has a description, the same sibling-aware rule shadcn ships. Use it inside the item's `leading` slot. A11y: media images MUST carry their own alt; decorative icons come from the Lucide partial (aria-hidden by default). The media tile itself is presentational. Params: gg.jte.Content content the icon / image / index markup. String variant = "default" default | icon | image. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the media tile. Usage: @@template.lievit.item(leading = @@` @@template.lievit.item.media(variant = "icon", content = @@`@@template.lievit.icon(name = "folder")`) `, content = @@`...`) @@template.lievit.item.media(variant = "image", content = @@``) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String variant = "default" @param String cssClass = "" !{var variantClass = switch (variant) { case "icon" -> "size-8 rounded-[var(--lv-radius-sm)] border border-[var(--lv-color-border)] bg-[var(--lv-color-muted-bg)] [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4"; case "image" -> "size-10 overflow-hidden rounded-[var(--lv-radius-sm)] [&_img]:size-full [&_img]:object-cover"; default -> "bg-transparent"; };}
${content}

items.docs: Copies the item partial (+ its item/title, item/description, item/header, item/footer, item/group, item/separator and item/media children) to your JTE root at lievit/item.jte, reached as @template.lievit.item(content=@`...`). v-next: element selects the root tag (li|dd|dt|div); variant (default|active|danger|muted); size (sm|md|lg); disabled dims the row. dataAttrs carries safe-escaped per-row data. Compose the content column from item/title + item/description (typed sub-parts); span the row with item/header / item/footer; wrap a list of rows in item/group (role=list, give each item element="li") and divide them with item/separator. Leading icon/image tile via item/media. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: kbd
items.description: A keyboard-key badge (v-next): the small recessed pill that shows a shortcut key (Ctrl, Cmd, Esc) or a chord (Ctrl+K). Takes a List<String> of key names; a single-entry list renders one <kbd>; a multi-entry list renders an outer <kbd> wrapping inner <kbd> elements with a separator. Size scale (sm|md|lg); ariaLabel smart attr; monospace font via --lv-font-mono. Renders the semantic <kbd> element (screen-reader-correct), token-styled with muted fill + 1px border. Server-first PARTIAL, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui kbd partial (v-next) -- a keyboard-key badge: the small recessed pill that shows a shortcut key (Ctrl, Cmd, Esc) or a chord (Ctrl+K). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure presentation, no client state. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): renders the semantic element (the screen-reader-correct element for keyboard input). Single key: one containing the key text. Chord (keys.size() > 1): outer wrapping one inner per key, separated by a Unicode separator so the chord reads as individual keys to AT. A11y: the native element is the correct semantic for keyboard input (HTML spec). pointer-events-none / select-none keep it from being selected as text, matching shadcn. ariaLabel on the outer element (smart attr: omitted when null) supports screen-reader overrides when the caller wants a bespoke announcement. TOKEN decision: bg from --lv-color-muted-bg, text from --lv-color-muted-fg (the same pair avatar/empty use), border from --lv-color-border (the 1px recessed "key" look), shadow from --lv-shadow-xs (slight depth), font from --lv-font-mono (monospace convention, confirmed present at tokens.css line 198). SIZE scale (text + padding, not height -- is inline): sm text-xs px-space-2 py-space-1 md text-sm px-space-2 py-space-1 (default) lg text-base px-space-3 py-space-1 Params: java.util.List keys REQUIRED: ordered list of key names (["Ctrl", "K"]). String size = "md" "sm" | "md" | "lg". String ariaLabel = null aria-label on the outer (omitted when null). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the outer . String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC strings only. Usage: @@template.lievit.kbd(keys = java.util.List.of("Esc")) @@template.lievit.kbd(keys = java.util.List.of("Ctrl", "K")) @@template.lievit.kbd(keys = java.util.List.of("Cmd", "Shift", "P"), size = "sm") @@template.lievit.kbd(keys = java.util.List.of("Enter"), ariaLabel = "Premi Invio per confermare") --%> @param java.util.List keys @param String size = "md" @param String ariaLabel = null @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" !{var sizeClass = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-xs)] px-[var(--lv-space-2)] py-[var(--lv-space-1)]"; case "lg" -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-base)] px-[var(--lv-space-3)] py-[var(--lv-space-1)]"; default -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)] px-[var(--lv-space-2)] py-[var(--lv-space-1)]"; };} !{var base = "inline-flex items-center select-none rounded-[var(--lv-radius-sm)] border border-[var(--lv-color-border)] bg-[var(--lv-color-muted-bg)] text-[var(--lv-color-muted-fg)] font-[var(--lv-font-mono)] shadow-[var(--lv-shadow-xs)]";} !{var innerBase = "inline-flex items-center";} !{boolean isChord = keys.size() > 1;} @if(isChord) @for(int i = 0; i < keys.size(); i++)@if(i > 0) @endif${keys.get(i)}@endfor @else ${keys.isEmpty() ? "" : keys.get(0)} @endif

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui kbd/group partial -- shadcn new-york-v4 KbdGroup (data-slot="kbd-group"): the inline
  wrapper that holds several <kbd> keys as one shortcut, e.g. "Ctrl + K" or the "Then" sequence.

  TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout (an inline flex row with a gap), no
  client state; server-rendered, zero JS, CSP-clean.

  STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's KbdGroup is `inline-flex items-center gap-1`; the
  keys (and any "+"/"then" connector text) are the children. We reproduce that exactly with the
  token gap, and the content slot carries the @@template.lievit.kbd calls + connectors.

  A11y: a plain grouping wrapper; each inner <kbd> is the screen-reader-correct element for the
  key. The visible "+"/"then" connectors are content, announced as text.

  Params:
    gg.jte.Content content      the <kbd> keys and any connector text (e.g. "+").
    String cssClass = ""        extra utility classes on the group.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.kbd.group(content = @@`
      @@template.lievit.kbd(key = "Ctrl") + @@template.lievit.kbd(key = "K")
    `)
--%>
@param gg.jte.Content content
@param String cssClass = ""
<kbd
  data-slot="kbd-group"
  class="inline-flex items-center gap-[var(--lv-space-1)] ${cssClass}"
  style="font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);"
>${content}</kbd>

Size: sm|md|lg. ariaLabel overrides the AT announcement when needed. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: key-value
items.description: A read-only two-column table of key -> value pairs, the detail-page primitive Filament's KeyValueEntry needs (a resolved map shown as a small ruled table inside an infolist). bordered (default true) adds card chrome; striped alternates a surface tint on even rows; emptyMessage renders a full-width cell when the map is empty. th headers carry scope='col' for a11y; no redundant role='table' (native implicit role). Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import java.util.Map <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui key-value partial -- a read-only two-column table of key -> value pairs, the detail-page primitive Filament's KeyValueEntry needs (a map shown as a small ruled table inside an infolist). The read-only twin of a form's key-value REPEATER: here the pairs are already resolved (the kit's KeyValueEntry.resolveMap output), the template only iterates them. TIER (planning two-tier model): STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout over an already-projected ordered map; no client state, no lifecycle => not a Lit island, not a wire component. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the semantic shape for a labelled key/value grid is a real with a (the keyLabel / valueLabel headers) + a of . shadcn has no dedicated key-value export (its examples compose a bordered table); Filament's KeyValueEntry renders exactly this two-column ruled table, so we ship it as the primitive. NOT description-list (that is a dt/dd grid for a record's fields, not a homogeneous map with column headers); NOT data-table (no sorting / selection / pagination). A native
key value
carries the implicit table role (WAI-ARIA 1.2 §4.c prohibits a redundant prohibits duplicating the implicit ARIA role of a host-language semantic element). TOKEN FIDELITY: the border reads --lv-color-border, the radius --lv-radius-md, headers read --lv-color-muted-fg, cell text --lv-color-fg, padding the space scale; a retheme retints the whole table without a markup edit. bordered: wraps the table in a card chrome (border + radius + overflow:hidden) when true (default). striped: alternates a surface tint on even rows via --lv-color-surface for readability on long maps. emptyMessage: when set, renders a full-width cell when the pairs map is empty instead of an empty tbody. When null, the tbody is left empty (no row rendered, only the header). A11y: native
with labels each column via scope="col" on each
; the table is read-only content, not a control, so no extra ARIA is needed. Params: pairs Map the ordered key -> value pairs (already projected to strings). keyLabel String = "Key" the key-column header. valueLabel String = "Value" the value-column header. boolean bordered = true wrap the table in bordered card chrome (border + radius). boolean striped = false tint even rows with --lv-color-surface for readability. String emptyMessage = null message when pairs is empty; null => empty tbody. cssClass String = "" extra utility classes on the table root. Usage: @@template.lievit.key-value(pairs = pairs, keyLabel = "Field", valueLabel = "Value") @@template.lievit.key-value(pairs = pairs, striped = true, emptyMessage = "No entries yet.") --%> @param Map pairs @param String keyLabel = "Key" @param String valueLabel = "Value" @param boolean bordered = true @param boolean striped = false @param String emptyMessage = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var borderedClass = bordered ? "border border-[var(--lv-color-border)] rounded-[var(--lv-radius-md)] overflow-hidden" : "";} @if(pairs.isEmpty()) @if(emptyMessage != null) @endif @else !{int[] _idx = {0};} @for(Map.Entry pair : pairs.entrySet()) !{_idx[0]++;} !{var rowClass = (striped && _idx[0] % 2 == 0) ? "bg-[var(--lv-color-surface)]" : "";} @endfor @endif
${keyLabel} ${valueLabel}
${emptyMessage}
${pair.getKey()} ${pair.getValue()}

items.name: key-value-input
items.description: An EDITABLE repeatable key/value FORM control (Filament's KeyValue field): rows of two text inputs (key + value) with add / remove. NOT the read-only `key-value` display primitive (a static dl/dt/dd) -- this is the editable form counterpart, named key-value-INPUT to avoid the collision. Each row is two native inputs named `<name>[<i>][key]` / `<name>[<i>][value]` (the canonical form-array convention a Spring binder maps to a List/Map), so the rows POST natively with zero JS. JS-OFF an Add submit (name=`<name>__add`) and a per-row remove submit (name=`<name>__remove`, value=index) round-trip to the server, which re-renders -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer clones a hidden <template> row to add and drops a row to remove, in place, then re-indexes the survivors so the POSTed indices stay contiguous. The native inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: aria-labelled inputs (role + row number), labelled add / remove buttons, aria-live announcements. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui key-value-input partial -- an EDITABLE repeatable key/value FORM control: rows of two text inputs (key + value) with add / remove (Filament's KeyValue field). The rows POST as INDEXED form-array fields so the server can rebuild an ordered map. NOT the read-only `key-value` display primitive (that one renders a static dl/dt/dd). This is the editable FORM counterpart; it is named key-value-INPUT to avoid the collision. TIER: PARTIAL (+ optional enhancer, ADR-0012 server-first). Each row is two native text inputs named `[][key]` / `[][value]` (the canonical form-array convention a Java/Spring binder maps to a List or Map). So the rows POST natively with ZERO JavaScript. JS-OFF, add is an explicit "Add row" submit (name=`__add`) and remove is a per-row submit (name=`__remove`, value=index) that round-trip to the server, which re-renders with one more / fewer row -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON the CSP-clean enhancer clones a hidden

items.docs: Copies the key-value-input partial to your JTE root at lievit/key-value-input.jte (reached as @template.lievit.key-value-input(name=..., entries=...)) and the CSP-clean enhancer to lievit/key-value-input.enhancer.ts. Import the enhancer once and call enhanceAllKeyValueInputs() on load (and after a DOM swap). The rows POST as `<name>[<i>][key]` / `<name>[<i>][value]`; bind server-side to a List of pairs or a Map. JS-OFF the user adds via the Add submit (`<name>__add`) and removes via the per-row submit (`<name>__remove`, value=index), both server round-trips. JS-ON the enhancer adds / removes in place (cloning the hidden <template> row) and re-indexes so the indices stay contiguous. Pass `entries` (an insertion-ordered Map), `keyLabel` / `valueLabel`, `keyPlaceholder` / `valuePlaceholder`, `addLabel`, plus `label` / `disabled`. Requires the icon partial (trash-2 + plus). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: label
items.description: A native <label for> partial with optional required marker (aria-hidden * + sr-only text), optional tag, hint icon (composed via tooltip partial), leading/trailing slots, size (sm/md/lg), variant (default/bold typographic), and visually-hidden (sr-only) mode. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012): a label is pure, deterministic markup; the platform supplies click-to-focus and accessible-name binding for free. Replaces the old label.jte (which had text/error params). Composes tooltip for the hint icon. Extra attributes via two escaping channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, $unsafe, STATIC author strings only) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map<String,String>, each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute).

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui label partial -- a native

items.docs: Copies the label partial to your JTE root at lievit/label.jte, reached as @template.lievit.label(forId="email", content=@`Email`). The content slot is MANDATORY. Set forId to the control's id for an explicit FOR/ID association; omit (null) for an implicit association when the control is a child of the label. required=true renders an aria-hidden * glyph in --lv-color-destructive + a sr-only " (required)" sibling that joins the label's accessible name. optional=true renders a visible "(optional)" tag in --lv-color-muted-fg (mutually exclusive with required; required wins). hint="..." + hintId="..." composes the tooltip partial around a circle-help icon; the consuming field partial wires aria-describedby=hintId on the control. size is sm/md/lg (text size + line-height matching the associated control's tier). variant is default (--lv-font-medium) or bold (--lv-font-semibold). hidden=true applies the sr-only class so the label stays in the accessibility tree (never display:none). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: link
items.description: A standalone styled anchor (v-next): the navigation affordance (distinct from button's action affordance). Always renders <a>; when disabled, href is smart-dropped and aria-disabled is set. external=true adds target=_blank + rel=noopener noreferrer + sr-only hint + inline Unicode glyph (no icon partial dependency). Variant (default|muted|destructive|ghost) drives colour via tokens; size (sm|md|lg|inherit) drives font size. Extra attributes via trusted `attrs` + safe-escaped `dataAttrs`. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012), zero JS, CSP-clean.

items.name: loading-section
items.description: Unified loading state component: three modes (spinner/skeleton/section) in one partial. spinner: SVG ring for action feedback. skeleton: shimmer layout placeholder. section: real content slot with overlay spinner for blocking async ops. role=status/region, aria-live, fullPage overlay, showTip, CSP-clean. Zero JS.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui loading-section partial -- unified loading state component. Three modes in one partial, selected via the `mode` param: spinner -- an animated SVG ring: use for action feedback (saving, submitting). skeleton -- a shimmer layout placeholder: use while fetching a whole content region. section -- a real content slot overlaid by a spinner when active: use for regions that show existing content but need to block interaction during an async op. TIER: PARTIAL presentational -> JTE partial (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). Pure display, no client state; the server picks the mode and the morph swaps it out once done. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): Filament v3 ships three sibling components (Loading Section, Skeleton, Spinner) with overlapping purposes and a composition hierarchy. lievit unifies them under one partial with a mode switch so the call site declares intent rather than composing primitives manually. Spinner and skeleton are also available as standalone partials (spinner.jte, skeleton.jte) for inline / narrow-context use. SELF-CONTAINED (no cross-partial deps): the spinner SVG ring and skeleton bars are inlined directly in each mode branch. This keeps loading-section.jte deployable as a single file and avoids the circular-reference risk that would arise if the section overlay called the spinner partial recursively. The standalone spinner.jte/skeleton.jte partials are equivalent; changes to the SVG ring anatomy must be mirrored in both files (intentional duplication, not drift). SPINNER SVG anatomy: background track circle (opacity 0.25) + foreground arc. Circumference = 2 * pi * 10 = 62.832; dasharray = 31.416 (half circumference); dashoffset = 10.472 (1/6 of circumference), giving a ~300-deg visible arc. viewBox="0 0 24 24" fixed; width/height attributes drive the rendered size. SKELETON shimmer: controlled by lv-skeleton--active class + skeleton.css. SECTION overlay: an aria-hidden div positioned absolute (over the content slot via relative container) showing an inlined SVG spinner ring. The content slot is the real page content; the overlay is removed when the op completes (htmx swap / morph). A11y: spinner/skeleton modes: role="status" + aria-live="polite" + aria-atomic="true" on root. section mode: root is role="region" (not a live region -- the content owns announcements); the overlay carries aria-hidden="true" so screen readers skip the decorative spinner; aria-busy="true" on the root signals the blocked state to AT. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Roles/status_role https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/status-messages Params: String mode = "spinner" "spinner" | "skeleton" | "section". String size = "md" "sm" | "md" | "lg" -- ring diameter (spinner/overlay) or text-row height (skeleton). NOT used in section mode. String variant = "default" "default" | "primary" -- spinner stroke colour. Skeleton + section modes ignore this param. String label = "Loading..." accessible name (sr-only in spinner/skeleton; visible tip in section mode when showTip=true). boolean showTip = false section mode: render label as a visible tip text line. boolean active = true skeleton mode: shimmer animation active. int skeletonRows = 3 skeleton mode, skeletonShape=lines: number of text rows. String skeletonShape = "lines" skeleton mode: "lines"|"card"|"avatar-row"|"image"|"button-bar". boolean fullPage = false section mode: fixed viewport overlay (blocks entire page). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root element. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only. Map dataAttrs = {} SAFE dynamic data-* (value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute). Content content = null section mode only: the real content slot. Usage: @@template.lievit.loading-section() @@template.lievit.loading-section(mode = "skeleton", skeletonShape = "avatar-row") @@template.lievit.loading-section(mode = "section", label = "Saving", showTip = true) { } @@template.lievit.loading-section(mode = "spinner", size = "lg", variant = "primary") --%> @param String mode = "spinner" @param String size = "md" @param String variant = "default" @param String label = "Loading…" @param boolean showTip = false @param boolean active = true @param int skeletonRows = 3 @param String skeletonShape = "lines" @param boolean fullPage = false @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param java.util.Map dataAttrs = java.util.Map.of() @param Content content = null !{var dataAttrs_ = new StringOutput();} !{for (var e : dataAttrs.entrySet()) { if (e.getKey() != null && e.getKey().matches("[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*")) { dataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-" + e.getKey() + "=\""); Escape.htmlAttribute(e.getValue() == null ? "" : e.getValue(), dataAttrs_); dataAttrs_.writeContent("\""); } }} !{var dataAttrsMarkup = dataAttrs_.toString();} <%-- ======================================================================== SPINNER MODE ======================================================================== --%> @if("spinner".equals(mode)) !{var svgSize = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "16"; case "lg" -> "40"; default -> "24"; };} !{var strokeColor = "primary".equals(variant) ? "var(--lv-color-primary)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} ${label} <%-- ======================================================================== SKELETON MODE ======================================================================== --%> @elseif("skeleton".equals(mode)) !{var activeClass = active ? "lv-skeleton--active" : "";} !{var lineHeights = java.util.Map.of("sm", "0.75rem", "md", "1rem", "lg", "1.25rem");} !{var lineH = lineHeights.getOrDefault(size, "1rem");} <%-- Deterministic width sequence: cycles by row index mod 5 -- no DB data, no RNG. --%> !{var widths = java.util.List.of("100%", "75%", "88%", "62%", "94%");}
${label} @if("lines".equals(skeletonShape))
@for(int i = 0; i < skeletonRows; i++)
@endfor
@elseif("card".equals(skeletonShape))
@elseif("avatar-row".equals(skeletonShape)) !{var avatarSize = "sm".equals(size) ? "2rem" : "lg".equals(size) ? "3rem" : "2.5rem";}
@elseif("image".equals(skeletonShape))
@elseif("button-bar".equals(skeletonShape))
@endif
<%-- ======================================================================== SECTION MODE -- real content slot + overlay spinner ======================================================================== --%> @else !{var overlaySvgSize = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "16"; case "lg" -> "40"; default -> "24"; };} !{var overlayStrokeColor = "primary".equals(variant) ? "var(--lv-color-primary)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} !{var positionStyle = fullPage ? "position:fixed;inset:0;z-index:var(--lv-z-overlay);" : "position:relative;";}
@if(content != null) ${content} @endif <%-- Overlay: aria-hidden -- purely visual; AT sees only the aria-busy on the root. --%>
<%-- Inlined SVG ring (no cross-partial call; self-contained per inline-trivial-bits rule). --%> @if(showTip) ${label} @endif
@endif

items.name: markdown-editor
items.description: A server-first markdown form field (Filament MarkdownEditor parity): a toolbar + a <textarea name=...> + a preview pane. The canonical control is the plain textarea, which POSTs its markdown with the surrounding <form> JS-off; a CSP-clean island adds the toolbar (wrap the current selection in markdown syntax, the pure transform is unit-tested here) and a LIVE PREVIEW that renders the markdown to SANITIZED HTML on every input. The markdown lib (e.g. marked, no eval) + sanitizer (e.g. DOMPurify) are injected by the adopter so they are pinned downstream and the preview cannot inject script under a strict CSP. Nothing about submission depends on the island.

files.content: @import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui markdown-editor partial (Filament MarkdownEditor parity) -- a SERVER-FIRST markdown form field: a toolbar + a

items.docs: Render the partial with a `name` (the form field). With JS off the <textarea> is the editor and POSTs its markdown. To enhance: `npm i marked dompurify`, then call enhanceMarkdownEditors({ render }) once from main.ts after startLievit(), passing render = (md) => DOMPurify.sanitize(marked.parse(md, { async: false })). SANITIZE is mandatory: the preview sets innerHTML, so the renderer MUST return sanitized HTML (DOMPurify), else markdown raw-HTML passthrough becomes XSS. Without a render, the toolbar still works and the preview toggle is simply not wired. The toolbar commands (bold/italic/code/link/heading/quote/ul) wrap the current textarea selection in markdown syntax (the same edit a user could type); the pure applyCommand transform is unit-tested. On submit you receive the field as raw markdown; render + sanitize again server-side before display. Adjust the template name freely once copied.

items.name: menubar
items.description: Horizontal persistent menu bar of top-level triggers (APG Menubar pattern). UNCONTROLLED: the bar carries no server-owned open state; each top-level trigger is a dropdown-menu whose open/closed lifecycle is browser-owned via the native popover API. The bar root uses collection-nav.enhancer.ts in horizontal roving-tabindex mode (Left/Right Arrow, Home/End, typeahead across top-level triggers). Content slot takes multiple dropdown-menu instances or plain button/link items; each trigger must carry role='menuitem' + data-lievit-item for the collection-nav to discover it. Size scale (sm/md/lg) sets bar block-size to --lv-space-8/9/10. No Lit island, CSP-clean, zero inline script. ENHANCER_NEEDED: Down/Up Arrow on horizontal bar to open submenu (lv:collection-submenu-open not fired by collection-nav in horizontal mode); two-axis hop (auto-open next submenu on Right/Left Arrow from within a vertical submenu).

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui menubar partial -- a horizontal bar of top-level menu buttons (APG Menubar pattern).

  TIER: PARTIAL (headless uncontrolled overlay, REFORGE-AGENT-BRIEF tier doctrine).

  UNCONTROLLED model: the bar itself carries no server-owned open state. Each top-level
  trigger in the `content` slot is an ordinary dropdown-menu (or a plain button/link)
  whose open/closed lifecycle is browser-owned via the native popover API + the
  collection-nav enhancer. No Java component, no wire round-trip to open or close a menu.
  Actions inside individual menu items carry their own l:click / href directives; THOSE
  are the server interactions. The bar is stateless.

  STRUCTURE: a single semantic landmark (nav or div) with role="menubar" + aria-label +
  aria-orientation="horizontal" that wraps the caller's top-level trigger items via the
  `content` Content slot. Each item inside the slot MUST carry:
    - role="menuitem" (or "menuitem" is implied by the platform for <button>/<a> within
      role="menubar", but explicit is clearer)
    - data-lievit-item (the collection-nav item selector)
    - tabindex="-1" (except the first item, which gets tabindex="0" as the roving seed)

  COLLECTION-NAV (horizontal roving-tabindex):
    The bar root carries data-lievit-collection + data-lievit-collection-orientation="horizontal"
    + data-lievit-collection-roving-tabindex="true" + data-lievit-collection-wrap="true".
    This makes the enhancer manage Left/Right Arrow roving across the top-level triggers,
    Home/End, and typeahead within the bar row.

  ENHANCER GAPS (ENHANCER_NEEDED -- do not hand-roll these):
    - Down/Up Arrow on a horizontal bar item should open the focused trigger's submenu and
      move focus into it. The current collection-nav does not fire a submenu-open event
      when orientation="horizontal" and ArrowDown is pressed (ArrowDown is not a navigation
      key in horizontal mode, so it falls through unhandled). A data-lievit-collection-down-opens
      hook is needed so the bar fires lv:collection-submenu-open on Down Arrow as well.
    - The two-axis hop (Right/Left Arrow while a vertical submenu is open closes the current
      submenu, moves to the next/previous top-level trigger, and opens its submenu) requires
      coordination between the horizontal collection-nav instance (bar) and each vertical
      collection-nav instance (submenu). The current enhancer's ArrowRight/Left in a vertical
      submenu fires the escape-action, which closes the submenu and returns focus to the
      bar trigger -- that IS the right intermediate step. The bar then receives focus, and
      subsequent Arrow keystrokes navigate the bar normally. Full "open-next-automatically"
      hop is not wired until the enhancer supports the two-axis handshake.
    See §ENHANCER_NEEDED in the structured report below.

  KEYBOARD (via collection-nav, horizontal mode):
    Bar context:
      Right Arrow          next top-level trigger (wraps to first)
      Left Arrow           previous top-level trigger (wraps to last)
      Home                 first top-level trigger
      End                  last top-level trigger
      typeahead            jump to trigger whose label starts with the typed char
      Down/Up Arrow        ENHANCER_NEEDED: should open the trigger's submenu
      Tab / Shift+Tab      exits the bar (no trap -- non-modal)
    Submenu context (owned by collection-nav on each dropdown-menu panel, vertical mode):
      Down/Up Arrow        roving within the submenu
      Home / End           first / last submenu item
      Esc                  close submenu + return focus to the top-level trigger
      Right/Left Arrow     close submenu + move to next/prev bar trigger
                           (ENHANCER_NEEDED: auto-open of next submenu)
      Tab / Shift+Tab      close ALL open submenus + exit bar
      typeahead            jump to submenu item starting with typed char
      Enter / Space        activate item or open nested submenu

  POPOVER SEAM: each dropdown-menu inside the content slot uses the native popover API for
  its panel (popover="auto"). The bar itself does not manage any popover.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Menubar, https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menubar/):
    - Bar container: role="menubar" aria-label aria-orientation="horizontal".
    - Top-level triggers: role="menuitem" (on <button> or <a> children inside the slot).
    - Roving tabindex: exactly one trigger has tabindex="0"; all others have tabindex="-1".
      The server renders the FIRST trigger with tabindex="0"; the enhancer manages subsequent
      roving as focus moves.
    - No focus trap: the menubar is non-modal. Tab exits the bar.

  COMPOSITION: the caller composes the content slot from multiple
  @template.lievit.dropdown-menu(...) calls (one per top-level menu). The dropdown-menu
  trigger element MUST additionally carry role="menuitem" + data-lievit-item so the
  collection-nav bar instance can discover it. Use the triggerClass / attrs channel on
  dropdown-menu to inject these.

  Sizes: size controls the bar block-size (height) via a CSS variable. Individual trigger
  items grow to fill the bar height via `height:100%` + `align-items:center` on the bar.

  Params:
    String           id          = "lv-menubar"  unique id for the bar element.
    String           ariaLabel                    accessible name for the role="menubar" container
                                                  (required; SR announces the bar by this name).
    String           size        = "md"           "sm" | "md" | "lg" -- bar block-size.
                                                  sm: --lv-space-8 (32px dense toolbar)
                                                  md: --lv-space-9 (36px, default)
                                                  lg: --lv-space-10 (40px prominent)
    gg.jte.Content   content                      top-level menu triggers (dropdown-menu instances
                                                  or plain button/link items). Each trigger MUST
                                                  carry role="menuitem" + data-lievit-item.
    String           cssClass    = ""             extra utility classes on the bar root.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.menubar(
      id = "app-menu",
      ariaLabel = "Application menu",
      content = @@`
        @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu(
          id = "file-menu",
          triggerClass = "h-full rounded-none px-[var(--lv-space-3)] hover:bg-[var(--lv-color-accent)] hover:text-[var(--lv-color-accent-fg)] focus-visible:bg-[var(--lv-color-accent)]",
          trigger = @@`File`,
          content = @@`
            @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(label = "New File", shortcut = "Ctrl+N")
            @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(label = "Open...", shortcut = "Ctrl+O")
            @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.separator()
            @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(label = "Save", shortcut = "Ctrl+S")
          `
        )
        @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu(
          id = "edit-menu",
          triggerClass = "h-full rounded-none px-[var(--lv-space-3)] hover:bg-[var(--lv-color-accent)] hover:text-[var(--lv-color-accent-fg)] focus-visible:bg-[var(--lv-color-accent)]",
          trigger = @@`Edit`,
          content = @@`
            @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(label = "Undo", shortcut = "Ctrl+Z")
            @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(label = "Redo", shortcut = "Ctrl+Y")
          `
        )
      `
    )
--%>
@param String id = "lv-menubar"
@param String ariaLabel
@param String size = "md"
@param gg.jte.Content content
@param String cssClass = ""

!{var barHeight = switch (size) {
    case "sm" -> "var(--lv-space-8)";
    case "lg" -> "var(--lv-space-10)";
    default   -> "var(--lv-space-9)";
  };}
!{var textSize = switch (size) {
    case "sm" -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-xs)]";
    case "lg" -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-base)]";
    default   -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)]";
  };}

<%-- BAR CONTAINER:
     role="menubar" is the landmark for the APG Menubar pattern.
     aria-orientation="horizontal" is required: the APG mandates it for menubars (vertical is the
     default when omitted, so explicit "horizontal" is load-bearing for AT behaviour).
     data-controller="lv-menubar" drives the horizontal roving-tabindex keyboard model (Left/Right
     Arrow across top-level triggers, Home/End, typeahead, ArrowDown opens a submenu) as a Stimulus
     controller, morph-safe via connect/disconnect. The data-lievit-collection* attributes remain
     as the declarative contract the controller reads (orientation, wrap); the legacy collection-nav
     enhancer SKIPS any root carrying data-controller="lv-menubar" so the keydown is handled once.
     data-size is the hook for adopter CSS or Tailwind variant overrides. --%>
<nav
  id="${id}"
  role="menubar"
  aria-label="${ariaLabel}"
  aria-orientation="horizontal"
  data-slot="menubar"
  data-size="${size}"
  data-controller="lv-menubar"
  data-lievit-collection
  data-lievit-collection-roving-tabindex="true"
  data-lievit-collection-orientation="horizontal"
  data-lievit-collection-wrap="true"
  class="flex flex-row items-stretch ${textSize} ${cssClass}"
  style="height:${barHeight};background:var(--lv-color-bg);color:var(--lv-color-fg);font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);"
>${content}</nav>

items.docs: Copies the menubar partial to your JTE root at lievit/menubar.jte, reached as @template.lievit.menubar(ariaLabel='...', content=@@`...`). The bar is a horizontal APG Menubar (role='menubar', aria-orientation='horizontal'). Compose the content slot from @template.lievit.dropdown-menu instances (one per top-level menu). Each dropdown-menu trigger must also carry role='menuitem' and data-lievit-item via the triggerClass / attrs params so the collection-nav bar instance can discover and rove through them. The bar uses collection-nav.enhancer.ts in horizontal roving-tabindex mode for Left/Right Arrow navigation, Home/End, and typeahead. Size sm/md/lg sets the bar height to --lv-space-8/9/10. Down Arrow to open submenu and the two-axis hop (auto-open-next) require a collection-nav enhancer update (see ENHANCER_NEEDED in the meta). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: modal
items.description: A centred overlay dialog, headless CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED (architecture contract overlay doctrine). UNCONTROLLED (default): the trigger is a real <button command="show-modal" commandfor> (browser invoker command API); the browser opens the <dialog> as a native modal (top-layer, ::backdrop, focus-trap, Escape) with zero JS; every close path is a <form method="dialog"> submit. CONTROLLED (open param set by the caller's @Wire field): pass open + closeAction; the dialog renders with the HTML `open` attribute when open=true; a positioned scrim covers the viewport; data-lievit-focus-trap activates the shared focus-trap enhancer (Tab cycling, scroll-lock, Esc fires closeAction); when open=false the dialog is hidden (absent from a11y tree). Closable controls the X button + scrim-click + Esc together. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal): role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby/aria-describedby, icon-only close button with aria-label. Content slots: content (owned body, never a slot), heading + description (labelled dialog), footer (actions). Sizes sm/md/lg/xl map to panel max-width. No Java component, no @Wire field.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui modal partial -- a centred overlay dialog, headless CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (focus-trap.enhancer.ts). No Java component, no @Wire field, no _component/_instance params. The open-state belongs to the CALLER. CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED doctrine (architecture contract, "Overlay & stateful primitives"): UNCONTROLLED (default; closeAction = null): The trigger is a real ` ) Controlled (server-owned open, e.g. a form that stays open on validation errors): @@template.lievit.modal( id = "confirm-modal", heading = "Delete?", description = "This cannot be undone.", content = @@`

Are you sure?

`, footer = @@``, size = "sm", closable = false, open = myForm.confirmOpen(), closeAction = "closeConfirm" ) --%> @param String id = "lv-modal" @param gg.jte.Content trigger = null @param gg.jte.Content content @param String heading = null @param String description = null @param gg.jte.Content footer = null @param String size = "md" @param boolean closable = true @param boolean open = false @param String closeAction = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var titleId = id + "-title";} !{var descId = id + "-description";} !{var hasHeading = heading != null && !heading.isEmpty();} !{var hasDesc = description != null && !description.isEmpty();} !{var isControlled = closeAction != null && !closeAction.isEmpty();} !{var maxW = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "24rem"; case "lg" -> "36rem"; case "xl" -> "42rem"; default -> "28rem"; };} !{var panelClass = "relative z-[var(--lv-z-modal)] flex w-[calc(100%-var(--lv-space-8))] max-h-[calc(100dvh-var(--lv-space-8))] flex-col overflow-hidden rounded-[var(--lv-radius-lg)] border border-[var(--lv-color-border)] bg-[var(--lv-color-popover)] text-[var(--lv-color-fg)] shadow-[var(--lv-shadow-xl)]";} @if(trigger != null) <%-- Trigger button: uncontrolled path -- browser opens the as a modal (no JS). --%> @endif @if(isControlled && open) <%-- Controlled-mode scrim: full-viewport cover behind the panel when open=true. --%>
@endif <%-- The element. UNCONTROLLED mode (isControlled=false): - No hidden attribute (browser manages show/hide via showModal()). - No data-lievit-focus-trap (browser native modal trap handles Tab cycling). - No data-lievit-escape-action (browser cancels on Esc natively). - Panel does NOT carry fixed positioning classes (browser top-layer handles it). CONTROLLED mode (isControlled=true): - hidden="${!open}" (smart attr: present=hidden when false; absent when true/visible). - data-lievit-focus-trap="" (enhancer activates trap + scroll-lock on mount). - data-lievit-escape-action="${closeAction}" only when closable (must-act: omit when not). - Panel needs fixed centering CSS because is not in the top-layer. --%>

items.docs: Copies the modal partial to your JTE root at lievit/modal.jte, reached as @template.lievit.modal(...). UNCONTROLLED (default): pass a `trigger` content slot and a unique `id` -- the browser opens and closes the dialog natively with zero JS (command="show-modal"). CONTROLLED: pass `open` (your @Wire boolean) and `closeAction` (the wire action name) to let the server own the open-state; the focus-trap.enhancer.ts handles the focus cycle + scroll-lock + Esc. Both modes support: `heading` + `description` (labelled dialog), `footer` (action buttons), `size` (sm|md|lg|xl), `closable` (X + Esc + scrim, default true). Install the focus-trap enhancer when using controlled mode: import installFocusTrap from your lievit runtime features and call it with the runtime instance.

items.name: native-select
items.description: Token-styled wrapper around the native <select> element: PARTIAL tier, zero JS, zero enhancer, full platform a11y for free. WAI-ARIA APG Listbox (native) -- the platform supplies role=listbox, all keyboard interaction (Tab, arrows, Home/End, type-ahead), and accessible-name binding. Three option-authoring paths with explicit precedence: content slot (hand-authored <option>/<optgroup>, value!=label) > optionGroups (Map of String to String-list, rendered as native <optgroup>) > options (flat List<String>, value==label). Height-based size scale (sm/md/lg; 'default' is back-compat alias of 'md') aligns flush with button and input of the same size in a toolbar row. Invalid state: aria-invalid + destructive border + ring (color-mix in oklch). Wire binding via l:model on the native <select> (change event -> WIRE field setter); null = plain form POST, zero JS. No attrs trusted-raw channel (no per-row dynamic wire action on this partial). CSP-clean: no inline script, no on* handler.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui native-select partial -- token-styled wrapper around the native carries role=listbox, full keyboard interaction, accessible-name binding, and form submission for free from the platform. No enhancer is created or needed: this is the canonical exemplar of "prefer a real native element" (architecture contract §2.a). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - WAI-ARIA APG Listbox native-select baseline: the native , role=option on ; no custom role emitted -- the platform supplies all. - aria-label: on when invalid=true; absent otherwise. - required / disabled: native boolean attributes on keeps its native focus for AT. - keyboard: entirely platform-supplied (Tab, arrows, Home, End, type-ahead, Space/Enter/Esc); no enhancer is created or needed. SIZE (toolbar-aligned per architecture contract §5.b -- form controls use height-based scale): sm -> h-[var(--lv-space-8)] (32px) compact toolbar, filter row md -> h-[var(--lv-space-9)] (36px) standard form; THE default; shadcn baseline lg -> h-[var(--lv-space-10)] (40px) prominent / standalone field default -> h-[var(--lv-space-9)] (36px) back-compat alias of md A button, input, and native-select of the same size sit flush-aligned in a toolbar row. Params: String name the for

items.docs: Copies the native-select partial to your JTE root at lievit/native-select.jte, reached as @template.lievit.native-select(name=...). Pass options (flat List<String>, value==label), optionGroups (Map of String to List<String>, renders native <optgroup>), or a content slot of hand-authored <option>/<optgroup> markup (value!=label, per-option disabled); content wins over optionGroups wins over options. Pass label for a real <label for> above the control (preferred accessible-name path) or ariaLabel for a label-free context. Pass placeholder for a disabled hidden first option (the native 'choose...' idiom). size (sm=32px, md=36px default, lg=40px; 'default' is back-compat alias of 'md') is height-based so the select lines up flush with a button and input of the same size in a toolbar. invalid=true adds aria-invalid + destructive border + ring. model binds the native change event to a WIRE field via l:model; null means plain form POST. Requires the icon partial for the decorative chevron-down indicator. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: navigation-menu
items.description: A site navigation landmark (<nav>, not role=menu) with real <a href> links and rich disclosure panels (APG Disclosure Navigation pattern). UNCONTROLLED HEADLESS: panels open/close via native [popover=auto] (Esc + light-dismiss built-in), enhanced by popover-anchor.enhancer.ts (focus return) and collection-nav.enhancer.ts (arrow keys + typeahead). Zero server round-trip to open/close. Server-first PARTIAL+ENH (v-next re-forge). Compose top-level items with navigation-menu/link (leaf links) and navigation-menu/menu (disclosure trigger+panel). Panels contain navigation-menu/link(inPanel=true) items grouped via navigation-menu/menu/group.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). navigation-menu partial --
landmark with top-level items that reveal rich panels via native [popover] + the shared popover-anchor.enhancer.ts (UNCONTROLLED, no server round-trip just to open/close). Keyboard supplement (Esc, arrow keys, typeahead) via collection-nav.enhancer.ts. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (ADR-0012, v-next). The disclosure open/close is UNCONTROLLED: - Native `[popover]` closes on Esc + light-dismiss natively (browser built-in). - popover-anchor.enhancer.ts wires opener recording + focus return on close. - collection-nav.enhancer.ts adds supplemental arrow-key navigation + typeahead. No WIRE component; no server round-trip to open/close. This is the `navigation-menu-static` tier described in the spec, promoted to the primary v-next API because the UNCONTROLLED path is the correct default (APG Disclosure Navigation). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure Navigation pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/examples/disclosure-navigation/). NOT role=menu / role=menubar -- those describe application-widget menus with mandatory arrow-key roving. This is a
landmark with real links; AT announces "navigation", not "menu". Disclosure semantics: each item is either a plain leaf link or a

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). navigation-menu/link partial -- one focusable item in a navigation-menu: either a top-level leaf link or a plain panel link (inside a navigation-menu/menu panel). TIER: PARTIAL -- pure server-rendered presentation, no client state. STRUCTURE (APG Disclosure Navigation leaf link): Top-level leaf link: a real in natural Tab order. Carries data-lievit-item so collection-nav.enhancer.ts can include it in the arrow-key / typeahead supplemental nav. Panel link: same , rendered as a richer row (icon + label + optional description), placed inside the panel
    of navigation-menu/menu. Does NOT carry data-lievit-item (panel links are NOT in the top-level collection; Tab naturally reaches them when the panel is open). A11y: - Real anchor: browser gives role=link + Tab focus + Enter navigation for free. - aria-current="page" when href matches the active route (server-supplied via activeHref). - Disabled: aria-disabled="true" + href omitted (NOT native `disabled`, which does not apply to ). The link remains focusable so Tab still reaches it (APG recommendation for disabled links; they are informational even when blocked). - Icon (when rendered): aria-hidden="true" via the icon partial's default (decorative). - Badge (when rendered): aria-hidden="true" (decorative; the label provides the accessible name). Params: String href the destination href; omitted (href not set) when disabled. String label the visible link text. String activeHref = "" the current page href; when href == activeHref the link gets aria-current="page" and the active accent style. String description = null optional sub-description beneath the label (panel rows only). String icon = null optional Lucide icon name (decorative; aria-hidden via icon partial). String badge = null optional badge text (e.g. "New"); aria-hidden. boolean disabled = false aria-disabled="true" + href omitted. boolean inPanel = false true => richer in-panel row style; false => top-level pill style. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the . --%> @param String href @param String label @param String activeHref = "" @param String description = null @param String icon = null @param String badge = null @param boolean disabled = false @param boolean inPanel = false @param String cssClass = "" !{boolean _isActive = href != null && !href.isBlank() && href.equals(activeHref);} !{var _href = disabled ? null : href;} !{var _ariaCurrent = _isActive ? "page" : null;} !{var _ariaDisabled = disabled ? "true" : null;} @if(inPanel) <%-- PANEL LINK: richer row with optional icon + description. Not in the top-level collection. --%>
  • @if(icon != null && !icon.isBlank()) @template.lievit.icon(name = icon, size = "1rem") @endif ${label}@if(badge != null && !badge.isBlank()) ${badge}@endif @if(description != null && !description.isBlank()) ${description} @endif
    • @else <%-- TOP-LEVEL LEAF LINK: pill style; in the collection for arrow-key nav. --%>
  • @if(icon != null && !icon.isBlank()) @template.lievit.icon(name = icon, size = "1rem") @endif ${label} @if(badge != null && !badge.isBlank()) ${badge} @endif
    • @endif
