items.name: notification-bell
items.description: An icon-button with an inline unread-count badge that opens a native-popover notification history panel. Icon-only bell button (aria-label embeds the count); unread count badge is inlined (self-contained, no badge partial dependency); items are List-of-Map (keys: id, variant, message, description, timestamp, read) so no dev.lievit import is needed. Panel is role=region with a <ul><li> item list in natural tab order (no roving tabindex). Positions via CSS Anchor Positioning (light-dismiss + Esc built-in). Bell button follows the sm/md/lg height-based toolbar-alignment scale. Token-driven, CSP-clean, zero JS shipped.
items.docs: Copies notification-bell.jte to your JTE root at lievit/notification-bell.jte, reached as @template.lievit.notification-bell(unreadCount=N, items=itemList, bellAriaLabel="Notifications"). Items are java.util.List<java.util.Map<String,String>> with keys id/variant/message/description/timestamp/read. The panel opens/closes via the native browser popover API; no JS is required. The bell button's aria-label embeds the unread count for AT. Wire clear-all via clearAllWireClick; the button is absent when null. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: pagination
items.description: APG navigation landmark with numbered page controls. Real <a href> per page (URL mode) or <button> elements with data-page (wire mode). Sliding-window algorithm with ellipsis gaps; prev/next; optional total summary, page-size switcher, quick jumper, simple mode. aria-current='page' on active page; aria-label required on the nav landmark. Zero client state: currentPage, totalPages, and baseUrl are all server facts passed as typed @params.
items.docs: Copies the pagination partial to your JTE root at lievit/pagination.jte. Pass currentPage (1-based, required), totalPages (required; 0 or 1 renders nothing), and either baseUrl (URL mode: emits real <a href> links, Turbo Drive ready) or omit it for wire mode (emits <button type='button'> with data-page on each item; wire the action via pageAttrs='l:click="goToPage"'). Requires the icon partial (chevron-left, chevron-right, ellipsis) and native-select partial (when showSizeSwitcher=true). Size sm/md/lg aligns flush with button/input in a toolbar row. Optional: showTotal (total-items live region), showSizeSwitcher (page-size native-select), showJumper (go-to-page form), simple (prev/next + fraction, no numbered list). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: popover
items.description: The shared positioning + light-dismiss + focus-return SEAM for every non-modal overlay (dropdown-menu, select, combobox, tooltip, hover-card, date-picker, context-menu, command, mentions). CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED headless overlay (NOT a bespoke WIRE component). UNCONTROLLED by default (controlled=false): the panel is always in the DOM; the browser's native popover API (popovertarget) shows/hides it client-side — zero JS, zero wire round-trip. CONTROLLED (controlled=true): pass `open` from the caller's own @Wire field; the server gates panel presence. Show/hide via the native HTML `popover` attribute; position via CSS Anchor Positioning (anchor-name / position-anchor / position-area + flip-block fallback) — all twelve placement positions supported. The `popover-anchor.enhancer.ts` handles the one irreducible client bit: focus-return on light-dismiss (click-outside) via data-lv-opener, autofocus delegation via data-lv-autofocus, and wire sync of close() in controlled mode. Panel content is OWNED server-rendered markup (the `content` Content slot). Optional `title` + `description` render a PopoverHeader and make the panel a labelled dialog (role=dialog + aria-labelledby). Three variant surfaces: default | muted | destructive. Optional decorative arrow/caret (withArrow). Replaces the <lv-popover> Lit island and the old PARTIAL. CSP-clean, no inline handlers, no @floating-ui/dom.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui popover partial -- the shared positioning + light-dismiss + focus-return SEAM. Every non-modal overlay in lievit composes this: dropdown-menu, select, combobox, tooltip, hover-card, date-picker, context-menu, command, mentions. This is NOT a standalone tooltip bubble; it is the ONE mechanism so no overlay hand-rolls anchor positioning or light-dismiss. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (popover-anchor.enhancer.ts handles the one irreducible client bit: CSS Anchor Positioning fallback wiring + focus-return bookkeeping on light-dismiss). This template is a CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED headless overlay (NOT a bespoke WIRE component): UNCONTROLLED (default, controlled=false): the panel is always present in the DOM; the browser's native popover API (popovertarget + popovertargetaction="toggle") shows/hides it client-side. Zero JS, zero wire round-trip. open-state is ephemeral (browser-owned). CONTROLLED (controlled=true): the caller OWNS an open-state field (e.g. a @Wire boolean on a parent component). The panel is present in the DOM only when open=true (server conditional). The trigger's l:click action is wired by the CALLER's owned copy (copy-in pattern); the seam does not hardcode it. This covers: content fetched on open; open must survive a round-trip; open is a genuine business fact. The open-state is NOT owned by this partial: the partial holds no @Wire field, no Java component, no _component/_instance/_componentSnapshot params. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): a11y source: WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure (Show/Hide) pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/) for the trigger/panel pair; the native HTML popover attribute (MDN + open-ui.org) supplies the browser-native light-dismiss + top-layer seam. No APG "Popover" pattern exists -- the APG Disclosure + native popover + context-specific ARIA roles (added by the composing component) is the correct assembly. positioning: CSS Anchor Positioning Level 1 (anchor-name / position-anchor / position-area). All twelve placement positions map to CSS position-area values; no JS positioning loop. NO @floating-ui/dom; NO hand-rolled pixel-offset JS. A11y (APG Disclosure + native popover attribute): Trigger: real native button, role implicit (button); aria-expanded server-emitted from open; aria-controls points to the panel id. popovertarget + popovertargetaction="toggle" wire the browser's native open/close signal (Enter, Space, click). The browser also sets the implicit anchor relationship (popover invoker target); we additionally set explicit anchor-name / position-anchor for CSS AP. Panel: popover attribute on a div (or semantically appropriate container); no fixed ARIA role on the seam itself -- the composing component adds role="menu" / "listbox" / "dialog" etc. An optional title + description make the panel a labelled dialog (role="dialog" + aria-labelledby) when the popover carries interactive or confirmable content. Keyboard (APG Disclosure + native popover): Enter / Space on trigger: toggle open/close (platform, native button) Escape: closes auto/hint popover (native browser signal); popover-anchor.enhancer.ts syncs the server close() action ONLY when the panel is controlled (data-lv-wire-close present); an uncontrolled panel closes client-side with no wire round-trip. Tab: non-trapping -- focus moves naturally through and out of the panel (non-modal). Focus: focus-return on Esc is native (browser popover spec); focus-return on light-dismiss (click-outside) is the enhancer's responsibility via data-lv-opener on the panel. No focus trap (use dialog/drawer + focus-trap.enhancer.ts for modal overlays). Enhancer wiring (data-attribute contract, READ runtime/features/popover-anchor.enhancer.ts): Panel carries: popover="${type}" (native), data-lv-opener="${triggerId}" (focus-return). Trigger carries: id="${triggerId}", style="anchor-name: --${triggerId}". Panel carries: style="position-anchor: --${triggerId}; position-area: ; ...". Inside panel: [data-lv-autofocus] -- the element the enhancer moves focus to after open. CONTROLLED only: the panel carries data-lv-wire-close="${escapeAction}", and on light-dismiss the enhancer fires that wire action, finding the component root via panel.closest("[data-lievit-component]"). UNCONTROLLED panels omit data-lv-wire-close, so the enhancer fires NO wire call -- the close is purely client-side (no spurious 410 page-expired). Params: String id = "lv-popover" the panel id (the popovertarget link). Unique per page. Derives triggerId="${id}-trigger" and panelId="${id}". gg.jte.Content trigger the trigger CONTENT (label / icon) inside the button. gg.jte.Content content the panel body, OWNED + server-rendered. boolean open = false controlled state: true = panel present in DOM. Ignored when controlled=false (always rendered). boolean controlled = false false = uncontrolled (browser owns open state); true = server owns open state via the open param. String escapeAction = "close" CONTROLLED only: wire action the popover-anchor enhancer fires on light-dismiss to sync server open-state. Stamped as data-lv-wire-close ONLY when controlled=true; uncontrolled panels omit it and close purely client-side (no wire round-trip). String placement = "bottom-start" CSS Anchor Positioning placement: one of the twelve positions (bottom-start | bottom | bottom-end | top-start | top | top-end | left-start | left | left-end | right-start | right | right-end). String type = "auto" native popover type: auto | manual | hint. auto = light-dismiss + Esc; manual = neither. int offset = 4 gap in px between trigger and panel. boolean withArrow = false render a decorative caret pointing at the trigger. String variant = "default" panel surface: default | muted | destructive. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the trigger button. boolean disabled = false disables the trigger (panel cannot open). gg.jte.Content title = null optional header title; makes the panel a labelled dialog (role="dialog" + aria-labelledby the title id). gg.jte.Content description = null optional subtitle under the title (aria-describedby). Usage: Uncontrolled (default -- browser toggles, no wire round-trip): @@template.lievit.popover( id = "settings-popover", trigger = @@`Settings`, content = @@`

Panel body, server-rendered.

` ) Controlled (caller's @Wire boolean gates the panel -- copy-in pattern for the l:click): @@template.lievit.popover( id = "filter-popover", open = filterPanel.open(), controlled = true, trigger = @@`Filter`, content = @@`...` ) --%> @param String id = "lv-popover" @param gg.jte.Content trigger @param gg.jte.Content content @param boolean open = false @param boolean controlled = false @param String escapeAction = "close" @param String placement = "bottom-start" @param String type = "auto" @param int offset = 4 @param boolean withArrow = false @param String variant = "default" @param String cssClass = "" @param boolean disabled = false @param gg.jte.Content title = null @param gg.jte.Content description = null <%-- Derive stable ids from the base id. The trigger gets an id for aria-controls + anchor-name. The anchor dashed-ident must be a valid (starts with --); the trigger id is always server-generated (lv-prefixed), so the inline anchor-name style is the safe channel. --%> !{var triggerId = id + "-trigger";} !{var panelId = id;} !{var anchorIdent = "--" + triggerId;} <%-- Map the twelve placement values to CSS position-area values (CSS Anchor Positioning Level 1). Each position-area value places the panel on the named side relative to the anchor. --%> !{var positionArea = switch (placement) { case "bottom" -> "bottom center"; case "bottom-start" -> "bottom span-right"; case "bottom-end" -> "bottom span-left"; case "top" -> "top center"; case "top-start" -> "top span-right"; case "top-end" -> "top span-left"; case "left" -> "left center"; case "left-start" -> "left span-bottom"; case "left-end" -> "left span-top"; case "right" -> "right center"; case "right-start" -> "right span-bottom"; case "right-end" -> "right span-top"; default -> "bottom span-right"; };} <%-- Arrow placement side: which side of the panel the caret visually points FROM (the side that faces the trigger). Derived from the block/inline direction of the placement. --%> !{var arrowSide = switch (placement) { case "top", "top-start", "top-end" -> "bottom"; case "left", "left-start", "left-end" -> "right"; case "right", "right-start", "right-end" -> "left"; default -> "top"; };} <%-- Panel surface tokens: variant drives bg + fg (no hardcoded colours, tokens only). --%> !{var panelBg = switch (variant) { case "muted" -> "var(--lv-color-muted-bg)"; case "destructive" -> "var(--lv-color-destructive)"; default -> "var(--lv-color-popover)"; };} !{var panelFg = switch (variant) { case "muted" -> "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)"; case "destructive" -> "var(--lv-color-destructive-fg)"; default -> "var(--lv-color-popover-fg)"; };} <%-- Inline offset: the gap between trigger and panel edge. Applied as margin on the axis that runs away from the trigger (block for top/bottom, inline for left/right placements). --%> !{var isBlock = placement.startsWith("bottom") || placement.startsWith("top");} !{var offsetPx = offset + "px";} !{var marginBlock = isBlock ? offsetPx + " 0" : "0";} !{var marginInline = !isBlock ? offsetPx + " 0" : "0";} <%-- Labelled-dialog wiring: a non-null title renders a PopoverHeader and adds role="dialog" + aria-labelledby so AT announces the panel's purpose. A header-less panel carries NO role (a role="dialog" with no accessible name lies to AT; the native popover relationship is the announced affordance). --%> !{var labelled = title != null;} !{var titleId = id + "-title";} !{var descId = id + "-description";} <%-- aria-expanded: server-emitted from the open state for consistent AT behaviour. In uncontrolled mode open=false (default), the browser updates it dynamically; we still emit it statically so AT sees the correct initial state. In controlled mode it tracks the server fact. --%> !{var ariaExpanded = open ? "true" : "false";} <%-- Panel presence gate: in uncontrolled mode the panel is always present in the DOM; the native popover attribute hides it until the trigger toggles it. In controlled mode the server conditional is the authoritative gate (panel absent = completely out of AT + DOM). --%> !{var renderPanel = !controlled || open;} <%-- The trigger is the FIRST root-level element (stays in normal document flow). anchor-name is set as an inline style -- safe: value is server-generated (lv- prefixed id), never user-supplied. popovertarget wires the browser's native show/hide signal for the uncontrolled case; in controlled mode the caller's owned copy adds l:click="toggleOpen". --%> <%-- The panel is the SECOND root-level element (adjacent sibling -- never wrapped with the trigger in a common container). The native popover attribute moves it to the top layer. In uncontrolled mode: always present, browser hides/shows via popovertarget. In controlled mode: server-conditional (panel absent from DOM + AT when closed). --%> @if(renderPanel)
@if(labelled) <%-- PopoverHeader: a title (+ optional description) makes the panel a labelled dialog. Header-less panels carry no role; labelled panels get role="dialog" + aria-labelledby. The title id is stable (derived from the panel id) so aria-labelledby resolves. --%>
${title}
@if(description != null)
${description}
@endif
@endif <%-- Panel body: OWNED server-rendered markup. The Content slot fills here; composing components that own a copy of this template fill this region with their content (options list, date grid, form fields, rich content). Never a native . --%>
${content}
@if(withArrow) <%-- Decorative caret: a rotated square clipped to a triangle via CSS. Positioned on the side of the panel that faces the trigger (arrowSide). aria-hidden: decorative only. The clip-path produces a triangle pointing toward the trigger for each side: top arrowSide -> bottom: panel is above trigger, caret points down (bottom edge) bottom arrowSide -> top: panel is below trigger, caret points up (top edge) left arrowSide -> right: panel is left of trigger, caret points right right arrowSide -> left: panel is right of trigger, caret points left --%> @endif
@endif

items.docs: Copies popover.jte to your JTE root at lievit/popover.jte, reached as @template.lievit.popover(...). Required params: `id` (unique panel id; derives the trigger id as <id>-trigger), `trigger` content (label/icon inside the button), `content` panel body (server-rendered, never a <slot>). By default (controlled=false) the panel is always in the DOM and the browser's native popover API handles open/close — zero JS, zero server round-trip. To gate panel presence from the server, set controlled=true and pass `open` from your caller's @Wire boolean field. Set `placement` (12 positions: bottom-start | bottom | bottom-end | top-start | top | top-end | left-start | left | left-end | right-start | right | right-end; default: bottom-start), `type` (auto|manual|hint; default: auto), `offset` (gap px; default: 4), `withArrow` (decorative caret; default: false), `variant` (default|muted|destructive; default: default), `disabled` (default: false). Optional `title` + `description` add a PopoverHeader and make the panel a labelled dialog (role=dialog + aria-labelledby). The popover-anchor.enhancer.ts must be registered in the runtime (installPopoverAnchor) for focus-return on light-dismiss and autofocus delegation.

items.name: popover-wire
items.description: The server-data-driven variant of the popover overlay seam (ADR-0012 blueprint R4): open-state lives in typed Java and the panel CONTENT is (re)derived from server state on each open (gest's calendar-filter feed). The server re-renders the conditionally-rendered panel, the client morphs it; no <slot>, no @floating-ui/dom, no Lit. Positioning stays the server-pure CSS Anchor Positioning the popover PARTIAL uses. Use the popover JTE partial for a static popover; this heavier WIRE tier is for server-held content + open-state.

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui popover WIRE template -- the server-data-driven popover variant whose open-state lives server-side in PopoverComponent (ADR-0012 blueprint R4). Pairs with registry/wire/popover/PopoverComponent.java. For a STATIC popover use the popover.jte PARTIAL (native `popover` attribute, zero JS); this wire form is for when the panel CONTENT is derived from server state on open (gest's calendar-filter feed). TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `toggle` action over the wire and the server re-renders this template with the panel CONTENT recomputed from server state, the client morphs it. No : the panel body is OWNED markup conditionally rendered (@if(open)), the server-first equivalent of children -- it CANNOT fail to project (the light-DOM slot bug the pivot exists to kill). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component (JteTemplateAdapter), so the body is NOT a JTE Content slot supplied at call time: it is owned markup the adopter edits in their copy, or drives from a @Wire field / the _instance. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA dialog-popover (Radix Popover model), positioned with CSS Anchor Positioning, the SAME server-pure positioning the popover PARTIAL uses: - the trigger is a real @if(open)
<%-- Owned panel body: replace this with your server-derived content (the filtered feed, a permission-gated form). Recomputed from server state on each open. --%>

Popover content.

@endif

items.docs: Register PopoverComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / @template.lievit.popover-wire(...); put your server-derived panel body in the owned data-popover-panel region (NOT a slot), or derive it from a @Wire field. The trigger fires `toggle` over the wire; `show`/`close` are also exposed. The panel is conditionally rendered (@if(open)) so its content cannot fail to project. The @LievitComponent(template="lievit/popover-wire") name + the dev.lievit.wire package are yours to change once copied.

items.name: progress
items.description: Determinate / indeterminate progress in four visual forms (line, circle, dashboard, steps). WAI-ARIA 1.2 progressbar role; aria-valuenow omitted for indeterminate; variant intent tokens; striped + animated stripes; showValue label; trailing and footer content slots. CSP-clean, zero JS, server-rendered.
items.docs: Copies the progress partial to your JTE root at lievit/progress.jte. Reached as @template.lievit.progress(value: 42, max: 100, label: "Uploading"). Pass value=null for the indeterminate animation. Four forms: line (default), circle, dashboard, steps. Variants: default, info, success, warning, destructive. The three @keyframes (lv-progress-slide, lv-progress-stripe-move, lv-progress-arc-spin) must be included from lievit-tokens.css.

items.name: radio-group
items.description: APG Radio Group -- mutually-exclusive option group (default pill, button-bar, native fieldset variants). Custom variants driven by radio-group.enhancer.ts (roving-tabindex + aria-checked); native variant is platform-supplied. Options arrive via @param, never hardcoded. Composes with the field partial via aria-labelledby / aria-describedby.
items.docs: Copies the radio-group partial + its enhancer to your project. The partial renders role=radiogroup + role=radio divs (custom) or a fieldset+legend+inputs (native). Install the enhancer in your main.ts: import { installRadioGroup } from './lievit/radio-group.enhancer'; installRadioGroup(runtime). The native variant (nativeInputs=true) needs no enhancer. Options come from a typed List<RadioOption> passed by the controller -- RadioOption must satisfy id(), label(), description(), disabled().

items.name: repeater
items.description: A repeatable list of nested-schema item cards (Filament's Repeater): the same set of fields repeated N times, each in a card with add / remove (reorder grip rendered, wiring is a follow-up). The item BODY is a RECURSIVE content slot the adopter authors (itemContent), so an item can hold any field -- inputs, a select, even another repeater -- the same shape as a React component taking children. The fields inside name themselves `<name>[<i>][...]` (an indexed form-array a Spring binder maps to a List of records), so the cards POST natively with zero JS. JS-OFF an Add submit (name=`<name>__add`) and a per-item remove submit (name=`<name>__remove`, value=index) round-trip to the server, which re-renders -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer clones a hidden <template> card to add and drops a card to remove, in place, then re-indexes every nested field name + label so the POSTed indices stay contiguous. The native inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: labelled fieldset/legend, each card a labelled region (Item N), labelled add / remove buttons, aria-live. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui repeater partial -- a repeatable list of NESTED-SCHEMA item cards (Filament's Repeater): the same set of fields repeated N times (e.g. "phone numbers", "tenants", "documents"), each in a card with add / remove (and optional reorder). The fields inside each item POST as an INDEXED form-array so the server rebuilds an ordered list of records. TIER: PARTIAL (+ optional enhancer, ADR-0012 server-first). The item BODY is a recursive content slot the adopter authors (`itemContent`), so a repeater item can itself contain any field -- inputs, a select, even another repeater. The adopter names the fields inside with the `__i__` index token (`[__i__][campo]`), which the server stamps to the real index per existing item and the enhancer re-stamps on add. So the rows POST as a clean indexed array with ZERO JavaScript. JS-OFF: add is an explicit "Add" submit (name=`__add`) and per-item remove is a submit (name=`__remove`, value=index) that round-trip to the server, which re-renders with one more / fewer card -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON the CSP-clean enhancer clones the hidden

items.docs: Copies the repeater partial to your JTE root at lievit/repeater.jte (reached as @template.lievit.repeater(name=..., count=..., itemContent=@`...`)) and the CSP-clean enhancer to lievit/repeater.enhancer.ts. Import the enhancer once and call enhanceAllRepeaters() on load (and after a DOM swap). Author the item body as the recursive `itemContent` slot (one card per index, naming the fields `<name>[<i>][...]`), and a blank `itemTemplate` slot using the `__i__` token for the JS-on clone (defaults to itemContent). Pass `count` (existing cards), `label`, `itemLabel`, `addLabel`, `reorderable` (renders the grip; reorder wiring is a follow-up), `disabled`. JS-OFF add / remove are server submits (`<name>__add` / `<name>__remove`); JS-ON the enhancer adds / removes in place and re-indexes so indices stay contiguous. Requires the icon partial (trash-2, plus, grip-vertical). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: rich-editor
items.description: A server-first rich-text form field (Filament RichEditor / shadcn rich-text parity). The canonical control is a plain <textarea name=...> that POSTs its HTML with the surrounding <form> JS-off; a CSP-clean island progressively enhances it into a TipTap (ProseMirror) WYSIWYG editor (toolbar + contenteditable surface) whose serialized HTML is written back into the SAME textarea on every change, so the form keeps POSTing the field with or without JS. TipTap/ProseMirror are CSP-clean (no eval, no inline script; view CSS injected as a <style> element). The TipTap engine is supplied by the adopter through an injectable factory so the version + extension set is pinned downstream and the island orchestration stays unit-testable.

files.content: @import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui rich-editor partial (Filament RichEditor / shadcn rich-text parity) -- a SERVER-FIRST rich-text form field. The canonical form control is a plain

items.docs: Render the partial with a `name` (the form field). With JS off the <textarea> is the editor and POSTs its value. To enhance: `npm i @tiptap/core @tiptap/starter-kit`, then call enhanceRichEditors({ factory }) once from main.ts after startLievit(), passing a factory that builds a TipTap Editor: `({ element, content, onUpdate }) => new Editor({ element, content, extensions: [StarterKit], onUpdate: ({ editor }) => onUpdate(editor.getHTML()) })`. The factory keeps the TipTap version + extension set (tables, links, images, mentions) pinned in YOUR app. The island maps the EditorHandle command/isActive onto editor.chain()...run() and editor.isActive(); the toolbar buttons carry data-rich-editor-cmd (bold/italic/strike/heading/bulletList/orderedList/blockquote) + an optional data-rich-editor-arg (e.g. heading level). On submit you receive the field as HTML; sanitize server-side before persisting/rendering. Adjust the template name freely once copied.

items.name: rich-select
items.description: A searchable Combobox (Filament Select parity, roadmap L1) whose options are filtered SERVER-SIDE: the query binds l:model.debounce, the server re-filters the catalog and re-renders the listbox (the canonical wire/htmx typeahead, no client filtering). Supports single-select (the `selected` string, set via $set) AND multiple-select with removable chips (the `selectedValues` list, toggled via $set('toggleValue',...)), preload (eager full-catalog on mount for small sets) vs lazy debounced search (large sets), inline create-option (a no-match query arms a new value), and rich option labels (avatar/icon + subtext). A tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer adds APG keyboard nav (ArrowUp/Down/Home/End/Enter). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-rich-select> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG combobox + listbox.
items.docs: Register RichSelectComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `allOptions` from your repository in the constructor or a @LievitRender hook (the seed list is placeholder data). The query filters server-side via l:model.debounce. Single mode: selection sets the `selected` field via $set('selected', '<value>'). Multiple mode (multiple=true): each pick / chip-remove toggles membership of its value in `selectedValues` via $set('toggleValue', '<value>'); the chosen options render as removable chips and submit as repeated hidden inputs (name=...). Preload (preload=true) shows the full catalog on mount for SMALL sets; leave it false and narrow `allOptions` server-side per keystroke for LARGE sets. allowCreate=true offers a 'Create "<query>"' affordance on a no-match query (value creation only; wire createOptionUsing server-side for a full form). Rich labels: an Option carries optional avatar (image URL) / icon (Lucide name) + subtext. Call enhanceRichSelects(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() for APG keyboard navigation. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/rich-select")` name assumes the .jte landed under lievit/; adjust freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: scroll-area
items.description: A scrollable viewport with optional overlay scrollbar rails (shadcn Scroll Area parity). Two modes: overlay=false uses the browser's native scrollbar (zero JS, CSP-clean, styled via token-driven CSS); overlay=true hides the native scrollbar and renders custom overlay rails driven by the scroll-area.enhancer.ts (thumb sizing via ResizeObserver, thumb drag via pointer events, aria-valuenow live updates). The three visibility policies (hover/always/scroll) are driven by CSS selectors on data-scrolling and data-pointer-over attributes toggled by the enhancer. Orientation: vertical / horizontal / both. The viewport carries tabindex=0 (keyboard-scrollable) and optionally role=region + aria-label for a named landmark. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012): overlay=false ships zero JS.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui scroll-area partial -- a scrollable viewport with optional overlay scrollbar rails. TIER (planning two-tier model): ENHANCER. When overlay=false this is a purely static partial (a div with overflow + optional ARIA), zero JS. When overlay=true the runtime enhancer (`data-lievit-scroll-area`) sizes and positions custom overlay rails that replace the native scrollbar, syncing thumb size/position via scroll and ResizeObserver. The CSP constraint (no inline script, no on* handler) is met in both modes: the enhancer is discovered via a data attribute, not an event handler. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn/Radix ship a JS scrollbar that papers over native scrollbar inconsistency. For overlay=false we use the STANDARDISED `overflow-*` properties (zero JS, CSP-clean, keyboard-scrollable). For overlay=true we render custom div rails driven by the enhancer (pointer drag, ResizeObserver sync, aria-valuenow updates), matching the shadcn API surface (type/hideDelay). Both modes expose the same data-slot anatomy so CSS selectors work uniformly. VIEWPORT ID: the overlay rails reference the viewport via aria-controls. We derive a per-call id from System.identityHashCode(content) -- this gives a stable-enough id within one HTTP response (two calls with different Content instances give different ids). The adopter can override via a named id in dataAttrs if true uniqueness across the page is required. OVERLAY SCROLLBAR VISIBILITY (data-type + data-scrolling + data-pointer-over): "hover" -- rail visible when the pointer is over the scroll area or it is actively scrolling. "always" -- rail always visible. "scroll" -- rail visible only while scrolling (fades on stop, after hideDelay ms). The CSS in scroll-area.css drives visibility via these data attributes; the enhancer sets them. When overlay=false none of these attributes are rendered (native scrollbar is visible). A11y: the viewport carries tabindex="0" (keyboard-scrollable) and conditionally role="region" + aria-label / aria-labelledby when a label is provided (named scrollable landmark). Each overlay rail carries role="scrollbar" + aria-controls + aria-orientation + aria-valuenow (the enhancer keeps aria-valuenow live); without them the custom rail would be decorative and AT users would depend solely on the keyboard-scrollable viewport. The platform's own scrollbar is suppressed via the lv-scrollbar-hide class (scroll-area.css: scrollbar-width:none + ::-webkit-scrollbar{display:none}) only when overlay=true. EXTRA ATTRIBUTES (two channels, by trust -- the XSS decision rule): attrs (TRUSTED, raw) -- $unsafe, STATIC author-typed strings only (wire directives, target/rel). NEVER interpolate a per-row DB-derived value into attrs. dataAttrs (SAFE, escaped) -- Map of dynamic data-* attributes. Each entry renders as data-="" with the VALUE escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute. Per-row DB values go here and ONLY here. Params: gg.jte.Content content the scrollable content (required). String height = null CSS max-height value e.g. "400px"; null = no max-height. String width = null CSS max-width value; null = inherits. String orientation = "vertical" "vertical" | "horizontal" | "both". boolean overlay = false true = hide native scrollbar + activate overlay rails. int hideDelay = 1000 ms before overlay rail hides after scroll stop (overlay=true only). String type = "hover" "hover" | "always" | "scroll" -- overlay rail visibility policy. String ariaLabel = null aria-label on viewport; triggers role="region" + landmark. String ariaLabelledBy = null aria-labelledby referencing an external element. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the scroll-area root. String viewportCssClass = "" extra utility classes on the viewport div. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only. java.util.Map dataAttrs = {} SAFE dynamic data-* (value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute). Usage: @@template.lievit.scroll-area(height = "400px", content = @@`

Long content...

`) @@template.lievit.scroll-area(orientation = "horizontal", content = @@`
Wide

items.docs: Copies scroll-area.jte and scroll-area.css to your project. Import the CSS alongside the template. When overlay=false (default) the native scrollbar is used: no JS, CSP-clean, token-styled. When overlay=true the native scrollbar is hidden (lv-scrollbar-hide class) and custom overlay rails are activated by the scroll-area.enhancer.ts (install it once via installScrollArea(runtime) in your main.ts). Rail visibility is controlled by data-type (hover/always/scroll) via CSS selectors in scroll-area.css; the enhancer sets data-scrolling and data-pointer-over on the root. Thumb drag is handled via pointer events with setPointerCapture. TOKENS_NEEDED (not yet in lievit-tokens.css): --lv-z-scrollbar:10, --lv-color-scrollbar-thumb, --lv-color-scrollbar-thumb-active, --lv-color-scrollbar-track (currently falling back to --lv-color-border / --lv-color-border-hover).

items.name: section
items.description: A titled, optionally collapsible container that groups fields / entries under a heading (v-next). Filament Section traits as server-pure variants: collapsible/collapsed (native details/summary), compact (tighter padding), divided (ruled header/body line), aside (header in a side rail). Four surface variants (default/outlined/elevated/ghost). A heading makes it a labelled landmark (aria-labelledby). Plain HTML + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies the section partial to your JTE root at lievit/section.jte, reached as @template.lievit.section(heading="...", content=@`...`). A non-collapsible section is a plain <section> + heading; pass collapsible=true (and collapsed=true to start closed) for a native <details> disclosure. compact tightens padding (--lv-space-3 vs --lv-space-4), divided rules a border-top between header and body, aside puts the header in a side rail grid. variant (default/outlined/elevated/ghost) tints the surface. The chevron rotate-on-open is opt-in via one adopter CSS rule keyed off [data-slot="section"][open] .lv-section-chevron; without it the section still opens + closes. Static ARIA goes in attrs; per-row dynamic data-* goes in dataAttrs (escaped). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: separator
items.description: A visual and semantic dividing line between content sections. Rendered as an <hr> element (horizontal or vertical) which carries role=separator implicitly in all browsers -- zero manual role attribute needed. Three line-stroke variants (default=solid, dashed, dotted). Optional centred label text replaces the <hr> with a flex-row [line][label][line] layout (the Ant Design Divider children use case). decorative=false by default (semantic); set decorative=true to hide the line from the a11y tree via role=presentation. No JS, no enhancer, no client state. CSP-clean. Tokens: --lv-color-border (line colour), --lv-border-width (stroke), --lv-space-4 (block margin), --lv-space-2 (inline margin for vertical), --lv-space-3 (label padding + flanking gap), --lv-text-sm + --lv-color-muted + --lv-font-sans + --lv-font-normal (label typography).

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui separator partial -- a visual and semantic dividing line between content sections. TIER: PARTIAL presentational -> JTE partial (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). A separator holds zero client state; the server renders the dividing line directly as an
(horizontal or vertical) or as a flex-row label variant. Zero JS, zero enhancer, trivially server-first. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): a11y source: WAI-ARIA 1.2 separator role (non-focusable, document-structure variant): https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-1.2/#separator HTML-ARIA implicit role mapping for
: https://www.w3.org/TR/html-aria/#el-hr The
element carries role="separator" IMPLICITLY in all conformant browsers and AT -- no manual role attribute needed. We prefer
over a div-with-role because native semantics are free and robust; a div-with-role requires an explicit role attribute on every branch including the decorative variant. The label variant (Ant Design Divider "children" use case) uses a
because the
element is void (cannot contain children); the outer container takes the explicit role and aria-label carries the accessible name. Inventory reference: Ant Design Divider (orientation, label in the middle, dashed/solid). Styling: original generation over --lv-* (OKLCH) tokens; look inspired by Tailwind UI. A11y (WAI-ARIA separator, non-focusable document-structure variant): -
carries role="separator" implicitly (zero manual role needed). - aria-orientation="horizontal" | "vertical" emitted on the element. The horizontal value is the spec default but we emit it explicitly for AT clarity. - decorative=true => role="presentation" (explicit, overrides the implicit
role), no aria-orientation (presentation elements carry no ARIA state properties). Default is decorative=false (semantic) because silently erasing the role is the worse error; use decorative=true only when the surrounding structure already communicates the grouping and the line is purely cosmetic. - label variant:
The two flanking line spans carry aria-hidden="true" (pure decoration; the container aria-label is the accessible name). Label variant is horizontal-only. - Not focusable: tabindex is never set. This component has an empty keyboard map. - No aria-valuenow / aria-valuemin / aria-valuemax: those belong on the focusable resizable pane splitter (resizable-panes, S2), not on a static document separator. Params: String orientation = "horizontal" "horizontal" | "vertical" -- the axis the line runs along; sets aria-orientation + layout. String variant = "default" "default" | "dashed" | "dotted" -- the line-stroke style. default=solid. Colour intent is always from --lv-color-border (no success/warning/destructive). boolean decorative = false true => role="presentation" (erased from a11y tree, no aria-orientation); use when the page structure already communicates grouping. String label = null optional centred label text (e.g. "OR", "Section A"); replaces the
with the flex-row label variant. Label is horizontal-only (vertical ignores it). String labelPosition = "center" "left" | "center" | "right" -- shifts the flex ratios so the label appears at the chosen edge. Ignored when label is null or orientation=vertical. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root element. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only (e.g. id="section-break-1"). NEVER feed per-row DB-derived values here (that channel is wireArgs/dataAttrs on WIRE components; this PARTIAL has no dataAttrs channel). Usage: @@template.lievit.separator() @@template.lievit.separator(orientation = "vertical") @@template.lievit.separator(variant = "dashed") @@template.lievit.separator(label = "OR") @@template.lievit.separator(label = "Section A", labelPosition = "left") @@template.lievit.separator(decorative = true) --%> @param String orientation = "horizontal" @param String variant = "default" @param boolean decorative = false @param String label = null @param String labelPosition = "center" @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" !{var isVertical = "vertical".equals(orientation);} !{var hasLabel = label != null && !isVertical;} !{var variantClass = switch (variant) { case "dashed" -> "border-dashed"; case "dotted" -> "border-dotted"; default -> "border-solid"; };} @if(hasLabel) <%-- Label variant:
replaces the void
. The two flanking spans are aria-hidden decoration; the accessible name comes from aria-label on the container. labelPosition shifts flex ratios: left=>left span flex-none (zero width), center=>both flex-1, right=>right span flex-none (zero width). --%> !{var leftClass = "left".equals(labelPosition) ? "hidden" : "flex-1 border-t " + variantClass;} !{var rightClass = "right".equals(labelPosition) ? "hidden" : "flex-1 border-t " + variantClass;}
${label}
@elseif(isVertical) <%-- Vertical: inline-block, self-stretch in flex row, border-left as the stroke. The adopter controls the container height; the component does not impose a fixed height. --%>
@else <%-- Horizontal (default): block full-width line, border-top as the stroke. --%>
@endif

items.docs: Copies the separator partial to your JTE root at lievit/separator.jte, reached as @template.lievit.separator(). Renders an <hr> element whose role=separator is implicit -- no manual role attribute needed. Pass orientation="vertical" for an inline full-height rule in a flex row. Pass variant="dashed" or variant="dotted" to change the stroke style (default is solid). Pass label="OR" for the [line][label][line] layout; labelPosition="left" | "center" | "right" shifts the label (default center). Pass decorative=true to erase the element from the a11y tree (role=presentation) when the page structure already communicates the grouping. Extra STATIC author-typed HTML attributes via attrs ($unsafe, trusted raw -- never per-row DB values). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: sheet
items.description: A headless CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED overlay panel that slides in from a viewport edge (right | left | top | bottom). UNCONTROLLED (default): the trigger is a real <button command="show-modal" commandfor> (browser invoker command API); the browser opens the <dialog> as a native modal (top-layer, ::backdrop, focus-trap, Escape) with zero JS; every close path is a <form method="dialog"> submit. CONTROLLED (open param set by the caller's @Wire field): pass open + closeAction; the dialog renders with the HTML `open` attribute when open=true; a scrim covers the viewport; data-lievit-focus-trap activates the shared focus-trap enhancer (Tab cycling, scroll-lock, Esc fires closeAction); when open=false the dialog is hidden. Closable controls the X button + scrim-click + Esc together. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal): role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby/aria-describedby, icon-only close button with aria-label. Content slots: content (owned body, never a slot), title + subtitle (labelled panel), footer (actions). Placements right/left/top/bottom; sizes sm/md/lg/xl/full. scrollBody keeps header/footer sticky while body scrolls. No Java component, no @Wire field.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui sheet partial -- a headless CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED overlay panel that slides in from a viewport edge (right | left | top | bottom). TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (focus-trap.enhancer.ts). No Java component, no @Wire field, no _component/_instance params. The open-state belongs to the CALLER. CONTROLLED / UNCONTROLLED doctrine (architecture contract, "Overlay & stateful primitives"): UNCONTROLLED (default; closeAction = null): The trigger is a real ` ) Controlled (server-owned open, e.g. a form that stays open on validation errors): @@template.lievit.sheet( id = "filter-sheet", placement = "left", size = "sm", title = "Filters", subtitle = "Refine the table results.", content = @@`...`, footer = @@``, closable = true, open = myForm.filterOpen(), closeAction = "closeFilters" ) --%> @param String id = "lv-sheet" @param gg.jte.Content trigger = null @param gg.jte.Content content @param String title = null @param String subtitle = null @param gg.jte.Content footer = null @param String placement = "right" @param String size = "md" @param boolean closable = true @param boolean scrollBody = true @param boolean open = false @param String closeAction = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var titleId = id + "-title";} !{var subtitleId = id + "-subtitle";} !{var hasTitle = title != null && !title.isEmpty();} !{var hasSubtitle = subtitle != null && !subtitle.isEmpty();} !{var isControlled = closeAction != null && !closeAction.isEmpty();} !{var isVertical = placement.equals("top") || placement.equals("bottom");} !{var panelWidth = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "20rem"; case "lg" -> "40rem"; case "xl" -> "50rem"; case "full" -> "100vw"; default -> "30rem"; };} !{var panelHeight = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "15rem"; case "lg" -> "30rem"; case "xl" -> "37.5rem"; case "full" -> "100vh"; default -> "22.5rem"; };} !{var panelPos = switch (placement) { case "left" -> "inset:0 auto 0 0; height:100dvh; width:" + panelWidth + "; border-right:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; case "top" -> "inset:0 0 auto 0; width:100vw; height:" + panelHeight + "; border-bottom:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; case "bottom" -> "inset:auto 0 0 0; width:100vw; height:" + panelHeight + "; border-top:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; default -> "inset:0 0 0 auto; height:100dvh; width:" + panelWidth + "; border-left:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; };} @if(trigger != null) <%-- Trigger button: uncontrolled path -- browser opens the as a modal (no JS). --%> @endif @if(isControlled && open) <%-- Controlled-mode scrim: full-viewport cover behind the panel when open=true. --%>
@endif <%-- The element. UNCONTROLLED mode (isControlled=false): - No hidden attribute (browser manages show/hide via showModal()). - No data-lievit-focus-trap (browser native modal trap handles Tab cycling). - No data-lievit-escape-action (browser cancels on Esc natively). - Panel does NOT carry fixed positioning classes (browser top-layer handles it). CONTROLLED mode (isControlled=true): - hidden="${isControlled && !open}" (smart attr: present=hidden when false; absent when true/visible). - data-lievit-focus-trap="" (enhancer activates trap + scroll-lock on mount). - data-lievit-escape-action="${closeAction}" only when closable (must-act: omit when not). - Panel needs fixed edge-anchoring CSS because is not in the top-layer. --%> <%-- Header: title + subtitle + optional close X button. --%>
@if(hasTitle)

${title}

@endif @if(hasSubtitle)

${subtitle}

@endif
@if(closable) <%-- Close button: icon-only -> aria-label is mandatory (APG rule). --%> <%-- Controlled path: l:click="${closeAction}" fires the wire round-trip. --%> <%-- Uncontrolled path:
@else
@endif @endif
<%-- Body: OWNED server-rendered content (never a native ; the projection contract). --%>
${content}
@if(footer != null) <%-- Footer: caller-provided action buttons. --%>
${footer}
@endif

items.docs: Copies the sheet partial to your JTE root at lievit/sheet.jte, reached as @template.lievit.sheet(...). UNCONTROLLED (default): pass a `trigger` content slot and a unique `id` -- the browser opens and closes the panel natively with zero JS (command="show-modal"). CONTROLLED: pass `open` (your @Wire boolean) and `closeAction` (the wire action name) to let the server own the open-state; the focus-trap.enhancer.ts handles the focus cycle + scroll-lock + Esc. Both modes support: `title` + `subtitle` (labelled panel), `footer` (action buttons), `placement` (right|left|top|bottom), `size` (sm|md|lg|xl|full), `closable` (X + Esc + scrim, default true), `scrollBody` (body scrolls independently, default true). Install the focus-trap enhancer when using controlled mode.

items.name: sheet
items.description: A modal side panel that slides in from any of the four viewport edges (the richer, shadcn-shaped generalisation of drawer): structured header/description/footer + a built-in Lucide close button. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): open state lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the body + footer are owned template regions, not slots. Replaces the <lv-sheet> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.

files.content:
@import dev.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui sheet WIRE template -- a modal side panel that slides in from any of the four viewport edges (the richer, shadcn-shaped generalisation of drawer) whose open state lives server-side in SheetComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/sheet/SheetComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires `open` and the backdrop / close button fire `close` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the header / body / footer are NOT JTE Content slots supplied at call time: they are OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body + footer regions directly (this is copy-in source), or drives them from @Wire fields / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children". STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal) pattern, Radix Dialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the panel is role="dialog" + aria-modal="true"; - aria-labelledby -> the title id when a heading is set, else aria-label="Sheet"; - aria-describedby -> the description id when a description is set; - the `side` field positions the panel against one edge (right/left/top/bottom); - a built-in close button (showClose) renders the vendored Lucide `x` via @template.icon; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree. Open-state is SERVER (wire); focus-trap + Escape are a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement (data-lv-sheet hooks). Escape + the backdrop dispatch the `close` action. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Dialog/Modal): div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true] labelled by the title + described by the description; closed overlay removed from the a11y tree via `hidden`. Params: open boolean modal open state (server-owned) side String edge to anchor: "right" | "left" | "top" | "bottom" heading String panel title; wired to aria-labelledby (empty omits the title) description String panel description; wired to aria-describedby (empty omits it) showClose boolean render the top-right close button dismissible boolean when true the backdrop + Escape route to `close` (locked server-side) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, then put YOUR body + footer markup in the owned regions):

Your owned, server-rendered body here.

--%> @param boolean open @param String side @param String heading @param String description @param boolean showClose @param boolean dismissible @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var titleId = "lv-sheet-title";} !{var descId = "lv-sheet-desc";} !{var hasHeading = heading != null && !heading.isEmpty();} !{var hasDesc = description != null && !description.isEmpty();} !{ var edge = side == null ? "right" : side; String panelPos; switch (edge) { case "left" -> panelPos = "inset: 0 auto 0 0; width: min(90vw, 24rem); border-right: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; case "top" -> panelPos = "inset: 0 0 auto 0; max-height: 85vh; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; case "bottom" -> panelPos = "inset: auto 0 0 0; max-height: 85vh; border-top: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; default -> panelPos = "inset: 0 0 0 auto; width: min(90vw, 24rem); border-left: 1px solid var(--lv-color-border);"; } }
@if(dismissible) @endif
@if(showClose) @endif
@if(hasHeading)

${heading}

@endif @if(hasDesc)

${description}

@endif
<%-- Owned body region: replace this with your sheet body markup (server-rendered). --%>

Sheet content.

<%-- Owned footer region: replace this with your action buttons (server-rendered). --%>

items.docs: Register SheetComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.sheet(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` to show it and `close` (backdrop / Escape / close button) to hide it; set `side` to 'right' (default) | 'left' | 'top' | 'bottom', `heading` + `description` for the labelled header, `showClose=false` to hide the close button. Put your sheet body + footer markup directly in the data-lv-sheet-body / data-lv-sheet-footer regions of the copied template (owned source, NOT slots). Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-sheet-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the server `close` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=close button), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). For the minimal heading+body edge panel use the drawer wire instead. Adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: sidebar
items.description: The application sidebar rendered server-side: a <nav> landmark of real <a href> nav items (groups -> menu-items -> collapsible sub-items via native <details>), aria-current on the active entry, plus a collapse/expand rail and a mobile off-canvas overlay. Ships the shadcn compound set: SidebarRail (a thin edge toggle), SidebarInset (a <main> body, required for the inset variant), SidebarMenuAction + SidebarGroupAction (trailing action affordances), the none/floating/inset variants (data-variant), and a Cmd/Ctrl+B toggle shortcut. Server-first PARTIAL + a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (ADR-0012, Wave 3); replaces the <lv-sidebar> Lit island. The nav is server-rendered (which item is current is a server fact); the collapse is pure client cosmetic with localStorage persistence, never a wire round-trip. shadcn Sidebar anatomy (header/content/footer, group/menu/menu-sub), WAI-ARIA navigation landmark.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui sidebar partial -- the application sidebar: a
landmark of real nav items (composed from sidebar/group + sidebar/item) PLUS a collapse/expand control. Server-first PARTIAL + a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (sidebar.enhancer.ts), NOT a Lit island (ADR-0012, Wave 3). Pairs with registry/jte/sidebar/{group,item}.jte + registry/jte/sidebar.enhancer.ts. TIER DECISION (why PARTIAL + enhancer, NOT WIRE): the blueprint classes sidebar as WIRE(PARTIAL): "nav is PARTIAL, collapse is tiny vanilla/wire state". Both halves resolve to server-pure: (1) NAV: real entries. Which one is current is a fact the SERVER already knows (the route being rendered), passed in as the item's `active` -> aria-current="page" -- not client state. Groups / menu-items / sub-items come in via @param Content composed from the sub-partials, filled by the caller from its typed model (gest's shell.jte already iterates a typed NavTab list into s the same way). No data is hardcoded in the partial. (2) COLLAPSE: a rail-width toggle + mobile off-canvas open/close. This is PURE CLIENT COSMETIC: there is nothing for the server to decide when a user narrows their own sidebar, so round-tripping it over the wire would be chat for no gain (the same reasoning input-otp's enhancer uses). Per the blueprint's own fallback -- "pure client cosmetic -> a registry:jte partial + a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS toggle (localStorage persistence), NO Lit" -- the collapse is the enhancer, and the choice persists to localStorage. There is therefore no @Wire field, no action, no wire component. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn Sidebar anatomy (header / content / footer; group -> menu -> menu-item -> menu-sub), the model the dropped Lit island cited, rendered as a
landmark +
    /
  • lists of real anchors. Desktop collapse is an icon rail (width animates to the icon width, labels/badges/group-headings hide via CSS on the collapsed state). Mobile is an off-canvas overlay: the SAME slide-in + backdrop + Escape/focus-return pattern as the drawer wire (Wave 2), but TS-toggled here because it is cosmetic, not server state -- reuse, not reinvent. No No light-DOM slot elements: items arrive via @param Content (the sub-partials), header/footer via their own Content slots, never slotted light-DOM children (the silent-slot bug the pivot kills). A11y (shadcn Sidebar + WAI-ARIA):
    landmark; the collapse trigger is a real
    <%-- The header bar always renders: it carries the collapse/open trigger, which MUST exist with or without `header` content (else there is no toggle on desktop and mobile cannot open -- the decoupling fix). When `header` is null the bar shows just the trigger. --%>
    @if(header != null)
    ${header}
    @else
    @endif
    ${content}
    @if(footer != null)
    ${footer}
    @endif

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui sidebar/group partial -- one labelled section of a sidebar: an optional heading + a
    menu of sidebar/item entries (Wave 3, server-first, no JS, no state). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A group is pure markup; its items arrive via the @param Content slot, filled by the caller from its typed model. The group heading hides when the sidebar collapses to the icon rail (CSS on the root's data-state, lv-sidebar-collapsible), so no client state lives here. STRUCTURE (shadcn Sidebar SidebarGroup model): a section with an optional uppercase heading and a
      of menu items. When the group is labelled the menu is associated to its heading via aria-labelledby so AT announces the section name. A11y: the heading is a real
      styled as a section label; the menu is a
        whose items are real (sidebar/item). When labelled, the
          is aria-labelledby the heading id. Params: String label = "" optional section heading (hidden on the collapsed icon rail). gg.jte.Content content the menu items (sidebar/item entries). gg.jte.Content action = null optional trailing action by the heading (sidebar/group-action), pinned to the group's top-right; the group div is its positioning ctx. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the group. "lv-sidebar-group-" + label.toLowerCase().replaceAll("[^a-z0-9]+", "-") : null;}

TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A leaf item is a plain anchor; navigation is the browser's, and which one is current is a server fact (`active` -> aria-current="page"), never client state. A parent item (one carrying a `content` sub-tree) discloses its children with a native
/ -- the CSP-clean, zero-JS disclosure (the same JS-off pattern gest uses for its dropdowns); `open` is the server-rendered hint (open it when a child is current). No wire, no enhancer for the sub-tree:

Params: String key a stable key (used in the disclosure id). String label the visible entry text. String href = "" leaf destination; required for a leaf, ignored for a parent. String rel = "" leaf only: the anchor rel attribute (e.g. "noopener" for external links). "var(--lv-color-sidebar-accent)" : "transparent") + "; font-weight: " + (active ? "var(--lv-font-medium)" : "var(--lv-font-normal)") + ";";} @if(isParent)

<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui sidebar/rail partial -- the thin clickable edge that toggles the sidebar collapse
  (shadcn Sidebar SidebarRail). Server-first PARTIAL; the click behaviour is wired in code by
  sidebar.enhancer.ts (the strict CSP refuses inline on* handlers), exactly like the header
  trigger -- the rail is a second, edge-docked affordance for the SAME collapse toggle.

  TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The rail has no state of its own: it is a
  <button> the enhancer binds to the root's collapse toggle (data-sidebar="rail" is the
  enhancer's hook, mirroring the data-sidebar="rail" attribute shadcn uses). It lives INSIDE
  the sidebar <nav> (place it after the content), absolutely positioned to the docked edge by
  the enhancer's injected CSS, so its position follows data-side without per-call styling.

  STRUCTURE (shadcn SidebarRail): a real <button aria-label> docked to the inner edge of the
  rail; tabindex=-1 (it duplicates the header trigger, which stays in the tab order), so it is
  a mouse affordance, not a redundant tab stop. Hidden on mobile (the off-canvas overlay has
  the backdrop + trigger instead) via the enhancer's media query.

  A11y: a real <button aria-label> the platform exposes; tabindex=-1 keeps it out of the tab
  order (the header trigger is the keyboard path). title mirrors the label for a hover hint.

  Params:
    String label = "Toggle sidebar"  accessible name + hover title for the rail button.
    String cssClass = ""             extra utility classes on the rail.

  Usage (place inside the sidebar content tree, typically last):
    @@template.lievit.sidebar.rail()
--%>
@param String label = "Toggle sidebar"
@param String cssClass = ""
<button
    type="button"
    data-slot="sidebar-rail"
    data-sidebar="rail"
    data-action="click->lv-sidebar#toggle"
    aria-label="${label}"
    title="${label}"
    tabindex="-1"
    class="lv-sidebar-rail ${cssClass}"
    style="position: absolute; inset-block: 0; z-index: 20; width: var(--lv-space-4); border: 0; padding: 0; background: transparent; cursor: pointer;"
></button>

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui sidebar/inset partial -- the
content area that sits beside the sidebar (shadcn Sidebar SidebarInset). It is the page body the sidebar docks next to; it is REQUIRED for the `inset` variant, where shadcn floats the main content as an inset card (margin + rounded corners + shadow) against the sidebar surface. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A plain
landmark wrapper; no state, no JS. The inset variant's visual treatment (the floating card) is applied by the enhancer's injected CSS keyed on the root's data-variant, so this partial stays pure markup. Pair it with the sidebar root rendered with variant = "inset" and lay them out side by side in a flex row, e.g.
@@template.lievit.sidebar(variant = "inset", content = ...) @@template.lievit.sidebar.inset(content = ...)

that grows to fill the row (flex: 1) and carries the page background. The enhancer's `[data-variant="inset"] ~ ...` rule (a sibling selector off the sidebar root) gives the adjacent inset its margin / radius / shadow only under the inset variant; the default and floating variants leave the inset flush. A11y: a real
landmark, so AT users get a "main content" region distinct from the
sidebar landmark. Params: gg.jte.Content content the page body. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the
. Usage: @@template.lievit.sidebar.inset(content = @@`

TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. No state of its own. It is positioned by the enhancer's injected CSS (absolute, pinned to the row end) keyed on data-slot="sidebar-menu-action"; the menu item's
  • is the positioning context, so pass this into the item's `action` slot. The optional `showOnHover` mirrors shadcn: the action is hidden until the row is hovered or a descendant has focus (so the row stays clean), revealed on interaction; without it the action is always visible. Hidden on the collapsed icon rail (lv-sidebar-collapsible). STRUCTURE (shadcn SidebarMenuAction): an absolutely-positioned @endif

items.docs: Copies the sidebar partial to your JTE root at lievit/sidebar.jte (reached as @template.lievit.sidebar(...)), its sub-partials at lievit/sidebar/{group,item,rail,inset,menu-action,group-action}.jte (@template.lievit.sidebar.group / .item / .rail / .inset / .menu-action / .group-action), and the CSP-clean enhancer to your alias root at lievit/sidebar.enhancer.ts. Compose the nav from your typed model: a sidebar.group per section, a sidebar.item per entry (key, label, href, icon, badge, active, disabled; pass a `content` sub-tree to make an item a collapsible parent; pass an `action` slot for a trailing sidebar.menu-action). Give a group an `action` slot for a sidebar.group-action by its heading. Drop a sidebar.rail inside the nav for an edge-docked collapse toggle. Pick a variant: sidebar (default), none, floating, or inset; for inset, lay the sidebar(variant="inset") beside a sidebar.inset(<main>) in a flex row. Set each item's `active` from the route being rendered so it carries aria-current=page. Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllSidebars() on load (and after any DOM swap); it toggles the desktop collapsed rail (persisted under storageKey via localStorage), the mobile off-canvas overlay, the rail edge, and the Cmd/Ctrl+B shortcut. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: skeleton
items.description: A shimmer-animated layout placeholder that reserves space while real content loads. Five shape presets (lines/card/avatar-row/image/button-bar), three sizes, active/inactive toggle. role=status + aria-live for AT; shimmer suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion (via skeleton.css). Zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies skeleton.jte + skeleton.css to your project. The shimmer animation uses --lv-skeleton-bg and --lv-skeleton-shimmer tokens (net-new; added to lievit-tokens.css by `lievit add skeleton`). Call as @template.lievit.skeleton(skeletonShape=..., skeletonRows=..., active=...). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: slide-over
items.description: A modal panel that slides in from a viewport edge (right | left | top | bottom) -- the shadcn Sheet, STATIC + trigger-driven, with ZERO JS. The modal partial pinned to an edge: same native HTML <dialog> seam (trigger <button command="show-modal" commandfor>, close via native <form method="dialog"> + the browser's Escape default), so the browser owns top-layer rendering, focus-trap, ::backdrop and AT-modal exposure -- CSP-clean, no enhancer. The `side` (data-side) pins + sizes the panel to the chosen edge; the markup carries the shadcn sheet-* data-slot contract. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby). Header / body / footer are OWNED Content slots, never <slot>. The static presentation shell behind the kit's edge-anchored panels: dev.lievit.kit.SlideOver (right-anchored detail) and any ModalConfig.asSlideOver(). The trigger-driven static counterpart of the server-state-held wire drawer / sheet (registry/wire/drawer/, registry/wire/sheet/), named slide-over to avoid the bare drawer/sheet wire item names. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens.
items.docs: Copies the slide-over partial to your JTE root at lievit/slide-over.jte, reached as @template.lievit.slide-over(...). Pass a unique `id` (the commandfor target), the `trigger` content and the `content` panel body (server-rendered, never a <slot>); choose the `side` ("right" | "left" | "top" | "bottom", reflected on data-side), add an optional `heading` + `description` (a labelled dialog) and a `footer` slot. Tune `closeButton`, `closeByClickingAway`. The browser owns open/close (native <dialog> showModal + Escape + focus-trap + return-focus): no JS, no enhancer. The source is yours: edit freely. For a panel whose open-state lives server-side (the SlideOver "view in panel" outcome the server drives), use the wire drawer / sheet component instead.

items.name: slider
items.description: APG Slider on a platform native input[type=range]. Single-thumb default, dual-thumb range mode, horizontal or vertical. Marks, value tooltip, disabled, aria-invalid. The slider.enhancer.ts handles --slider-pct sync, range clamp + ARIA constraint updates, and tooltip text on drag.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui slider partial -- APG Slider on a platform native input[type=range]. Single-thumb default; dual-thumb range mode (rangeMode=true); horizontal or vertical. Marks, value tooltip, disabled, aria-invalid. The enhancer handles the cases the native element does not cover: range clamp, --slider-pct sync, tooltip text update. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (slider.enhancer.ts). The native range input carries role=slider, aria-valuemin/max/now, and the full APG keyboard map (Arrow/Home/End/PageUp-Down) for free; no JS keyboard handler is needed. The enhancer's job is purely the irreducible client bits: real-time --slider-pct updates on drag/key, range-mode clamp + ARIA constraint sync, tooltip visibility toggling. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): APG Slider (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ patterns/slider/) for single-thumb; APG Slider (Multi-Thumb) (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ ARIA/apg/patterns/slider-multithumb/) for range mode. Both URLs verified 2026-06-24. The native input[type=range] is the ACCESSIBLE SPINE -- do not replace it with a div[role=slider]. The styled overlay (track rail + fill + thumb overlay + tooltip + marks) is aria-hidden="true" throughout. The initial fill percentage (--slider-pct) is computed server-side and set as an inline CSS custom property on the root; the enhancer keeps it in sync on every interaction without a round-trip. Inventory reference: Ant Design Slider (range mode, marks, tooltip, vertical, disabled) -- no code copied, original generation. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Slider + APG Slider Multi-Thumb): Roles: role="slider" is implicit on every native input[type=range]. ARIA -- single-thumb: aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax/aria-valuenow: native (from min/max/value attributes). aria-label: set from ariaLabel param when no visible label exists. aria-valuetext: set from ariaValuetext param for human-readable override. aria-orientation="vertical": emitted when orientation="vertical" (horizontal is default). ARIA -- range mode (two thumbs, APG multithumb rule): lower thumb: aria-valuemin="${min}" aria-valuemax="${valueHigh}" (upper value is its cap). upper thumb: aria-valuemin="${valueLow}" aria-valuemax="${max}" (lower value is its floor). Each thumb carries aria-label from ariaLabelLow/ariaLabelHigh. aria-valuetext per thumb from ariaValuetextLow/ariaValuetextHigh. Keyboard: Arrow/Home/End/PageUp-Down supplied by the platform for all thumbs. Enhancer only adds: range clamp on input event + aria-valuemin/max attribute sync. Focus: platform Tab focus on each native input. No trap. No roving tabindex. Tab order low-thumb -> high-thumb is constant; enhancer never re-sorts DOM. Styled overlay: aria-hidden="true" on every visual-only element (thumb div, track fill, tooltip, mark row). The overlay carries no role and no interactive ARIA. Disabled: native `disabled` on input[type=range]; overlay gets pointer-events-none. aria-invalid: the field partial sets it on a parent; CSS [aria-invalid] selectors on the track fill + thumb overlay recolour to --lv-color-destructive. The slider partial itself does NOT emit aria-invalid (it is inherited from the field wrapper). Params: String name form field name (plain form POST, not l:model). double value = 0 current value (single-thumb). double min = 0 minimum (aria-valuemin). double max = 100 maximum (aria-valuemax). double step = 1 keyboard/snap increment; 0 = continuous. boolean rangeMode = false dual-thumb range selection. double valueLow = 0 range-mode: lower thumb value. double valueHigh = 100 range-mode: upper thumb value. String orientation = "horizontal" "horizontal" | "vertical". boolean disabled = false dims + blocks activation; native disabled. java.util.List> marks = null tick marks: each map has "value" (numeric string) and "label" (may be blank for unlabelled tick). String showTooltip = "hover" "always" | "hover" | "never". String tooltipFormatter = null server-formatted label for current value; null = raw. String tooltipFormatterLow = null range-mode: label for valueLow. String tooltipFormatterHigh = null range-mode: label for valueHigh. String ariaLabel = null aria-label for single thumb. String ariaLabelLow = null range-mode: aria-label for lower thumb. String ariaLabelHigh = null range-mode: aria-label for upper thumb. String ariaValuetext = null aria-valuetext for single thumb. String ariaValuetextLow = null range-mode: aria-valuetext for lower thumb. String ariaValuetextHigh = null range-mode: aria-valuetext for upper thumb. String size = "md" "sm" | "md" | "lg". String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root wrapper. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) STATIC strings only (wire directives, data-testid static). NEVER feed per-row DB-derived values here. java.util.Map dataAttrs = {} SAFE escaped dynamic data-* on root (each value through Escape.htmlAttribute). Usage: @@template.lievit.slider(name = "prezzo", min = 0, max = 1000, step = 50, value = 250, ariaLabel = "Prezzo") @@template.lievit.slider(name = "fascia", rangeMode = true, min = 0, max = 1000, valueLow = 200, valueHigh = 800, ariaLabelLow = "Minimo", ariaLabelHigh = "Massimo") @@template.lievit.slider(name = "volume", orientation = "vertical", value = 60, ariaLabel = "Volume", showTooltip = "always") --%> @param String name = null @param double value = 0 @param double min = 0 @param double max = 100 @param double step = 1 @param boolean rangeMode = false @param double valueLow = 0 @param double valueHigh = 100 @param String orientation = "horizontal" @param boolean disabled = false @param java.util.List> marks = null @param String showTooltip = "hover" @param String tooltipFormatter = null @param String tooltipFormatterLow = null @param String tooltipFormatterHigh = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param String ariaLabelLow = null @param String ariaLabelHigh = null @param String ariaValuetext = null @param String ariaValuetextLow = null @param String ariaValuetextHigh = null @param String size = "md" @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param java.util.Map dataAttrs = java.util.Map.of() <%-- Derived booleans + orientation flag. --%> !{var isVertical = "vertical".equals(orientation);} !{var hasTicks = marks != null && !marks.isEmpty();} <%-- Fill percentage for the server-rendered initial state. Single-thumb: ((value - min) / (max - min)) * 100. Range mode: fill from valueLow to valueHigh. Guard against division by zero when min == max. --%> !{var range_ = (max - min) != 0 ? (max - min) : 1.0;} !{var pct = (int) Math.round(((value - min) / range_) * 100);} !{var pctLow = (int) Math.round(((valueLow - min) / range_) * 100);} !{var pctHigh = (int) Math.round(((valueHigh - min) / range_) * 100);} <%-- Tooltip visibility class: always=opacity-100, hover=opacity-0 (enhancer shows on hover), never=opacity-0 sr-only (hidden from layout; still in DOM for a11y backup). --%> !{var tooltipVisClass = "always".equals(showTooltip) ? "opacity-100" : "opacity-0";} !{var tooltipSrClass = "never".equals(showTooltip) ? " pointer-events-none select-none" : "";} <%-- Tooltip label displayed at server render time. --%> !{var tooltipLabel = tooltipFormatter != null ? tooltipFormatter : String.valueOf((int) value);} !{var tooltipLabelLow = tooltipFormatterLow != null ? tooltipFormatterLow : String.valueOf((int) valueLow);} !{var tooltipLabelHigh = tooltipFormatterHigh != null ? tooltipFormatterHigh : String.valueOf((int) valueHigh);} <%-- Size-specific token classes for thumb diameter and track thickness. All class strings are COMPLETE + static so the Tailwind scanner picks them up. --%> !{var thumbSize = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "var(--lv-space-3-5)"; case "lg" -> "var(--lv-space-5-5)"; default -> "var(--lv-space-4-5)"; };} !{var trackThickness = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "var(--lv-space-1)"; case "lg" -> "var(--lv-space-2)"; default -> "var(--lv-space-1-5)"; };} <%-- Root layout: horizontal = flex-row, vertical = flex-col. --%> !{var rootLayout = isVertical ? "flex flex-col items-center" : "flex flex-row items-center";} <%-- SAFE dynamic data-* fragment: build once, emit with $unsafe. JTE forbids @for in attribute-NAME position; this sidesteps it correctly. --%> !{var dataAttrs_ = new StringOutput();} !{for (var e : dataAttrs.entrySet()) { if (e.getKey() != null && e.getKey().matches("[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*")) { dataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-" + e.getKey() + "=\""); Escape.htmlAttribute(e.getValue() == null ? "" : e.getValue(), dataAttrs_); dataAttrs_.writeContent("\""); } }} !{var dataAttrsMarkup = dataAttrs_.toString();} <%-- Root wrapper: data-slot, data-size, data-orientation, data-range, --slider-pct. data-lievit-enhancer="slider" triggers the slider.enhancer.ts lifecycle hook. --slider-pct drives the fill width/height and the thumb translate via CSS. data-tooltip-formatter / data-tooltip-formatter-low / data-tooltip-formatter-high carry the formatter template to the enhancer (raw strings -- author-controlled, never DB-derived without explicit sanitization by the calling template). --%>
<%-- TRACK LAYER (aria-hidden; purely visual rail + fill) --%>
<%-- Track fill: coloured region between min and thumb (single) or between the two thumbs (range). Width/height driven by CSS --slider-pct; updated by the enhancer on drag. --%>
<%-- NATIVE INPUT LAYER: the real accessible spine. Single-thumb: one input.
