Range mode: two inputs (low then high, constant DOM order per APG multithumb rule -- the tab order must not change when thumbs cross). --%> @if(rangeMode) <%-- LOW THUMB --%> <%-- HIGH THUMB --%> @else @endif <%-- STYLED THUMB OVERLAY (aria-hidden; visual only; the real interaction is on the native input). Single thumb: position driven by --slider-pct. Range mode: low thumb at --slider-pct, high thumb at --slider-pct-high. The overlay is pointer-events-none so clicks pass through to the native input above. --%> @if(rangeMode) <%-- Low thumb overlay --%>
<%-- High thumb overlay --%>
<%-- Tooltips: range mode (low + high). Always in DOM; CSS visibility controlled by showTooltip. aria-hidden: the a11y value is on the native input via aria-valuenow/aria-valuetext. --%>
${tooltipLabelLow}
${tooltipLabelHigh}
@else <%-- Single thumb overlay --%>
<%-- Single tooltip. Always in DOM regardless of showTooltip; CSS opacity controls visibility. --%>
${tooltipLabel}
@endif <%-- MARKS ROW (aria-hidden; visual decoration only; a11y value travels via aria-valuetext). --%> @if(hasTicks)
@for(java.util.Map mark : marks) !{var markVal = mark.get("value");} !{var markLabel = mark.get("label");} !{var markPct = markVal != null ? (int) Math.round(((Double.parseDouble(markVal) - min) / range_) * 100) : 0;}
<%-- Tick dot --%> <%-- Tick label: only when non-blank --%> @if(markLabel != null && !markLabel.isBlank()) ${markLabel} @endif
@endfor
@endif

items.docs: Copies the slider partial to your JTE root at lievit/slider.jte. The native range input is the accessible spine (role=slider + Arrow/Home/End keyboard for free). The optional slider.enhancer.ts keeps --slider-pct in sync and handles range-mode clamp + APG multithumb ARIA constraint updates. Single-thumb: pass value/min/max/step + ariaLabel. Range mode: set rangeMode=true + valueLow/valueHigh + ariaLabelLow/ariaLabelHigh. Marks: pass a List of Map(value,label). Tooltip: showTooltip="always|hover|never".

items.name: spinner
items.description: An animated SVG ring (background circle + foreground arc) that signals an indeterminate background operation. Two variants (default/primary), three sizes (sm/md/lg), role=status + aria-live for AT, animate-spin + motion-reduce:animate-none for reduced-motion. Zero JS, CSP-clean, inline-context safe (<span> root).

files.content:
@import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui spinner partial -- an animated SVG ring that signals an indeterminate background operation in progress. TIER: PARTIAL presentational -> JTE partial (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). Pure display, no client state: the server renders it or not; the morph removes it when done. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): an SVG ring (background circle + foreground arc, both stroke=currentColor). The arc uses stroke-dasharray to produce a 300-degree chasing arc; the SVG rotates via Tailwind animate-spin (a standard rotate keyframe). Ant Design Spin and react-aria both use a rotating SVG ring for their spinner; lievit generates an original variant over --lv-* tokens. prefers-reduced-motion: animate-spin suppressed via motion-reduce:animate-none (Tailwind built-in). A11y (WAI-ARIA role=status): role="status" + aria-live="polite" + aria-atomic="true" + aria-label on the root (explicit mirrors of the implicit defaults for cross-AT consistency, per MDN recommendation). A sr-only span carries the label text inside the live region so aria-atomic="true" announces the full text. The decorative SVG is aria-hidden="true". No focus management -- a loading indicator must NOT receive focus on update (WCAG 4.1.3). https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Roles/status_role https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/status-messages SVG ring anatomy: background track circle (opacity 0.25) + foreground arc. Circumference = 2 * pi * 10 = 62.832; dasharray = 31.416 (half circumference = 180-deg arc); dashoffset = 10.472 (1/6 of circumference) shifts the arc start, giving a 300-deg visible arc. The SVG is sized via width/height attributes (sm=16px, md=24px, lg=40px) over a fixed viewBox="0 0 24 24" so the proportions are identical at every scale. VARIANT colours: default uses --lv-color-muted-fg (neutral busy); primary uses --lv-color-primary (branded busy, e.g. inside a primary button). Both derive from the --lv-* token set so they retheme automatically. Inline context: the root is a (not
) so the spinner can be composed inside inline or flex contexts (e.g. inside a switch thumb span or a toast flex row) without generating an invalid block-inside-inline nesting. role=status on a is valid per ARIA 1.2 (roles are orthogonal to HTML element semantics). Params: String size = "md" "sm" (16px) | "md" (24px, default) | "lg" (40px) -- ring diameter. String variant = "default" "default" (--lv-color-muted-fg) | "primary" (--lv-color-primary). String label = "Loading..." accessible name; visually hidden (sr-only). Pass "" when the spinner is inside an aria-hidden parent (decorative context). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only. Map dataAttrs = {} SAFE dynamic data-* (value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute). Usage: @@template.lievit.spinner() @@template.lievit.spinner(size = "lg", variant = "primary", label = "Caricamento in corso") @@template.lievit.spinner(size = "sm", label = "") --%> @param String size = "md" @param String variant = "default" @param String label = "Loading…" @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param java.util.Map dataAttrs = java.util.Map.of() !{var svgSize = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "16"; case "lg" -> "40"; default -> "24"; };} !{var strokeColor = "primary".equals(variant) ? "var(--lv-color-primary)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} !{var dataAttrs_ = new StringOutput();} !{for (var e : dataAttrs.entrySet()) { if (e.getKey() != null && e.getKey().matches("[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*")) { dataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-" + e.getKey() + "=\""); Escape.htmlAttribute(e.getValue() == null ? "" : e.getValue(), dataAttrs_); dataAttrs_.writeContent("\""); } }} !{var dataAttrsMarkup = dataAttrs_.toString();} ${label}

items.docs: Copies the spinner partial to your JTE root at lievit/spinner.jte, reached as @template.lievit.spinner(size=..., variant=..., label=...). The root is a <span role=status> so it composes safely inside inline or flex contexts. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: stat-card
items.description: A single-metric KPI display card: prominent label + value + optional trend indicator (direction + delta magnitude) + leading/trailing/footer slots + optional whole-card link. Purely presentational PARTIAL; the server pre-formats the value. Variant drives a left-border accent colour; size drives the value font prominence (sm=2xl, md=3xl, lg=4xl). No enhancer, no client state.
items.docs: Copies the stat-card partial to your JTE root at lievit/stat-card.jte, reached as @template.lievit.stat-card(title="Nuovi contatti", value="284"). Params: title (required), value (required, pre-formatted by controller), prefix/suffix for inline unit symbols, trend (up|down|neutral|none) + trendValue for the delta annotation, variant (default|info|success|warning|destructive) for a left-border accent, size (sm|md|lg) for typographic prominence, href to make the whole card a navigable link, loading=true for the skeleton state, and three content slots (leading, trailing, footer). Wire HTMX refresh via the attrs channel (trusted raw, static strings only). NOTE: --lv-text-4xl (used by size=lg) must be defined in your token file; it is not present in the base lievit-tokens.css as of v-next wave 1.

items.name: switch
items.description: A binary toggle control following the WAI-ARIA APG Switch pattern. Primary variant: <button role="switch" aria-checked> — the native button gives role + Enter/Space + focus for free, no JS. Form variant (asCheckbox=true): <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> for submitting with a form. The thumb slide is driven by CSS attribute selectors (zero JS, CSP-clean). Height-based sm/md/lg sizes (18/22/28px track, --lv-space-5/6/7 thumb) align flush with form controls in a toolbar row. Supports loading state (spinner in thumb, aria-busy), inner on/off labels (aria-hidden, Ant Design style), leading/trailing Content slots for label composition, and the full SAFE wire escaping channel (wireClick + wireArgs + dataAttrs) matching button.jte. The toggle action is wired by the consuming WIRE template; the partial renders the styled snapshot. Net-new tokens: --lv-space-7, --lv-opacity-disabled, --lv-motion-switch-thumb (with prefers-reduced-motion suppression).
items.docs: Copies the switch partial to your JTE root at lievit/switch.jte, reached as @template.lievit.switch(checked=..., ariaLabel=...). The partial renders a <button role="switch" aria-checked> (primary) or <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> (asCheckbox=true) with a sliding thumb. Size is height-based: sm (18px track), md (22px, default), lg (28px). Wire a toggle SAFELY with wireClick="toggle" plus wireArgs=Map.of("id", row.id()) — same escaping channel as button.jte, no hand-rolled $unsafe needed. Pass leading/trailing (gg.jte.Content) to compose a visible label. Pass ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy — a switch without an accessible name is an a11y violation. Inner on/off labels (onLabel/offLabel) are aria-hidden; they are decorative, not the accessible name. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: table
items.description: Composable table primitive set (shadcn-style, NOT data-driven). Seven sub-partials: table.jte (the <table> scroll wrapper), table/header.jte (<thead>, optional stickyHeader), table/body.jte (<tbody>), table/footer.jte (<tfoot>), table/row.jte (<tr> with ariaSelected, ariaRowIndex, state, hoverable, striped, bordered, dataRowId), table/head.jte (<th scope> with ariaSort for sortable column headers), table/cell.jte (<td>), table/caption.jte (<caption> for the table accessible name). The caller owns the row and cell loop; composability matches shadcn Table / TableHeader / TableBody / TableRow / TableHead / TableCell / TableCaption / TableFooter. WAI-ARIA APG Table pattern: native elements, scope attributes, aria-sort on <th> (not on inner button), aria-selected / aria-rowindex on rows. The data-driven HTMX-sortable variant is the separate data-table.jte, which COMPOSES these primitives. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012). Zero client state, CSP-clean, --lv-* tokens only.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui table partial -- the scroll wrapper (composable set root). TIER: PARTIAL presentational (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). Zero client state. The table composable set mirrors shadcn Table / TableHeader / TableBody / TableRow / TableHead / TableCell / TableCaption / TableFooter: the caller owns the row and cell loop, this partial is the
wrapper + horizontal-scroll container. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - WAI-ARIA APG Table pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/table/). Native
supplies implicit role="table"; scope, colspan, rowspan, and browser column-width algorithms come for free -- NEVER a
. - Composable set (shadcn-style): table.jte (wrapper) + table/{header,body,footer, row,head,cell,caption}.jte. The caller composes, owns the loop; this partial provides ONLY the chrome. data-table.jte is the separate HTMX-driven data view that COMPOSES these primitives. - Styling: ORIGINAL generation over --lv-* tokens; look inspired by Tailwind UI (no code copied). A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Table): - Native
(implicit role="table"). ariaLabel or a visible
(rendered via table.caption) is REQUIRED for the accessible name. - Keyboard: APG states "Not applicable" -- the platform handles Tab focus on any focusable child (sort buttons, checkboxes, action links). - No focus trap, no roving tabindex; static structure. Params: String ariaLabel = null aria-label on when no visible caption is composed as a child. One of ariaLabel or a table.caption child is REQUIRED for an accessible name. String ariaLabelledBy = null aria-labelledby pointing to an element id that labels the table. Mutually exclusive with ariaLabel. String ariaDescribedBy = null aria-describedby pointing to an external description element id. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the
element. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only (e.g. data-controller="..."). gg.jte.Content content the thead / tbody / tfoot / caption blocks composed by the caller. Usage: @@template.lievit.table(ariaLabel = "Q2 Listings", content = @@` @@template.lievit.table.caption(content = @@`Q2 Listings`) @@template.lievit.table.header(content = @@` @@template.lievit.table.row(content = @@` @@template.lievit.table.head(content = @@`Ref`) @@template.lievit.table.head(content = @@`Agent`) @@template.lievit.table.head(cssClass = "text-right", content = @@`Price`) `) `) @@template.lievit.table.body(content = @@` @@template.lievit.table.row(content = @@` @@template.lievit.table.cell(content = @@`A-1024`) @@template.lievit.table.cell(content = @@`Cecchetto`) @@template.lievit.table.cell(cssClass = "text-right", content = @@`240000`) `) `) `) --%> @param String ariaLabel = null @param String ariaLabelledBy = null @param String ariaDescribedBy = null @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param gg.jte.Content content
${content}

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui table.header partial -- the <thead> of the composable table set.

  TIER: PARTIAL (sub-partial; composed inside @@template.lievit.table).

  STRUCTURE: native <thead> (implicit role="rowgroup"). Holds one or more
  @@template.lievit.table.row blocks of @@template.lievit.table.head cells.
  A sticky-header mode is supported: the caller passes stickyHeader=true and
  provides a scroll-area wrapper; this partial sets position:sticky + a bottom
  shadow separator + z-index above scrolling rows.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Table):
    - Native <thead> (implicit role="rowgroup").
    - No ARIA role override needed: the semantic is correct by element choice.

  Params:
    boolean stickyHeader  = false  position:sticky + z-var(--lv-z-sticky) on this
                                   element. Caller must supply an overflow scroll wrapper.
    String cssClass       = ""     extra utility classes on <thead>.
    gg.jte.Content content         the header row(s): @@template.lievit.table.row blocks.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.table.header(content = @@`
      @@template.lievit.table.row(content = @@`
        @@template.lievit.table.head(content = @@`Name`)
        @@template.lievit.table.head(content = @@`Price`)
      `)
    `)
    @@template.lievit.table.header(stickyHeader = true, content = @@`...`)
--%>
@param boolean stickyHeader = false
@param String cssClass = ""
@param gg.jte.Content content
!{var _theadStyle = stickyHeader
    ? "position:sticky;top:0;z-index:var(--lv-z-sticky);background:var(--lv-color-bg);box-shadow:0 1px 0 0 var(--lv-color-border);"
    : "";}
<thead
  data-slot="table-header"
  data-sticky-header="${stickyHeader ? "true" : null}"
  class="${cssClass}"
  style="${_theadStyle}background-color:${stickyHeader ? "var(--lv-color-bg)" : "color-mix(in srgb, var(--lv-color-surface) 35%, var(--lv-color-bg))"};"
>${content}</thead>

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui table.body partial -- the <tbody> of the composable table set.

  TIER: PARTIAL (sub-partial; composed inside @@template.lievit.table).

  STRUCTURE: native <tbody> (implicit role="rowgroup"). HTMX swaps this element
  (+ the pagination region) on sort / page / filter operations when data-table.jte
  composes this set with HTMX wiring.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Table):
    - Native <tbody> (implicit role="rowgroup").

  Params:
    String cssClass       = ""     extra utility classes on <tbody>.
    gg.jte.Content content         the data rows: @@template.lievit.table.row blocks.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.table.body(content = @@`
      @@template.lievit.table.row(content = @@`...`)
    `)
--%>
@param String cssClass = ""
@param gg.jte.Content content
<%-- [&_tr:last-child]:border-b-0 -- shadcn parity: drop the last row's bottom divider. --%>
<tbody data-slot="table-body" class="[&_tr:last-child]:border-b-0 ${cssClass}">${content}</tbody>

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui table.footer partial -- the <tfoot> of the composable table set.

  TIER: PARTIAL (sub-partial; composed inside @@template.lievit.table).

  STRUCTURE: native <tfoot> (implicit role="rowgroup"). Used for totals rows,
  summary rows, or the pagination region when composed via data-table.jte.
  Muted fill + top border + medium weight (shadcn TableFooter parity).

  A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Table):
    - Native <tfoot> (implicit role="rowgroup").
    - The last row's bottom border is suppressed via [&>tr]:last-child:border-b-0.

  Params:
    String cssClass       = ""     extra utility classes on <tfoot>.
    gg.jte.Content content         the footer row(s): @@template.lievit.table.row blocks.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.table.footer(content = @@`
      @@template.lievit.table.row(content = @@`
        @@template.lievit.table.cell(content = @@`Total`)
        @@template.lievit.table.cell(cssClass = "text-right", content = @@`$480,000`)
      `)
    `)
--%>
@param String cssClass = ""
@param gg.jte.Content content
<tfoot
  data-slot="table-footer"
  class="border-t font-[var(--lv-font-medium)] [&>tr]:last:border-b-0 ${cssClass}"
  style="border-color:var(--lv-color-border);background-color:color-mix(in srgb, var(--lv-color-surface) 60%, var(--lv-color-bg));"
>${content}</tfoot>

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui table.row partial -- a <tr> in the composable table set.

  TIER: PARTIAL (sub-partial; composed inside table.header, table.body, table.footer).

  STRUCTURE: native <tr> (implicit role="row"). Bottom border + optional hover highlight.
  A selected row is communicated via aria-selected="true" (server-owned state, passed as a
  param by the caller / data-table; the table-select.enhancer.ts toggles it on the fly for
  the bulk-select scenario). data-state="selected" drives the CSS tint for consistency with
  shadcn patterns; both are kept in sync.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Table):
    - Native <tr> (implicit role="row").
    - aria-selected: "true" | "false" | null. Omit (null) when the table has no selection
      model (default). When selectable=true, emit "false" on each row; the enhancer
      flips it on check.
    - aria-rowindex: 1-based row index within the FULL dataset (not just the current page).
      Omit when the table is not paginated (null -> attribute dropped).

  Params:
    String ariaSelected   = null   "true" | "false" | null (attribute dropped when null).
    String ariaRowIndex   = null   1-based row index in the full dataset; null -> omitted.
    String state          = ""     "selected" sets data-state for CSS hooks (shadcn parity).
    String dataRowId      = null   row identity (already HTML-escaped by caller) written to
                                   data-row-id for the table-select.enhancer + HTMX swaps.
    boolean hoverable     = true   row hover highlight via --lv-color-accent.
    boolean striped       = false  even-row tint (caller passes true for even rows).
    boolean bordered      = false  per-cell border style (matches parent table bordered flag).
    String cssClass       = ""     extra utility classes on <tr>.
    gg.jte.Content content         the head / cell blocks for this row.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.table.row(content = @@`
      @@template.lievit.table.head(content = @@`Name`)
      @@template.lievit.table.head(content = @@`Price`)
    `)
    @@template.lievit.table.row(ariaSelected = "false", dataRowId = row.id(),
      state = "selected", content = @@`...`)
--%>
@param String ariaSelected = null
@param String ariaRowIndex = null
@param String state = ""
@param String dataRowId = null
@param boolean hoverable = true
@param boolean striped = false
@param boolean bordered = false
@param String cssClass = ""
@param gg.jte.Content content
<%-- data-state: null drops the attribute when empty (smart attribute, no @if in name position). --%>
!{var _hoverClass = hoverable ? "hover:bg-[color-mix(in_srgb,var(--lv-color-accent)_70%,var(--lv-color-bg))] data-[state=selected]:bg-[color-mix(in_srgb,var(--lv-color-accent)_40%,var(--lv-color-bg))]" : "";}
!{var _stripedStyle = striped ? "background-color:color-mix(in srgb, var(--lv-color-surface) 40%, var(--lv-color-bg));" : "";}
!{var _borderStyle = bordered ? "border:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);" : "border-bottom:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);";}
<%-- `group` so per-row actions can reveal on hover (group-hover) the way Filament's row does; a
     selected row gets a 2px primary left-accent stripe via a `before:` pseudo (relative anchor),
     gated by data-[state=selected] so it shows only on the selected row. The stripe is decorative;
     the selection meaning is carried by aria-selected + data-state (never by colour alone). --%>
!{var _selectedStripe = "relative data-[state=selected]:before:absolute data-[state=selected]:before:inset-y-0 data-[state=selected]:before:left-0 data-[state=selected]:before:w-[2px] data-[state=selected]:before:bg-[var(--lv-color-primary)] data-[state=selected]:before:content-['']";}
<tr
  data-slot="table-row"
  data-state="${state.isEmpty() ? null : state}"
  data-row-id="${dataRowId}"
  aria-selected="${ariaSelected}"
  aria-rowindex="${ariaRowIndex}"
  class="group transition-colors ${_hoverClass} ${_selectedStripe} ${cssClass}"
  style="${_borderStyle}${_stripedStyle}"
>${content}</tr>

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui table.head partial -- a header cell in the composable table set. TIER: PARTIAL (sub-partial; composed inside table.header rows). STRUCTURE: native (implicit role="columnheader" or "rowheader" per scope). Renders the muted header look. When ariaSort is set, the aria-sort attribute is emitted on THIS element (the th), NOT on an inner button: the APG sortable-table example is explicit. The sort interaction button lives inside the content slot (composed by data-table.jte); this partial is the container only. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Table + sortable-table example): - scope="col" (default) -> implicit role="columnheader". - scope="row" -> implicit role="rowheader" (for row-header columns, e.g. name/ID). - scope="colgroup" | "rowgroup" -> spanning group headers. - aria-sort: "ascending" | "descending" | "none" | null. Set by the server (data-table.jte reads sort state); null (default) drops the attribute for non-sortable columns. Per the APG, aria-sort goes on , not on the inner button. - Hidden columns: set cssClass="sr-only" to keep the header in the a11y tree while hiding it visually (required for colspan / a11y math correctness). Params: String scope = "col" HTML scope: "col" | "colgroup" | "row" | "rowgroup". String ariaSort = null "ascending" | "descending" | "none" | null. null -> attribute dropped (non-sortable column). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes (e.g. "text-right", "sr-only"). gg.jte.Content content the column header label (plain text or a sort button slot). Usage: @@template.lievit.table.head(content = @@`Name`) @@template.lievit.table.head(scope = "row", content = @@`ID`) @@template.lievit.table.head(ariaSort = "ascending", content = @@` `) @@template.lievit.table.head(cssClass = "sr-only", content = @@`Actions`) --%> @param String scope = "col" @param String ariaSort = null @param String cssClass = "" @param String edge = "" @param gg.jte.Content content <%-- Filament fi-ta edge gutter: the first/last header cell gets an extra outer inset (ps-6 / pe-6) so the header label aligns with the body content off the card frame. Complete static literals so the Tailwind scanner keeps them; "" (interior column) adds nothing. --%> !{var _edgeClass = switch (edge) { case "first" -> "ps-[var(--lv-space-6)]"; case "last" -> "pe-[var(--lv-space-6)]"; default -> ""; };} <%-- Header cell styling: - Height via min-height (not fixed: a group header spanning two lines must still be readable). - px-[var(--lv-space-4)] horizontal + py-[var(--lv-space-2)] vertical (md default; size variant is the data-table.jte responsibility, not this primitive's concern). - Filament-grade: subtle tint (35% surface), small-semibold muted label. - [&:has([role=checkbox])]:pr-0 aligns a checkbox col header flush with the cell checkbox. --%> ${content}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui table.cell partial -- a data cell in the composable table set. TIER: PARTIAL (sub-partial; composed inside table.body rows). STRUCTURE: native (implicit role="cell"). Comfortable cell padding matching Filament fi-ta-cell density (px-4 + py-3 as default, overridable via cssClass). Numeric columns (right-aligned, tabular-nums) pass cssClass="text-right font-mono tabular-nums". Row-header columns use table.head with scope="row", not this partial. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Table): - Native (implicit role="cell"). - colspan: pass as a standard HTML attribute via cssClass (e.g. for empty-state spans); for colspan in attribute position use the attrs param (TRUSTED) or compose directly. - [&:has([role=checkbox])]:pr-0 aligns a checkbox column cell flush with the header. Params: String cssClass = "" extra utility classes (e.g. "text-right", "font-mono tabular-nums", "sr-only" for hidden column cells). String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only (e.g. colspan="3", data-col-id="name"). gg.jte.Content content the cell value or composed markup (badge, link, action button). Usage: @@template.lievit.table.cell(content = @@`Cecchetto`) @@template.lievit.table.cell(cssClass = "text-right font-mono tabular-nums", content = @@`240000`) @@template.lievit.table.cell(attrs = "colspan=\"3\"", content = @@`No data.`) --%> @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param String edge = "" @param gg.jte.Content content <%-- Filament fi-ta edge gutter: the first/last cell of a row gets an extra outer inset so the content sits off the card frame. Complete static class literals so the Tailwind scanner keeps them. "" (interior cell) adds nothing. --%> !{var _edgeClass = switch (edge) { case "first" -> "ps-[var(--lv-space-6)]"; case "last" -> "pe-[var(--lv-space-6)]"; default -> ""; };} ${content}

@import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui table.caption partial -- the <caption> of the composable table set.

  TIER: PARTIAL (sub-partial; composed inside @@template.lievit.table).

  STRUCTURE: native <caption> (implicit "caption" role). The preferred mechanism for
  the accessible name of a table: screen readers announce it before reading cells.
  The parent table.jte sets caption-side:bottom (shadcn parity); the caption appears
  below the table visually.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Table):
    - <caption> is the preferred accessible name mechanism over aria-label. When a
      visible caption is present, do NOT also set ariaLabel on table.jte (duplicate
      name, axe-core table-duplicate-name violation).
    - Visually-hidden captions: pass cssClass="sr-only" to hide the caption visually
      while keeping it in the a11y tree. This is the pattern when an external heading
      already labels the table visually but an in-table accessible name is also needed
      (the APG allows it; axe-core allows it when it is not a duplicate of visible text).

  Params:
    String cssClass  = ""     extra utility classes (e.g. "sr-only" for a visually-hidden
                               caption, "text-left" for left-aligned caption text).
    gg.jte.Content content    the caption text or markup.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.table.caption(content = @@`Q2 Listings`)
    @@template.lievit.table.caption(cssClass = "sr-only", content = @@`Users table`)
--%>
@param String cssClass = ""
@param gg.jte.Content content
<caption
  data-slot="table-caption"
  class="mt-[var(--lv-space-4)] text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)] ${cssClass}"
  style="color:var(--lv-color-muted);"
>${content}</caption>

items.docs: Copies the composable table primitive set to your JTE root under lievit/. Compose via @template.lievit.table(ariaLabel="...", content=@`...`) wrapping @template.lievit.table.header / .body / .footer / .row / .head / .cell / .caption. The caller owns the row loop. For the data-driven HTMX-sortable variant, use data-table.jte (separate component that composes this set). WAI-ARIA APG Table: native elements, scope attributes on <th>, aria-sort on <th> for sortable columns, aria-selected and aria-rowindex on <tr>. Pass ariaSelected / ariaRowIndex / dataRowId to table.row; ariaSort to table.head; stickyHeader to table.header. The accessible name is either a <caption> (table.caption) or ariaLabel on the table wrapper (not both). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: tabs
items.description: WAI-ARIA APG Tabs: a role=tablist strip of role=tab triggers + role=tabpanel regions. Controlled/uncontrolled headless primitive -- the active tab is server state passed via the plain `activeTab` @param; the caller binds it to their own @Wire field. No @Wire field of its own, no Java component. ENH: collection-nav.enhancer.ts in roving-tabindex mode handles ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down, Home/End, Enter/Space (manual mode), automatic activation. Supports sm/md/lg sizes (toolbar-aligned), line/card/pill types, eager (all panels in DOM) or lazy HTMX panel swap, closable tabs, addable tab button, manual/automatic activation modes. data-slots: tabs / tabs-list / tabs-trigger / tabs-close / tabs-add / tabs-content. Pure HTML + --lv-* tokens, zero inline script, zero on*= handlers, CSP-clean.
items.docs:
Copies the headless controlled/uncontrolled tabs primitive to lievit/tabs.jte. Call @template.lievit.tabs(tabsId=..., tabIds=..., tabLabels=..., activeTab=..., tabsLabel=..., selectAction="activate"). The tabsId param is REQUIRED and must be unique on the page -- derive it from your wire component id (e.g. _component.id()) so the ARIA anchors are deterministic. Pass tabIcons / tabClosable / tabDisabled as parallel lists (index-aligned with tabIds; may be shorter -- missing entries default to no icon / false / false). For controlled use: bind activeTab to your @Wire field and set selectAction to your activate action. For lazy HTMX panels: set lazyLoad=true and provide a GET endpoint at /tabs/{tabsId}/panel/{tabId} returning the panel fragment. The panel body regions in this copied-in template are OWNED markup: replace the placeholder

per tab with your real server-rendered content. collection-nav.enhancer.ts must be installed on the runtime (installCollectionNav(runtime)) for arrow-key roving and automatic/manual activation.

items.name: tabs
items.description: A tab bar + panels where the active tab is server state; only the active panel is shown. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the active id lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-tabs> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG tabs (tablist > tab[aria-selected][aria-controls] + tabpanel[aria-labelledby], roving tabindex). An HTMX content-swap recipe is the lighter alternative for independent panel fragments (see docs).
items.docs: Register TabsComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `tabIds` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `disabledIds`, as locked server config, then render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.tabs(...)`. Each tab switches with `l:click="$set('active','<id>')"` (the active tab is a scalar, so the $set magic IS the select; no custom action needed); the @LievitRender hook falls back to the first enabled tab and refuses a disabled/unknown active id. The panel BODY per tab is OWNED markup inside the `data-tabs-panel` div: edit it in this copied-in template (there is no <slot>). HTMX alternative: drop the wire and give each tab link an `hx-get` to a controller endpoint returning the panel partial, swapped into the panel container, when the panels are independent server fragments. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/tabs")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: tags-input
items.description: A free-text TAGS field (Filament TagsInput; shadcn composes Badge + Input): existing tags shown as removable chips + a text input to add more, with optional quick-pick suggestions and optional clear-all button. TIER: CLIENT-ENHANCER (form-native, NOT WIRE). Each committed tag is a chip carrying a hidden <input name=...> so the set POSTs as a repeated `name` with zero JS. JS-OFF an explicit Add submit (name=`<name>__add`) and per-chip remove submit (name=`<name>__remove`) round-trip to the server; handle those server-side to append/drop. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer makes editing in-place: Enter/delimiter/Add appends a chip + hidden input, the chip x removes it, Backspace-on-empty pops the last, paste splits on the delimiter. Chip keyboard navigation (ArrowLeft/Right/Home/End) uses roving tabindex. An aria-live region announces add/remove. Hidden inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA BUILT model (no APG pattern for token entry): root role=group aria-labelledby; chips role=option aria-selected=true roving tabindex; live region role=status. Size param (sm/md/lg) controls well min-height and chip height. clearable=true shows a clear-all button when tags are present. delimiter param controls paste-split and keydown trigger. dataAttrs param for SAFE dynamic data-* on root.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui tags-input partial -- a free-text TAGS field: existing tags shown as removable chips + a text input to add more, with optional suggestions (Filament TagsInput; shadcn composes Badge + Input). The committed tags POST as a REPEATED `name` via one hidden input per chip, so the set submits with zero JS (the platform does the work). JS-OFF an explicit Add submit button (name=name__add) and a per-chip remove submit (name=name__remove) round-trip to the server. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer makes the editing in-place: Enter / delimiter key / Add appends a chip + hidden input without a round-trip, chip x deletes it, Backspace-on-empty pops the last, paste splits on the delimiter, and chip keyboard navigation (ArrowLeft/Right, Home/End) uses roving tabindex; an aria-live region announces changes. Hidden inputs stay the form source of truth either way. TIER: CLIENT-ENHANCER (form-native, NOT WIRE). Every tag value is a hidden input whose name matches the field name; the set POSTs as a repeated name. No wire round-trips for add/remove (the enhancer owns that locally). The server re-renders the whole template on full submit. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): JS-only token fields reimplement chips + value plumbing in script and break no-JS submit. Hidden inputs per tag give the repeated-name POST for free from the platform, so they are the base layer; chip editing is an enhancement on top, never the value itself. Same pattern as checkbox-list / file-upload. A11y (WAI-ARIA BUILT model -- no APG pattern for token entry; built from three APG sources): (1) APG Keyboard Interface practices for roving-tabindex focus management after deletion. (2) APG Combobox for the optional suggestion group (no popup in form-native; just a group of buttons). (3) Raw WAI-ARIA group + option semantics. root: role="group" aria-labelledby -> label element id; aria-disabled when disabled; aria-invalid when invalid. chips: role="option" aria-selected="true" tabindex="-1" (roving: enhancer sets one to 0 at a time). aria-label includes "press Delete or Backspace to remove" instruction. Remove button is a real button type="submit" (JS-off) / type="button" (JS-on), tabindex="-1". entry input: plain input type="text" (no combobox role; no popup in form-native). aria-labelledby -> label id (if label present); aria-label fallback; aria-describedby. suggestions: role="group" aria-label="Suggestions" container; each suggestion is a real button. live region: role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true". Announces add/remove/dedup. clear-all: real button aria-label="Clear all tags" (present only when clearable and hasTags). Escaping channels (the XSS decision rule): tag values in attr-value position (data-tags-input-chip, data-tags-input-remove, value): -> JTE default ${} escaping covers attr-value position safely. delimiter in data-delimiter attribute: -> built via StringOutput + Escape.htmlAttribute, emitted $unsafe (full fragment). dynamic data-* from dataAttrs map: -> built via StringOutput + Escape.htmlAttribute, emitted $unsafe. attrs: -> $unsafe, STATIC author-typed strings only. NEVER interpolate per-row DB values. Params: String name required: field name for POST. String id = null entry input id (defaults to name). java.util.List of String values = null existing tags (rendered as chips). java.util.List of String suggestions = null optional quick-pick suggestions. String label = null visible label text (for element). String ariaLabel = null aria-label when no visible label. String describedBy = null id of external hint/error element. String placeholder = "Add a tag..." add-input placeholder. String addLabel = "Add" JS-off Add submit text. String delimiter = "," paste-split delimiter (and keydown trigger). String size = "md" "sm" or "md" or "lg" toolbar-aligned. boolean disabled = false disables the control. boolean invalid = false danger ring + aria-invalid. boolean clearable = false show clear-all button when hasTags. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on root. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw attrs on root. Map of String to String dataAttrs = {} SAFE dynamic data-* on root. Usage: @@template.lievit.tags-input(name = "etichette", label = "Etichette", values = contatto.etichette(), suggestions = java.util.List.of("urgente", "richiamare", "trattativa")) @@template.lievit.tags-input(name = "tags", size = "sm", clearable = true, values = entity.tags(), delimiter = ";") --%> @param String name @param String id = null @param java.util.List values = null @param java.util.List suggestions = null @param String label = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param String describedBy = null @param String placeholder = "Add a tag..." @param String addLabel = "Add" @param String delimiter = "," @param String size = "md" @param boolean disabled = false @param boolean invalid = false @param boolean clearable = false @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param java.util.Map dataAttrs = java.util.Map.of() !{var inputId = (id != null && !id.isBlank()) ? id : name;} !{var labelId = label != null && !label.isBlank() ? inputId + "__label" : null;} !{var tagList = values != null ? values : java.util.List.of();} !{var hasTags = !tagList.isEmpty();} !{var hasSuggestions = suggestions != null && !suggestions.isEmpty();} !{var showClearAll = clearable && hasTags && !disabled;} !{var wellMinH = switch(size) { case "sm" -> "var(--lv-space-8)"; case "lg" -> "var(--lv-space-10)"; default -> "var(--lv-space-9)"; };} !{var chipH = switch(size) { case "sm" -> "var(--lv-space-6)"; case "lg" -> "var(--lv-space-8)"; default -> "var(--lv-space-7)"; };} !{var textSz = switch(size) { case "sm" -> "var(--lv-text-xs)"; case "lg" -> "var(--lv-text-base)"; default -> "var(--lv-text-sm)"; };} <%-- Escape the delimiter for data-delimiter attribute. --%> !{var _delimOut_ = new StringOutput();} !{_delimOut_.writeContent(" data-delimiter=\"");} !{Escape.htmlAttribute(delimiter, _delimOut_);} !{_delimOut_.writeContent("\"");} <%-- SAFE dynamic data-* attributes. --%> !{var _dataAttrs_ = new StringOutput();} !{for (var _e_ : dataAttrs.entrySet()) { if (_e_.getKey() != null && _e_.getKey().matches("[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*")) { _dataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-" + _e_.getKey() + "=\""); Escape.htmlAttribute(_e_.getValue() == null ? "" : _e_.getValue(), _dataAttrs_); _dataAttrs_.writeContent("\""); } }}
@if(label != null && !label.isBlank()) @endif <%-- Well: visual input container holding chips + entry input + clear-all --%>
<%-- Chips host: each chip carries a hidden input (the form POST channel) --%>
@for(String tag : tagList) ${tag} @endfor
<%-- Entry input: plain text (no combobox role; no popup in form-native tier) --%> @if(showClearAll) @endif <%-- JS-OFF: explicit Add submit round-trips to the server (enhancer hides JS-on). --%>
@if(hasSuggestions)
@for(String suggestion : suggestions) @endfor
@endif <%-- Live region: announces add/remove/dedup to screen readers. --%>

items.docs: Copies the tags-input partial to your JTE root at lievit/tags-input.jte (reached as @template.lievit.tags-input(name=..., values=...)) and the CSP-clean enhancer to lievit/tags-input.enhancer.ts. Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllTagsInputs() on load (and after any DOM swap). The committed tags POST as a repeated `name` (a hidden input per chip). JS-OFF the user adds via the Add submit (`<name>__add`) and removes via the per-chip submit (`<name>__remove`), both server round-trips; handle those parameters server-side to append/drop. JS-ON the enhancer adds on Enter/delimiter/Add and removes via the chip x, all in place, and hides the Add button. Paste splits on the `delimiter` param (default comma). Chip keyboard navigation: ArrowLeft/Right between chips, ArrowLeft from entry at pos 0 goes to last chip, ArrowRight from last chip goes to entry, Home/End, Delete/Backspace/Enter/Space on chip removes it and restores focus. `clearable=true` shows a clear-all button when tags non-empty. `size` (sm/md/lg) controls well min-height and chip height. Pass `values` (existing tags), optional `suggestions`, `label`/`ariaLabel`/`describedBy`, `placeholder`, `addLabel`, `delimiter`, `size`, `disabled`, `invalid`, `clearable`. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: textarea
items.description: A token-styled multi-line native textarea (PARTIAL+ENH). Carries a real name/id pair (POSTs JS-off), binds via l:model. Supports sm|md|lg size scale, autosize grow-to-content, live character count (showCount), built-in hint, destructive invalid state (aria-invalid). The textarea-autosize enhancer is the only client bit; without it the bare partial is fully accessible.
items.docs: Copies the textarea partial to your JTE root at lievit/textarea.jte, reached as @template.lievit.textarea(name=...). The native textarea POSTs under `name` with zero JS; bind to a wire field with `model`. The textarea-autosize enhancer (runtime/features/textarea-autosize.enhancer.ts) adds grow-to-content and live character count when autosize=true or showCount=true; call installTextareaAutosize(runtime) in your app main.ts. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: theme-switcher
items.description: A three-state (light / dark / system) theme-preference control. Three variants: icon (compact toolbar of three icon-only toggle buttons), labeled (icon + text per option), icon-labeled (single cycling button). Persists the user's choice to localStorage and reflects it as data-theme on the configured root element (the single repoint mechanism the lievit token system uses). Server-rendered as a static HTML frame (no wire round-trip — the preference is a client-side fact); the colocated typed-TS enhancer reads localStorage on mount, syncs aria-pressed states, manages roving tabindex in the toolbar, applies data-theme to the root, and tracks prefers-color-scheme live when system is chosen. APG Toolbar + APG Toggle Button (aria-pressed) pattern. CSP-clean: zero inline script, zero on* handlers.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui theme-switcher partial -- a three-state (light / dark / system) theme-preference control that persists the user's choice to localStorage and reflects it as data-theme on the configured root element, which is the single repoint mechanism the lievit token system uses. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (theme-switcher.enhancer.ts, colocated). The control structure + initial state are server-rendered (CSP-clean, zero inline script). The irreducibly-client behaviour -- reading prefers-color-scheme, writing localStorage, toggling data-theme on the root, keeping aria-pressed in sync -- lives entirely in the colocated typed-TS enhancer that binds off data-lievit-enhancer="theme-switcher". No server round-trip; the theme preference is a client-side display fact, not a domain value the server owns. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): three variants ship under one `variant` param. "icon" (default): three icon-only button elements in a role="toolbar" wrapper; each button is square; each carries an aria-label from the label param. Pattern: WAI-ARIA APG Toolbar (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/toolbar/) containing APG Toggle Buttons (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/) with aria-pressed. "labeled": three buttons with icon + visible text label side by side; same toolbar grouping. "icon-labeled": a single button that cycles through states on click; its label + icon update to reflect the currently-active theme; behaves as a three-state cycling toggle. When showSystemOption=false the group shrinks to two options (light / dark). A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Toolbar + APG Button / Toggle Button): icon / labeled variants: root div has role="toolbar" + aria-label="Theme" (or adopter value); each option is a real @else <%-- THREE-BUTTON TOOLBAR VARIANTS ("icon" and "labeled") -- A role="toolbar" div groups three aria-pressed toggle buttons (APG Toolbar pattern). The toolbar itself is a single tab stop; the enhancer manages roving tabindex within it so only the active button has tabindex="0" (APG Toolbar + APG Toggle Button §keyboard). All three buttons start with aria-pressed="false" and tabindex="-1"; the enhancer corrects both on mount, synchronously, before un-hiding the root (display:none guard). --%>
<%-- Light option button --%> <%-- Dark option button --%> @if(showSystemOption) <%-- System option button (conditional on showSystemOption) --%> @endif
@endif

items.docs: Copies the theme-switcher partial to lievit/theme-switcher.jte and the colocated typed-TS enhancer to lievit/theme-switcher.enhancer.ts. Import the enhancer in your entry module and register it with the lievit runtime: registerEnhancer("theme-switcher", ThemeSwitcherEnhancer). The partial renders a display:none root (to avoid flash-of-wrong-state); the enhancer removes the guard synchronously on mount after syncing aria-pressed. Three variants via the `variant` param: icon (default, compact toolbar), labeled (icon + text), icon-labeled (single cycling button). Size via `size`: sm/md (default)/lg — height-based, toolbar-aligned. `storageKey` names the localStorage key (default lievit-theme); `rootSelector` names the element that gets data-theme (default html); `defaultTheme` is the fallback (default system). `showSystemOption=false` reduces to a two-state light/dark toggle. Labels are i18n-friendly: set labelLight/labelDark/labelSystem from the Spring controller's i18n layer. The enhancer applies data-theme, never toggles a class (ADR-0005 single repoint mechanism). Multiple instances on the same page are allowed; they stay in sync via the shared localStorage key.

items.name: toast
items.description: Transient live-region status announcer (v-next). Three server-rendered JTE partials sharing one data model: toast-item (toast.jte) stamps a single notification card; toast/region.jte renders the two always-present live-region sub-containers (role=status polite for info/success, role=alert assertive for warning/destructive); notification-bell.jte is the icon-button + badge + popover history hub. The colocated toast.enhancer.ts owns queue management, countdown timers, auto-dismiss-pause-on-focus (react-aria useToast model), Esc-dismiss with focus restore, SSE insertion path, and bell counter. Variant vocabulary: info | success | warning | destructive. PARTIAL + ENH (ADR-0012); replaces the standalone toast.jte + viewport.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui toast/region partial -- the live-region stack container. Render ONCE in the page layout immediately before , outside any aria-modal surface. TIER: PARTIAL. Zero client state: the two sub-containers are always present in the DOM (the live-region pre-existence invariant -- a dynamically-created live region is unreliable across AT; the containers MUST exist before any toast fires). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - WAI-ARIA: two sub-containers, one role="status" aria-live="polite" (info/success) and one role="alert" aria-live="assertive" (warning/destructive). aria-atomic="false" so each inserted child is announced individually, not the full container. - The enhancer (toast.enhancer.ts) inserts toast-item.jte fragments into the correct sub-container based on data-variant. This partial only provides the structural seam. - placement maps to CSS fixed positioning utilities. A11y (WAI-ARIA live region): - role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="false": info + success toasts. AT announces when the user is idle. Appropriate for non-urgent confirmations. - role="alert" aria-live="assertive" aria-atomic="false": warning + destructive toasts. AT interrupts immediately. Appropriate for errors requiring immediate attention. - aria-relevant="additions" (the default for live regions) is left implicit. - Both sub-containers are rendered at page load; never created dynamically. Params: String placement = "bottom-end" "top-start" | "top-center" | "top-end" | "bottom-start" | "bottom-center" | "bottom-end". int maxVisible = 5 Max items shown simultaneously (read by enhancer). String sseUrl = null If set, enhancer opens an EventSource on this URL. String bellId = null If set, enhancer increments the bell badge counter. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed only. Usage: @@template.lievit.toast.region() @@template.lievit.toast.region(placement = "top-end", maxVisible = 3) --%> @param String placement = "bottom-end" @param int maxVisible = 5 @param String sseUrl = null @param String bellId = null @param String attrs = "" !{var posStyle = switch (placement) { case "top-start" -> "top:var(--lv-space-4);left:var(--lv-space-4);align-items:flex-start;"; case "top-center" -> "top:var(--lv-space-4);left:50%;transform:translateX(-50%);align-items:center;"; case "top-end" -> "top:var(--lv-space-4);right:var(--lv-space-4);align-items:flex-end;"; case "bottom-start" -> "bottom:var(--lv-space-4);left:var(--lv-space-4);align-items:flex-start;"; case "bottom-center"-> "bottom:var(--lv-space-4);left:50%;transform:translateX(-50%);align-items:center;"; default -> "bottom:var(--lv-space-4);right:var(--lv-space-4);align-items:flex-end;"; };} !{var flexDir = placement.startsWith("bottom") ? "column-reverse" : "column";}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui toast/viewport partial -- the fixed stack container that holds several toasts at one corner (sonner's Toaster region; data-slot="toast-viewport"). Server-rendered, zero JS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout (a fixed-corner flex column with a gap), no client state: the server already knows which toasts to show, so it renders them stacked. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): sonner renders ONE fixed region per corner that column- stacks its toasts. We reproduce that with a fixed flex column anchored to the chosen `position` (the six sonner corners) and a token gap between toasts. Bottom positions stack column-reverse so a new toast appears nearest the edge and pushes the older ones away from it, matching sonner. The toasts inside are rendered with standalone=false so they flow in this column instead of fixing themselves. A11y: a plain layout container; each child toast owns its own role=alert/status live region, so the viewport itself carries no role. Params: String position = "bottom-right" one of: top-left | top-center | top-right | bottom-left | bottom-center | bottom-right. gg.jte.Content content the @template.lievit.toast(standalone = false, ...) calls. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the viewport. Usage: @@template.lievit.toast.viewport(position = "top-right", content = @@` @@template.lievit.toast(standalone = false, variant = "success", content = @@`Saved.`) @@template.lievit.toast(standalone = false, variant = "info", content = @@`Heads up.`) `) --%> @param String position = "bottom-right" @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = "" !{var bottom = position.startsWith("bottom");} !{var posStyle = switch (position) { case "top-left" -> "top: var(--lv-space-4); left: var(--lv-space-4); align-items: flex-start;"; case "top-center" -> "top: var(--lv-space-4); left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); align-items: center;"; case "top-right" -> "top: var(--lv-space-4); right: var(--lv-space-4); align-items: flex-end;"; case "bottom-left" -> "bottom: var(--lv-space-4); left: var(--lv-space-4); align-items: flex-start;"; case "bottom-center" -> "bottom: var(--lv-space-4); left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); align-items: center;"; default -> "bottom: var(--lv-space-4); right: var(--lv-space-4); align-items: flex-end;"; // bottom-right };}
${content}

items.docs: Render toast/region.jte ONCE before </body> (outside any aria-modal surface). The two live-region sub-containers (polite + assertive) are always present at page load -- this is the load-order invariant for reliable AT announcements. Toasts arrive via: (1) the Lievit-Toast response header on any wire round-trip; (2) SSE when data-toast-sse-url is set on the region; (3) window.lievit.toast({...}) from adopter scripts. The enhancer manages the queue (maxVisible cap), countdown timers (paused while focused), Esc-dismiss with focus restore, and the notification-bell unread counter. Variant drives both the live-region sub-container (polite for info/success, assertive for warning/destructive) and the accent token pair. notification-bell.jte is optional: include it in your toolbar to give users a browsable notification history that accumulates across page load.

items.name: toggle
items.description: A two-state pressed <button> (WAI-ARIA APG Toggle Button: aria-pressed) with four variants (outline/ghost/primary/secondary), three toolbar-aligned sizes (sm/md/lg), optional leading/trailing slots, and icon-only square mode. The pressed state is server-owned (data down); CSS [aria-pressed=true] drives the visual without JS. Click wired via wireClick + wireArgs (SAFE escaped) or a static attrs string (TRUSTED raw). Server-first replacement for the Lit island toggle.
items.docs: Copies the toggle partial to your JTE root at lievit/toggle.jte, reached as @template.lievit.toggle(...). Render `pressed` from the controller; wire `wireClick` (SAFE action name) + `wireArgs` (SAFE Map) to the action that flips it. `variant` (outline|ghost|primary|secondary), `size` (sm|md|lg). For icon-only toggles set `iconOnly=true` and supply `ariaLabel`. Use `leading` / `trailing` content slots for icons or badges flanking the label. Dynamic data-* go through `dataAttrs` (SAFE escaped Map); static directives go through `attrs` (TRUSTED raw). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: toggle-buttons
items.description: A segmented control used as a FORM control (Filament's ToggleButtons; shadcn's ToggleGroup, but form-bound and JS-off-safe). A joined row of options where one (single) or several (multiple) can be pressed; the selection POSTs natively with ZERO JavaScript. The segments are REAL native form inputs styled to look like buttons: single = radios sharing one name in a fieldset (platform single-selection + roving arrow focus + radiogroup role), multiple = checkboxes sharing one name (each toggles, repeated POST). The native input is sr-only (focusable, tab-ordered, submitted) and its sibling label is the visible segment painted by peer-checked, so the control is fully keyboard- and screen-reader-operable and submits in a plain form. Reuses the button-group joined-look CSS (collapsed inner rounding + shared borders) for the segmented visual. Invalid state adds a destructive ring on the fieldset and a destructive border on each segment label via peer-[aria-invalid=true]. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean, no island.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui toggle-buttons partial -- a segmented control used as a FORM control: a joined row of options where one (single) or several (multiple) can be pressed, and the selection POSTs natively (Filament's ToggleButtons field; shadcn's ToggleGroup, but form-bound and JS-off-safe). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial (zero JS). The trick that buys "no JS, no dead control, full a11y, native POST": the segments are REAL native form inputs with their label styled to LOOK like a button, not

items.docs: Copies the toggle-buttons partial to your JTE root at lievit/toggle-buttons.jte (reached as @template.lievit.toggle-buttons(name=..., options=...)). It is a zero-JS segmented form control: pass `options` (value->label Map) or a `content` slot, set `multiple` for checkbox segments (single = radios), `value` (single) or `values` (multiple) for the initial selection, `size` (sm/md/lg), `orientation` (horizontal/vertical), plus `label` / `ariaLabel` / `describedBy` / `disabled` / `required` / `invalid`. Single-mode POSTs the one chosen value under `name`; multiple POSTs each pressed value under the repeated `name`. The selection is the native :checked input (never colour alone), so it is accessible and submits with no JavaScript. When `invalid=true` the fieldset gets a destructive shadow ring and each segment label shows a destructive border via peer-[aria-invalid=true]. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: toggle-group
items.description: A set of toggle buttons with single- or multiple-selection; the selected value(s) are server state. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the selection lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-toggle-group> Lit island. Radix ToggleGroup a11y: single = radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple = group > button[aria-pressed].
items.docs: Register ToggleGroupComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `values` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `icons` (index-aligned, '' = none) and `disabledValues`, plus `mode` ('single' | 'multiple') and the whole-group `disabled`, as locked server config; the buttons render themselves. Each item toggles with `l:click="$set('toggleValue','<value>')"`; the @LievitRender hook flips it in the selected set and clears the arm (one click, fully server-side; single mode keeps at most one and allows deselect-to-empty, Radix behaviour). Single mode renders role=radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple renders role=group > button[aria-pressed]. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/toggle-group")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: tokens
items.description: The lievit-ui design tokens as --lv-* CSS custom properties. Copied in on first use; the shared design vocabulary every component references.
items.docs: Import styles/lievit-tokens.css once from your global stylesheet. Override any --lv-* value in :root to retheme.

items.name: tooltip
items.description: A hover/focus-triggered floating description panel. WAI-ARIA APG Tooltip pattern: bubble carries role="tooltip" + a stable id; tooltip.enhancer.ts wires aria-describedby on the first focusable descendant at mount (or the wrapper span when no focusable descendant exists) and removes it on destroy. Show/hide timing: hover delay (configurable, default 600ms) + immediate focus, Escape-while-visible dismiss (document-level capture listener, registered only while open), hover persistence (pointer entering the bubble cancels the hide timer). Native popover="manual" + CSS Anchor Positioning for positioning -- the shared popover seam. No round-trip: open state is ephemeral, fires no wire actions. Four variants (default/info/warning/destructive) map to the shared intent token pairs. CSS-only arrow (lv-tooltip__arrow pseudo-element). CSP-clean, no inline handlers, no eval.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui tooltip partial -- a hover/focus-triggered floating description panel. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (tooltip.enhancer.ts, minimal). The content is static server-rendered text; the open state is a purely client-side ephemeral concern (mouse position / keyboard focus is not worth a round-trip). The irreducible client piece -- show/hide timing (hover delay + instant focus, dismiss on blur/mouseleave/Esc), aria-describedby binding, and native popover attribute management -- lives in tooltip.enhancer.ts. Server renders both the trigger wrapper and the bubble (hidden via popover="manual"); the enhancer surfaces them on demand. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Tooltip pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tooltip/) + tooltip ARIA role (https://w3c.github.io/aria/#tooltip). The bubble carries role="tooltip" and a stable id. The enhancer wires aria-describedby on the first focusable descendant of the wrapper (or the wrapper itself) at mount -- NOT server-side, because the trigger element identity is a rendering detail, not a template concern. Native popover="manual" is used so the UA hides the bubble by default and the enhancer calls showPopover()/hidePopover() explicitly. The popover="auto" mode is intentionally avoided: it adds UA light-dismiss-on-click, which conflicts with the hover/focus-driven show model. CSS Anchor Positioning (position-anchor + position-area) is the shared popover seam for all floating surfaces in lievit; the tooltip does not hand-roll getBoundingClientRect() or position:absolute math. A11y (WAI-ARIA Tooltip): - role="tooltip" on the bubble (server-rendered, static). - id on the bubble matches data-lievit-tooltip-id on the wrapper; enhancer resolves the pairing and sets aria-describedby at onInit; removes at onDestroy. - Keyboard: Tab into trigger -> shows immediately (focusin ignores delay). Tab/Shift+Tab out -> hides immediately (no hide-delay on focusout). Escape while visible -> dismisses; focus stays on trigger (APG rule: tooltip is non-modal, no focus trap). The Esc handler is registered on document (capture) only while the bubble is open and removed on hide. - Hover persistence: pointer from trigger to bubble cancels the hide timer (APG rule). The enhancer hooks pointerenter on the bubble to cancel the pending hide. - The tooltip never receives keyboard focus (APG rule); no roving tabindex. - No live region: content surfaces via aria-describedby, not aria-live. - Bubble omitted when content is blank (role="tooltip" on an empty element is a violation). Params: String id REQUIRED. Unique id stamped on the bubble and mirrored into data-lievit-tooltip-id on the wrapper; the enhancer reads these to wire aria-describedby. String content REQUIRED. Tooltip text (plain text; HTML-escaped by JTE's default ${} -- never $unsafe; never the sole carrier of essential information). gg.jte.Content trigger REQUIRED. The element the tooltip describes (button, icon, link, text span); rendered inside the wrapper span. String variant "default" | "info" | "warning" | "destructive". Intent maps to the shared token pairs (--lv-color- bg + -fg text). "default" uses --lv-color-popover so all floating surfaces share a look at rest. String placement "top" | "top-start" | "top-end" | "bottom" | "bottom-start" | "bottom-end" | "left" | "right" -- CSS Anchor Positioning preferred placement (position-area). Falls back gracefully via position-try-fallbacks. Default "top". int delay Hover show-delay in ms (default 600). 0 = immediate (useful for icon-only buttons where the label IS the tooltip). focusin always shows immediately regardless. int hideDelay Hover hide-delay in ms after mouseleave (default 0). Non-zero lets the pointer traverse into the bubble (APG hover-persistence rule). boolean arrow Render the directional CSS-only arrow (default true). The arrow is a pseudo-element; no new token needed. String maxWidth Inline override for --_lv-tooltip-max-width (default "14rem"). A structural dimension, not a colour; safe in the style attr. boolean disabled When true the enhancer no-ops all events; bubble stays hidden. Stamped as data-lievit-tooltip-disabled on the wrapper. String cssClass Extra utility classes on the bubble. String attrs TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only, e.g. data-testid="save-tooltip". Never interpolate DB-derived values. Usage: @@template.lievit.tooltip(id = "save-tip", content = "Save document", trigger = @@` `) @@template.lievit.tooltip(id = "del-tip", content = "Delete forever", variant = "destructive", placement = "bottom", delay = 0, trigger = @@` @@template.lievit.button(variant = "destructive", iconOnly = true, ariaLabel = "Delete", content = @@`@@template.lievit.icon(name = "trash-2", size = "1rem")`) `) --%> @param String id @param String content @param gg.jte.Content trigger @param String variant = "default" @param String placement = "top" @param int delay = 600 @param int hideDelay = 0 @param boolean arrow = true @param String maxWidth = "14rem" @param boolean disabled = false @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" <%-- CSS Anchor Positioning: map the placement param to a CSS position-area value. The spec placement vocabulary mirrors floating-ui/Radix and maps to the two-keyword position-area syntax (block-position inline-position). --%> !{var positionArea = switch (placement) { case "top-start" -> "top span-right"; case "top-end" -> "top span-left"; case "bottom" -> "bottom center"; case "bottom-start" -> "bottom span-right"; case "bottom-end" -> "bottom span-left"; case "left" -> "left center"; case "right" -> "right center"; default -> "top center"; // "top" (the default) };} <%-- Variant -> bg and fg token resolution (the shared intent vocabulary). "default" maps to the popover surface so all floating surfaces share a look at rest. No new token needed: the existing --lv-color-popover family is the correct bucket. --%> !{var variantBg = switch (variant) { case "info" -> "var(--lv-color-info)"; case "warning" -> "var(--lv-color-warning)"; case "destructive" -> "var(--lv-color-destructive)"; default -> "var(--lv-color-popover)"; };} !{var variantFg = switch (variant) { case "info" -> "var(--lv-color-info-fg)"; case "warning" -> "var(--lv-color-warning-fg)"; case "destructive" -> "var(--lv-color-destructive-fg)"; default -> "var(--lv-color-popover-fg)"; };} <%-- The anchor name is set inline on the wrapper span via style so CSS Anchor Positioning can resolve the bubble's position-anchor reference. Both the anchor-name and position-anchor use the same CSS custom ident derived from the id param. --%> !{var anchorName = "--" + id + "-anchor";} <%-- trigger slot: the element whose focus/hover shows the tooltip --%> ${trigger} <%-- tooltip bubble: always present in the DOM, hidden via popover="manual" until the enhancer calls showPopover(). Omitted entirely when content is blank so a role="tooltip" is never present on an empty or missing element (APG + ARIA spec). --%> @if(content != null && !content.isBlank())
${content}@if(arrow)@endif
@endif

items.docs: Copies the tooltip partial to your JTE root at lievit/tooltip.jte. Requires tooltip.enhancer.ts registered in your runtime (installTooltip(runtime)). Call as @template.lievit.tooltip(id="save-tip", content="Save document", trigger=@`<button>Save</button>`). id is REQUIRED and must be unique per page (it becomes the bubble's id and the aria-describedby value). The enhancer wires aria-describedby at mount -- do NOT set it in the trigger slot markup. variant selects the intent tone (default/info/warning/destructive). placement (default "top") sets the CSS Anchor Positioning preferred side; falls back via position-try-fallbacks. delay (default 600ms) is the hover show-delay; focus always shows immediately. Set delay=0 for icon-only buttons where the tooltip IS the label. hideDelay (default 0) allows the pointer to traverse into the bubble. Set arrow=false to suppress the directional pointer. disabled=true makes the enhancer no-op all events. attrs is TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) for STATIC author-typed attribute strings only -- never interpolate DB-derived values. The --lv-z-tooltip: 950 token is NET-NEW; add it to your lievit-tokens.css :root and .dark blocks.

items.name: wizard
items.description: A multi-step form stepper (CONTROLLED headless PARTIAL). The active step is a server fact owned by the CALLER's @Wire int field; this partial renders the presentational chrome only: an ordered step-indicator strip (linear or skippable), a content panel (the active step's body via a Content slot), and a Prev/Next/Submit action bar. Skippable mode composes collection-nav.enhancer.ts (APG roving-tabindex, Arrow/Home/End/Enter navigation on step buttons). A11y: WAI-ARIA aria-current=step + W3C WAI Multi-page Forms tutorial (ordered-list + aria-current model, NOT role=tablist). Features: linear/skippable modes, horizontal/vertical orientation, bottom/inline label placement, number/icon/dot indicator variants, sm/md/lg sizes, per-step status (process/finish/error/wait), visually-hidden SR status text, aria-live progress announcement. Pure HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens; no Java component, no @Wire, no _component/_instance params; CSP-safe.
items.docs: Copies wizard.jte to your JTE template root at lievit/wizard.jte. Reach as @template.lievit.wizard(activeStep=myForm.activeStep(), stepIds=..., stepTitles=..., content=@`...`). The CALLER's @Wire component owns int activeStep and exposes next()/prev()/goTo(int) actions; the partial wires l:click to them via the prevAction/nextAction/goToAction params (default: 'prev'/'next'/'goTo'). Skippable mode requires collection-nav.enhancer.ts installed (installCollectionNav(runtime)) and allowedSteps set to the reachable step indexes. The content slot renders the active step's owned markup; use @if(myForm.activeStep() == N) blocks inside to swap per-step content. The sub-partials wizard/step.jte and wizard/footer.jte from the previous API are removed; all chrome is self-contained in this single template.
